C: The angle measures of △A′B′C′ are the same as those of △ABC, but the side lengths of △A′B′C′ are twice the size of those of △ABC. D: The angle measures of △A′B′C′ depend on the angle of rotation, but the side lengths of △A′B′C′ are half the size of those of △ABC.
alt.es: Pregunta 44. El triángulo acutángulo △ABC es rotado alrededor de un punto y luego dilatado por un factor de escala de 1/2 para producir △A'B'C'. ¿Qué afirmación compara correctamente △A'B'C' con △ABC? Opciones: A: Las medidas de los ángulos y las longitudes de los lados de △A'B'C' tienen la mitad del tamaño de las de △ABC. B: Las medidas de los ángulos de △A'B'C' son las mismas que las de △ABC, pero las longitudes de los lados de △A'B'C' tienen la mitad del tamaño de las de △ABC. C: Las medidas de los ángulos de △A'B'C' son las mismas que las de △ABC, pero las longitudes de los lados de △A'B'C' tienen el doble del tamaño de las de △ABC. D: Las medidas de los ángulos de △A'B'C' dependen del ángulo de rotación, pero las longitudes de los lados de △A'B'C' tienen la mitad del tamaño de las de △ABC.

text.en: A rotation preserves both angle measures and side lengths. A dilation with scale factor 1/2 also preserves angle measures but multiplies every side length by 1/2. Therefore the new triangle has equal angles and half-length sides, as stated in choice B.
text.es: Una rotación conserva las medidas de los ángulos y de los lados. Una dilatación de factor 1/2 también conserva los ángulos, pero multiplica cada lado por 1/2. Por lo tanto, el nuevo triángulo tiene ángulos iguales y lados de la mitad, como indica B.

alt.en: Question 45. Which expression is equivalent to (4.5 × 10²) + (6.0 × 10³) and written in scientific notation? Choices: A: 1.05 × 10⁶. B: 2.7 × 10⁶. C: 6.45 × 10³. D: 10.5 × 10⁵.
alt.es: Pregunta 45. ¿Qué expresión es equivalente a (4.5 × 10²) + (6.0 × 10³) y está escrita en notación científica? Opciones: A: 1.05 × 10⁶ B: 2.7 × 10⁶ C: 6.45 × 10³ D: 10.5 × 10⁵

text.en: Convert to the same power of ten: 4.5 × 10^2 = 0.45 × 10^3. Then (0.45 + 6.0) × 10^3 = 6.45 × 10^3. Its coefficient is between 1 and 10, so this is scientific notation, choice C.
text.es: Se convierten los términos a la misma potencia de diez: 4.5 × 10^2 = 0.45 × 10^3. Luego, (0.45 + 6.0) × 10^3 = 6.45 × 10^3. El coeficiente está entre 1 y 10, así que es notación científica, la opción C.

alt.en: Question 46. The points (2, −2) and (−4, 13) lie on the graph of a linear function of x. Which point also lies on the graph of this function? Choices: A: (−6, 18). B: (−1, 5). C: (7, 14.5). D: (13, −4).
alt.es: Pregunta 46. Los puntos (2, −2) y (−4, 13) se encuentran en el gráfico de una función lineal de x. ¿Qué punto también se encuentra en el gráfico de esta función? Opciones: A: (−6, 18) B: (−1, 5) C: (7, 14.5) D: (13, −4)

text.en: The slope through (2, -2) and (-4, 13) is [13 - (-2)] ÷ (-4 - 2) = 15/-6 = -2.5. Using (2, -2) gives y = -2.5x + 3. At x = -6, y = -2.5(-6) + 3 = 18, so (-6, 18), choice A, lies on the line.
text.es: La pendiente entre (2, -2) y (-4, 13) es [13 - (-2)] ÷ (-4 - 2) = 15/-6 = -2.5. Con (2, -2), la recta es y = -2.5x + 3. Si x = -6, y = -2.5(-6) + 3 = 18; por eso (-6, 18), la opción A, está en la recta.

alt.en: Question 47. What value for the constant, h, in the equation 6x + 18 = h(3x + 9) will result in an infinite number of solutions? Choices: A: −2. B: −3. C: 2. D: 3.
alt.es: Pregunta 47. ¿Qué valor para la constante, h, en la ecuación que se muestra a continuación tendrá como resultado un número infinito de soluciones? Ecuación: 6x + 18 = h(3x + 9). Opciones: A: −2 B: −3 C: 2 D: 3

text.en: An equation has infinitely many solutions when both sides are identical. With h = 2, the right side is 2(3x + 9) = 6x + 18, exactly the left side for every x. Therefore h = 2, choice C.
text.es: Una ecuación tiene infinitas soluciones cuando ambos lados son idénticos. Si h = 2, el lado derecho es 2(3x + 9) = 6x + 18, exactamente igual al izquierdo para todo x. Por lo tanto, h = 2, la opción C.

alt.en: Question 50. The graphs show the relationship between elapsed time and distance traveled by airplane A and airplane B after each airplane reaches its cruising speed. Both coordinate graphs have a horizontal x-axis labeled Time (minutes), a vertical y-axis labeled Distance (miles), and a straight line beginning at (0, 0) and extending up and to the right with an arrow. For airplane A, the x-axis is marked 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 minutes; the y-axis is marked 6, 12, 18, 24, and 30 miles; and the line passes through (1, 6), (2, 12), (3, 18), (4, 24), and (5, 30). For airplane B, the x-axis is marked 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 minutes; the y-axis is marked 4, 8, 12, 16, and 20 miles; and the line passes through (1, 4), (2, 8), (3, 12), (4, 16), and (5, 20). Airplane C is traveling at a different cruising speed. The equation y = (27/6)x can be used to determine y, the number of miles traveled by airplane C in x minutes. Which statement accurately compares the cruising speed of airplane C to airplanes A and B? Choices: A: The cruising speed of airplane C is less than the cruising speeds of both airplanes A and B. B: The cruising speed of airplane C is greater than the cruising speeds of both airplanes A and B. C: The cruising speed of airplane C is greater than the cruising speed of airplane A and less than the cruising speed of airplane B. D: The cruising speed of airplane C is less than the cruising speed of airplane A and greater than the cruising speed of airplane B.
alt.es: Pregunta 50. Los siguientes gráficos muestran la relación entre el tiempo transcurrido y la distancia recorrida por el avión A y el avión B después de que cada avión alcanza su velocidad de crucero. En ambos gráficos, el eje horizontal x representa el tiempo en minutos y el eje vertical y representa la distancia en millas. El gráfico del AVIÓN A tiene una recta ascendente que parte del origen y pasa por los puntos de la cuadrícula (1, 6), (2, 12), (3, 18), (4, 24) y (5, 30); continúa con una flecha hacia arriba y a la derecha. En el eje x aparecen 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 y 5, y en el eje y aparecen 6, 12, 18, 24 y 30. El gráfico del AVIÓN B tiene una recta ascendente que parte del origen y pasa por los puntos de la cuadrícula (1, 4), (2, 8), (3, 12), (4, 16) y (5, 20); continúa con una flecha hacia arriba y a la derecha. En el eje x aparecen 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 y 5, y en el eje y aparecen 4, 8, 12, 16 y 20. El avión C está volando a una velocidad de crucero diferente. La ecuación y = (27/6)x puede usarse para determinar y, el número de millas recorridas por el avión C en x minutos. ¿Qué afirmación compara de manera precisa la velocidad de crucero del avión C con los aviones A y B? Opciones: A: La velocidad de crucero del avión C es menor que las velocidades de crucero de ambos aviones A y B. B: La velocidad de crucero del avión C es mayor que las velocidades de crucero de ambos aviones A y B. C: La velocidad de crucero del avión C es mayor que la velocidad de crucero del avión A y menor que la velocidad de crucero del avión B. D: La velocidad de crucero del avión C es menor que la velocidad de crucero del avión A y mayor que la velocidad de crucero del avión B.

text.en: Speed is the slope of each distance-time graph. Airplane A travels 6 miles per minute and B travels 4 miles per minute. For C, the coefficient in y = (27/6)x is 27/6 = 4.5 miles per minute. Since 4 < 4.5 < 6, C is slower than A but faster than B, choice D.
text.es: La velocidad es la pendiente de cada gráfica. El avión A recorre 6 millas por minuto y B recorre 4. Para C, el coeficiente de y = (27/6)x es 27/6 = 4.5 millas por minuto. Como 4 < 4.5 < 6, C es más lento que A y más rápido que B, la opción D.

alt.en: Question 51. Two transformations are performed on a figure on a coordinate plane. The first transformation is a translation 8 units to the left. Which second transformation will result in an image that is similar to, but not congruent to, the original figure? Choices: A: a clockwise rotation of 90° about the center. B: a clockwise rotation of 180° about the center. C: a dilation by a scale factor of 1 with the origin as the center of dilation. D: a dilation by a scale factor of 1/2 with the origin as the center of dilation.
alt.es: Pregunta 51. Se realizan dos transformaciones en una figura en un plano de coordenadas. La primera transformación es una traslación de 8 unidades hacia la izquierda. ¿Qué segunda transformación tendrá como resultado una imagen que sea similar a la figura original, pero no congruente con ella? Opciones: A: una rotación de 90° en el sentido del reloj alrededor del centro B: una rotación de 180° en el sentido del reloj alrededor del centro C: una dilatación por un factor de escala de 1 con el origen como centro de dilatación D: una dilatación por un factor de escala de 1/2 con el origen como centro de dilatación

text.en: Translations and rotations preserve size, and a dilation with scale factor 1 also leaves size unchanged, so choices A-C remain congruent to the original. A dilation by 1/2 preserves angles and proportions but halves every length, making the image similar but not congruent. Thus D is correct.
text.es: Las traslaciones y rotaciones conservan el tamaño, y una dilatación de factor 1 tampoco lo cambia; por eso A-C siguen siendo congruentes. Una dilatación de 1/2 conserva ángulos y proporciones, pero reduce cada longitud a la mitad. Por lo tanto, la imagen es semejante, no congruente, y D es correcta.

title.en: New York Grade 3 Math — 2016 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 3 — preguntas publicadas de 2016

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2016 New York State Grade 3 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 3.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016.

sourceTitle.en: 2016 NYS Grade 3 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 3.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016

alt.en: Question 1. Which analog clock shows the time 7:08? Choices: A: the longer hand points between 1 and 2, and the shorter hand points just past 7; B: the longer hand points to 8, and the shorter hand is between 7 and 8; C: the longer hand points to 7, and the shorter hand is between 1 and 2; D: the longer hand points between 1 and 2, and the shorter hand points just past 8.

text.en: At 7:08, the short hour hand should be just past 7 and the long minute hand should point to the eighth minute mark. Clock A shows both positions.
text.es: A las 7:08, la manecilla corta de la hora debe estar un poco después del 7 y la manecilla larga de los minutos debe señalar la octava marca de minuto. El reloj A muestra ambas posiciones.

alt.en: Question 2. What is 836 rounded to the nearest 10? Choices: A: 800; B: 830; C: 840; D: 870.

text.en: To round 836 to the nearest ten, look at the ones digit. Since 6 is at least 5, round the tens place up, giving 840.
text.es: Para redondear 836 a la decena más cercana, se mira la cifra de las unidades. Como 6 es al menos 5, se aumenta la cifra de las decenas; por lo tanto, resulta 840.

alt.en: Question 3. Which two values are located at the same point on a number line? Choices: A: 4/1 and 4; B: 1/3 and 3; C: 8/8 and 8; D: 6/2 and 4.

text.en: The fraction 4/1 equals 4 because dividing by 1 does not change a number. Therefore, 4/1 and 4 are at the same point on a number line.
text.es: La fracción 4/1 es igual a 4 porque dividir entre 1 no cambia un número. Por lo tanto, 4/1 y 4 están en el mismo punto de la recta numérica.

alt.en: Question 7. Umi created the number pattern below by adding the same amount each time to get the next number. 20, 40, 60, 80,... What will be the eighth number in the pattern? Choices: A: 160; B: 240; C: 320; D: 640.

text.en: The pattern adds 20 each time. Continuing 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120, 140 gives 160 as the eighth number.
text.es: El patrón suma 20 cada vez. Al continuar 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120, 140, por lo tanto se obtiene 160 como el octavo número.

alt.en: Question 8. In which equation could the number six replace the question mark to make the equation true? Choices: A: 9 × □ = 56; B: 48 ÷ □ = 8; C: 30 × 5 = □; D: 24 ÷ 3 = □.

text.en: Substituting 6 into 48 ÷ ? = 8 gives 48 ÷ 6 = 8, which is true. The other equations are not made true by 6.
text.es: Al sustituir 6 en 48 ÷ ? = 8 se obtiene 48 ÷ 6 = 8, que es verdadero. Las otras ecuaciones no se hacen verdaderas con 6.

alt.en: Question 12. A table titled Wheels Needed for Wagons shows: 1 wagon, 4 wheels; 2 wagons, 8 wheels; 3 wagons, 12 wheels; 4 wagons, 16 wheels. What is one pattern that can be seen in the table? Choices: A: The number of wheels increases by 1 each time; B: The number of wheels increases by 3 each time; C: The number of wheels increases by 4 each time; D: The number of wheels increases by 12 each time.

text.en: The totals in the table are 4, 8, 12, and 16 wheels. Each total is 4 more than the one before it, so the number of wheels increases by 4 each time.
text.es: Los totales de la tabla son 4, 8, 12 y 16 ruedas. Cada total es 4 mayor que el anterior, así que el número de ruedas aumenta en 4 cada vez.

alt.en: Question 13. If the equation 5 × □ = 45 is true, then which expression can be used to find the missing value? Choices: A: 9 ÷ 45; B: 5 ÷ 45; C: 45 ÷ 9; D: 45 ÷ 5.

text.en: The missing factor in 5 × ? = 45 is found with the inverse operation: 45 ÷ 5 = 9. Therefore, 45 ÷ 5 is the expression that finds the missing value.
text.es: El factor que falta en 5 × ? = 45 se encuentra con la operación inversa: 45 ÷ 5 = 9. Por lo tanto, 45 ÷ 5 es la expresión que encuentra el valor faltante.

alt.en: Question 14. Keisha’s step-shaped poster board is composed of an upper rectangle measuring 8 inches by 3 inches and a lower rectangle measuring 8 inches by 4 inches. The rectangles meet along part of an edge and do not overlap. What was the total area, in square inches, of Keisha’s poster board? Choices: A: 46 square inches; B: 56 square inches; C: 112 square inches; D: 192 square inches.

text.en: Split the poster into two nonoverlapping rectangles. Their areas are 8 × 4 = 32 and 8 × 3 = 24 square inches, and 32 + 24 = 56 square inches.
text.es: Se divide el cartel en dos rectángulos que no se superponen. Sus áreas son 8 × 4 = 32 y 8 × 3 = 24 pulgadas cuadradas, y 32 + 24 = 56 pulgadas cuadradas.

alt.en: Question 19. Maddie will ride her bike a total of 56 miles over 7 days. She will ride the same number of miles each day. What is the total number of miles Maddie will ride each day? Choices: A: 8; B: 9; C: 49; D: 63.

text.en: The 56 miles are shared equally among 7 days, so divide: 56 ÷ 7 = 8. Maddie rides 8 miles each day.
text.es: Las 56 millas se reparten por igual entre 7 días, así que se divide: 56 ÷ 7 = 8. Maddie recorre 8 millas cada día.

alt.en: Question 20. Four students each drew a figure and shaded part to represent a fraction. Selena’s diamond is divided into 8 equal triangles with 5 shaded. Tara’s five-sided figure is divided into 2 equal vertical parts with 1 shaded. Carson’s triangle is divided into smaller triangular regions with the two bottom regions shaded. Erik’s octagon is divided into 8 triangular sectors with the bottom 3 shaded. Which sentence about the figures is true? Choices: A: Selena shaded 5/8 of her figure; B: Tara shaded 1/1 of her figure; C: Carson shaded 2/4 of his figure; D: Erik shaded 5/3 of his figure.

text.en: Selena's figure is divided into 8 equal triangles, and 5 of them are shaded. The shaded fraction is therefore 5/8.
text.es: La figura de Selena está dividida en 8 triángulos iguales y 5 de ellos están sombreados. Por lo tanto, la fracción sombreada es 5/8.

alt.en: Question 21. A group of students played a basketball game after school. Which total can be found using the expression 7 × 2? Choices: A: the total number of points if a player made 7 shots and each shot was worth 2 points; B: the total number of basketballs if 7 basketballs were old and 2 basketballs were new; C: the total number of points if one player had 7 points and a different player had 2 points; D: the total number of basketballs used if there were 7 basketballs and 2 of the basketballs were not used.

text.en: The expression 7 × 2 represents 7 equal groups of 2. Seven made shots worth 2 points each give 7 × 2 = 14 total points.
text.es: La expresión 7 × 2 representa 7 grupos iguales de 2. Siete tiros encestados de 2 puntos cada uno dan 7 × 2 = 14 puntos en total.

alt.en: Question 22. Which two fractions both represent the same location on a number line? Choices: A: 2/3 and 1/5; B: 3/4 and 6/8; C: 2/4 and 3/5; D: 5/6 and 5/8.

text.en: Multiplying both the numerator and denominator of 3/4 by 2 gives 6/8. Since 3/4 and 6/8 are equivalent, they mark the same point on a number line.
text.es: Al multiplicar por 2 el numerador y el denominador de 3/4 se obtiene 6/8. Como 3/4 y 6/8 son equivalentes, señalan el mismo punto en una recta numérica.

alt.en: Question 23. What number makes the equation 81 ÷ □ = 9 true? Choices: A: 8; B: 9; C: 72; D: 90.

text.en: The equation asks for a number that makes 81 ÷ ? = 9. Since 81 ÷ 9 = 9, the missing number is 9.
text.es: La ecuación pide un número que haga verdadera 81 ÷ ? = 9. Como 81 ÷ 9 = 9, el número faltante es 9.

alt.en: Question 24. Which expression is equal to 720? Choices: A: 7x20; B: 8x80; C: 9x80; D: 9x90.

text.en: Since 9 × 8 = 72, multiplying 9 by 80 gives ten times as much: 9 × 80 = 720.
text.es: Como 9 × 8 = 72, multiplicar 9 por 80 da diez veces esa cantidad: 9 × 80 = 720.

alt.en: Question 25. Mr. Kohlberg owns a flower shop. At the beginning of the day, he had 152 roses. Mr. Kohlberg sold 96 of the roses and then wanted to separate the rest of the roses equally among 8 vases. What will be the total number of roses in each vase? Choices: A: 7; B: 12; C: 48; D: 56.

text.en: After selling 96 roses, 152 − 96 = 56 roses remain. Sharing them equally among 8 vases gives 56 ÷ 8 = 7 roses per vase.
text.es: Después de vender 96 rosas, quedan 152 − 96 = 56 rosas. Al repartirlas por igual entre 8 floreros, se obtiene 56 ÷ 8 = 7 rosas por florero.

alt.en: Question 26. Which of four shapes is 2/3 shaded? Shape 1 is a triangle divided by horizontal lines into 3 unequal regions, with the middle and bottom regions shaded. Shape 2 is a square with a diagonal white band and 2 shaded corner regions. Shape 3 is a triangle divided into 3 equal regions, with the top and bottom-left regions shaded. Shape 4 is a rectangle whose left half is unshaded and whose right half is divided into 2 shaded regions. Choices: A: shape 1; B: shape 2; C: shape 3; D: shape 4.

text.en: Shape 3 is divided into 3 equal-area parts, and 2 of those parts are shaded. It therefore shows 2/3 shaded.
text.es: La figura 3 está dividida en 3 partes de igual área y 2 de esas partes están sombreadas. Por eso representa 2/3 sombreado.

alt.en: Question 27. a7 Which situation could be represented by the expression 6 x 2? Choices: A: Rocco hiked six miles each day for two days; B: Rocco had six baseballs and gave away two of them; C: Rocco had a total of six tennis balls in two cans; D: Rocco biked six miles and then continued for two more miles.

text.en: Hiking 6 miles each day for 2 days creates 2 equal groups of 6 miles, so it is represented by 6 × 2. The other situations describe addition, subtraction, or sharing.
text.es: Caminar 6 millas cada día durante 2 días forma 2 grupos iguales de 6 millas, así que se representa con 6 × 2. Las otras situaciones describen suma, resta o reparto.

alt.en: Question 31. Mr. Gomez built a deck with an area of 29 square meters. The key says each small square equals 1 square meter. Which figure could represent the deck? Choices: A: a 4-by-7 rectangular grid; B: a figure with 3 rows of 10 squares and a centered 2-by-4 extension below; C: a 2-by-9 rectangular grid; D: an upside-down U-shaped figure with 7 squares in each of the top 3 rows and 4 squares in each of the bottom 2 rows.

text.en: Each small square represents 1 square meter. Figure D has 21 squares in its top three rows and 8 more in the two lower legs, for 21 + 8 = 29 square meters.
text.es: Cada cuadrado pequeño representa 1 metro cuadrado. La figura D tiene 21 cuadrados en sus tres filas superiores y 8 más en las dos patas inferiores, para un total de 21 + 8 = 29 metros cuadrados.

alt.en: Question 32. A number belongs in a box. When the number is rounded to the nearest hundred, the result will be 900. Which number belongs in the box? Choices: A: 849; B: 852; C: 960; D: 999.

text.en: A number from 850 through 949 rounds to 900 to the nearest hundred. Of the choices, 852 is in that interval, so it rounds to 900.
text.es: Un número desde 850 hasta 949 se redondea a 900 a la centena más cercana. Entre las opciones, 852 está en ese intervalo, así que se redondea a 900.

alt.en: Question 33. Kara has a bucket marked in 1-liter increments and labeled at 5 L, 10 L, and 15 L. The water level is at 12 liters. Kara wants to pour all of the water equally into 3 bowls for her dogs. How many liters of water should Kara pour into each bowl? Choices: A: 4; B: 5; C: 9; D: 15.

text.en: The water level is at 12 liters. Dividing 12 liters equally among 3 bowls gives 12 ÷ 3 = 4 liters in each bowl.
text.es: El nivel del agua está en 12 litros. Al repartir 12 litros por igual entre 3 tazones, se obtiene 12 ÷ 3 = 4 litros en cada tazón.

alt.en: Question 34. Leroy made a diamond-shaped game board divided into 9 equal small squares arranged as a rotated 3-by-3 grid. The center square is shaded. What fraction of the game board is shaded? Choices: A: 1/9; B: 1/8; C: 1/6; D: 1/3.

text.en: The game board contains 9 equal small squares, and 1 square is shaded. The shaded fraction is 1 out of 9, or 1/9.
text.es: El tablero contiene 9 cuadrados pequeños iguales y 1 cuadrado está sombreado. Por lo tanto, la fracción sombreada es 1 de 9, es decir, 1/9.

alt.en: Question 38. Tomas’s poster is shown as a shaded rectangle 6 unit squares wide and 4 unit squares high on a grid; the key says each square equals 1 square unit. Which figure has the same area as the poster? Choices: A: a rectangle measuring 8 units by 3 units; B: a square measuring 6 units by 6 units; C: a rectangle measuring 7 units by 3 units; D: a square measuring 5 units by 5 units.

text.en: The shaded poster covers 24 unit squares. Figure A has area 8 × 3 = 24 square units, so it has the same area.
text.es: El cartel sombreado cubre 24 cuadrados unitarios. La figura A tiene un área de 8 × 3 = 24 unidades cuadradas, así que tiene la misma área.

alt.en: Question 39. The first number in a number pattern is 28. The pattern rule is to add 14 to get the next number in the pattern. If the pattern continues, which statement is true? Choices: A: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 10; B: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 4; C: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 8; D: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 7.

text.en: Both the starting number 28 and the amount added, 14, are multiples of 7. Adding another multiple of 7 keeps every term divisible by 7.
text.es: Tanto el número inicial 28 como la cantidad que se suma, 14, son múltiplos de 7. Sumar otro múltiplo de 7 hace que cada término siga siendo divisible entre 7.

alt.en: Question 40. There were 6 rows of chairs set up for a meeting. Each row had 8 chairs. What was the total number of chairs set up for the meeting? Choices: A: 14; B: 36; C: 48; D: 64.

text.en: There are 6 equal rows with 8 chairs in each row. Multiplying gives 6 × 8 = 48 chairs.
text.es: Hay 6 filas iguales con 8 sillas en cada fila. Al multiplicar, 6 × 8 = 48 sillas.

alt.en: Question 41. A circle is divided into parts. Each part is 1/4 of the total area of the circle. Which sentence describes the circle? Choices: A: The circle has 1 small part and 3 large parts; B: The circle has 1 small part and 4 large parts; C: The circle has 4 parts that are each the same size; D: The circle has 5 parts that are each the same size.

text.en: If each part is 1/4 of the circle, four equal parts are needed to make the whole because 4 × 1/4 = 1. Therefore, the circle has 4 equal-size parts.
text.es: Si cada parte es 1/4 del círculo, se necesitan cuatro partes iguales para formar el entero porque 4 × 1/4 = 1. Por lo tanto, el círculo tiene 4 partes del mismo tamaño.

alt.en: Question 42. A baker made 232 muffins. He sent 190 of the muffins to a local hotel. He will put the rest of the muffins in boxes. Each box can hold 6 muffins. Which equation can be used to find b, the number of boxes the baker will need? Choices: A: (232 − 190) ÷ 6 = b; B: (232 + 190) × 6 = b; C: (232 − 190) × 6 = b; D: (232 + 190) ÷ 6 = b.

text.en: First subtract the 190 muffins sent away from the 232 made, then divide the remaining muffins among boxes of 6. This gives the equation (232 − 190) ÷ 6 = b.
text.es: Primero se restan de los 232 muffins hechos los 190 que se enviaron y luego se dividen los muffins restantes en cajas de 6. Esto da la ecuación (232 − 190) ÷ 6 = b.

title.en: New York Grade 4 Math — 2016 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 4 — preguntas publicadas de 2016

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2016 New York State Grade 4 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 4.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016.

sourceTitle.en: 2016 NYS Grade 4 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 4.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016

alt.en: Question 1. What expression can be used to show 270,240 written in expanded form? Choices: A: 200,000 + 7,000 + 200 + 4; B: 200,000 + 7,000 + 200 + 40; C: 200,000 + 70,000 + 200 + 40; D: 200,000 + 70,000 + 2,000 + 40.

text.en: The digits in 270,240 represent 2 hundred-thousands, 7 ten-thousands, 2 hundreds, and 4 tens. Thus, 270,240 = 200,000 + 70,000 + 200 + 40.
text.es: Las cifras de 270,240 representan 2 centenas de millar, 7 decenas de millar, 2 centenas y 4 decenas. Por lo tanto, 270,240 = 200,000 + 70,000 + 200 + 40.

alt.en: Question 2. There are 20 students in Mr. Clark’s fourth-grade class. There are 5 times as many students in the entire fourth grade as there are in Mr. Clark’s class. Which equation can be used to determine the number of students, n, in the entire fourth grade? Choices: A: 5 × n = 20; B: 5 + 20 = n; C: 20 × 5 = n; D: 20 ÷ n = 5.

text.en: The entire grade has 5 times the 20 students in Mr. Clark's class. Multiplying gives 20 × 5 = n, so n = 100.
text.es: Todo el grado tiene 5 veces los 20 estudiantes de la clase del Sr. Clark. Al multiplicar se obtiene 20 × 5 = n, así que n = 100.

alt.en: Question 3. A bar representing 1 whole is divided into 3 equal parts with 1 part shaded. Which model shows an equivalent fraction? Choices: A: a bar divided into 12 equal parts with 4 shaded; B: a bar divided into 4 equal parts with 1 shaded; C: a bar divided into 6 equal parts with 3 shaded; D: a bar divided into 3 equal parts with all 3 shaded.

text.en: The original model has 1 of 3 equal parts shaded, or 1/3. Model A has 4 of 12 equal parts shaded, and 4/12 simplifies to 1/3.
text.es: El modelo original tiene sombreada 1 de 3 partes iguales, o 1/3. El modelo A tiene sombreadas 4 de 12 partes iguales, y 4/12 se simplifica a 1/3; por lo tanto, las fracciones son equivalentes.

alt.en: Question 4. What value can replace the question mark to make the statement 3 2/8 + □ = 7 1/8 true? Choices: A: 3 1/8; B: 3 7/8; C: 4 1/8; D: 4 7/8.

text.en: Find the missing addend by subtracting: 7 1/8 − 3 2/8. Regroup 7 1/8 as 6 9/8; then 6 9/8 − 3 2/8 = 3 7/8.
text.es: Se encuentra el sumando faltante restando: 7 1/8 − 3 2/8. Se reagrupa 7 1/8 como 6 9/8; entonces 6 9/8 − 3 2/8 = 3 7/8.

alt.en: Question 5. If 30,000 is divided by 10 and then divided by 10 again, what will be the resulting number? Choices: A: 3; B: 30; C: 300; D: 3,000.

text.en: Dividing by 10 moves every digit one place to the right. Starting with 30,000 gives 3,000 after the first division and 300 after the second.
text.es: Dividir entre 10 mueve cada cifra un lugar hacia la derecha. Al comenzar con 30,000 se obtiene 3,000 después de la primera división y 300 después de la segunda.

alt.en: Question 6. A table titled Team Name Votes shows: Bears, 12 votes; Comets, 36; Hawks, 117; Stars, 39; Wolves, 108. Which team name received 3 times as many votes as Comets? Choices: A: Bears; B: Hawks; C: Stars; D: Wolves.

text.en: Comets received 36 votes. Three times 36 is 3 × 36 = 108, and the table shows that Wolves received 108 votes.
text.es: Comets recibió 36 votos. Tres veces 36 es 3 × 36 = 108, y la tabla muestra que Wolves recibió 108 votos.

alt.en: Question 7. A diagram shows a ray with endpoint G that passes through K and continues in the direction of K. A separate segment passes through points F, G, H, and J. What is the name of the ray? Choices: A: ray K; B: ray FJ; C: ray GK; D: ray KGJ.

text.en: A ray is named with its endpoint first and then a point in its direction. The pictured ray starts at G and passes through K, so its name is ray GK.
text.es: Una semirrecta se nombra primero por su extremo y después por un punto en su dirección. La semirrecta dibujada comienza en G y pasa por K, así que se llama semirrecta GK.

alt.en: Question 8. The measure of angle G is 20°. What fraction of a complete circle is the measure of angle G? Choices: A: 20/360; B: 20/340; C: 20/180; D: 20/100.

text.en: A complete circle measures 360°. An angle of 20° is therefore 20 out of 360 degrees of the circle, so the requested fraction is 20/360.
text.es: Un círculo completo mide 360°. Por lo tanto, un ángulo de 20° ocupa 20 de los 360 grados del círculo, así que la fracción pedida es 20/360.

alt.en: Question 9. Dawn needs to fix windows in her house. She must buy 3 feet of wood, which costs $7 per foot. She also needs to buy 4 pieces of glass. Each piece of glass costs $23. What will be the total cost for fixing the windows? Choices: A: $30; B: $90; C: $113; D: $176.

text.en: The wood costs 3 × $7 = $21, and the glass costs 4 × $23 = $92. Adding the two costs gives $21 + $92 = $113.
text.es: La madera cuesta 3 × $7 = $21 y el vidrio cuesta 4 × $23 = $92. Al sumar ambos costos se obtiene $21 + $92 = $113.

alt.en: Question 13. Jason makes aprons. A bar representing one yard is divided into 3 equal parts, and 2 parts are shaded to represent the fabric he uses for each apron. How many yards of fabric, in all, will Jason need to make 14 aprons? Choices: A: 4 2/3; B: 9 1/3; C: 13 1/3; D: 14 2/3.

text.en: Each apron uses 2/3 yard, so 14 aprons use 14 × 2/3 = 28/3 yards. Since 28/3 = 9 1/3, Jason needs 9 1/3 yards.
text.es: Cada delantal usa 2/3 de yarda, así que 14 delantales usan 14 × 2/3 = 28/3 yardas. Como 28/3 = 9 1/3, Jason necesita 9 1/3 yardas.

alt.en: Question 17. Which angle has a measure of 45°? Choices: A: an acute angle between a left-pointing horizontal ray and a diagonal ray pointing up and left at a 45-degree-looking slant; B: a narrower acute angle between a left-pointing horizontal ray and a steeper ray pointing up and left; C: a wide obtuse angle between a right-pointing horizontal ray and a shallow ray pointing up and left; D: an obtuse angle between a right-pointing horizontal ray and a 45-degree-looking ray pointing up and left.

text.en: A 45° angle is half of a 90° right angle. Angle A represents half of a right-angle-sized turn, while the other choices are noticeably larger.
text.es: Un ángulo de 45° es la mitad de un ángulo recto de 90°. El ángulo A representa aproximadamente la mitad de un giro de ángulo recto, mientras que las otras opciones son claramente mayores.

alt.en: Question 18. The value of the digit 5 in 24,513 is how many times the value of the digit 5 in 357? Choices: A: 10; B: 100; C: 1,000; D: 10,000.

text.en: The digit 5 in 24,513 has value 500, while the digit 5 in 357 has value 50. Since 500 = 10 × 50, the first value is 10 times the second.
text.es: La cifra 5 en 24,513 vale 500, mientras que la cifra 5 en 357 vale 50. Como 500 = 10 × 50, el primer valor es 10 veces el segundo.

alt.en: Question 23. «In December, a toy store sold 934 puzzles. Each puzzle cost $6, including tax. What was the total cost of the puzzles sold, including tax? Choices: A: $5,434; B: $5,484; C: $5,604; D: $5,684.

text.es: Se multiplican los 934 rompecabezas por $6 cada uno: 934 × 6 = (900 × 6) + (34 × 6) = 5,400 + 204 = $5,604.

alt.en: Question 24. Which figure appears to show a pair of perpendicular lines? Choices: A: two separate lines with different shallow slopes; B: a horizontal line and a vertical line intersecting at a square corner; C: two separate horizontal parallel lines.

text.en: Perpendicular lines intersect to form a 90° angle. In figure B, the vertical and horizontal lines meet at a right angle, so they are perpendicular.
text.es: Las rectas perpendiculares se cruzan para formar un ángulo de 90°. En la figura B, las rectas vertical y horizontal forman un ángulo recto, así que son perpendiculares.

alt.en: Question 25. Which fraction goes into the blank to make the number sentence 2/3 < □ true? Choices: A: 1/6; B: 3/6; C: 3/5; D: 3/4.

text.en: Use a common denominator to compare: 2/3 = 8/12 and 3/4 = 9/12. Because 8/12 < 9/12, placing 3/4 in the blank makes the inequality true.
text.es: Se usa un denominador común para comparar: 2/3 = 8/12 y 3/4 = 9/12. Como 8/12 < 9/12, colocar 3/4 en el espacio hace verdadera la desigualdad.

alt.en: Question 26. Which model shows an angle that appears to represent 45/360 of a circle? Choices: A: an angle inside a circle that is about one quarter of a turn; B: a straight angle spanning half a circle; C: a small acute angle inside a circle that is about one eighth of a turn; D: a reflex angle covering most of a circle except for a small wedge.

text.en: The fraction 45/360 is the part of a full 360° turn made by a 45° angle, which is 1/8 of a circle. Model C shows an angle of about 45°, or one-eighth of a turn.
text.es: La fracción 45/360 es la parte de un giro completo de 360° que forma un ángulo de 45°, lo que equivale a 1/8 de círculo. El modelo C muestra aproximadamente 45°, o un octavo de giro.

alt.en: Question 27. The number pattern 5, 20, 35, 50, 65 follows a skip-counting rule. Which number pattern follows the same rule? Choices: A: 5, 20, 25, 30, 35; B: 13, 18, 23, 28, 33; C: 12, 27, 42, 57, 72; D: 15, 30, 40, 55, 65.

text.en: The given pattern adds 15 each time: 5, 20, 35, 50, 65. Choice C also adds 15 each time: 12, 27, 42, 57, 72, so it follows the same rule.
text.es: El patrón dado suma 15 cada vez: 5, 20, 35, 50, 65. La opción C también suma 15 cada vez: 12, 27, 42, 57, 72; por lo tanto, sigue la misma regla.

alt.en: Question 28. An acute angle is formed by a horizontal right-pointing ray and a diagonal ray rising to the right; the opening appears a little less than half of a right angle. Which measurement best represents the measure of the angle? Choices: A: 36°; B: 42°; C: 138°; D: 142°.

text.en: The angle is acute and appears slightly smaller than a 45° angle. Therefore, 42° best matches that size; 36° is too small and 138° and 142° are obtuse.
text.es: El ángulo es agudo y parece un poco menor que un ángulo de 45°. Por lo tanto, 42° se aproxima mejor; 36° es demasiado pequeño y 138° y 142° son obtusos.

alt.en: Question 29. Kailyn read 24 pages of a book. Logan read three times as many pages as Kailyn. Which equation can be used to find the total number of pages Logan read? Choices: A: 3 × □ = 24; B: 3 + □ = 24; C: 24 × 3 = □; D: 24 ÷ 3 = □.

text.en: Logan read 3 times Kailyn's 24 pages, so the unknown number of Logan's pages is found by multiplying. The matching equation is 24 × 3 = ?.
text.es: Logan leyó 3 veces las 24 páginas de Kailyn, así que el número desconocido de páginas se encuentra multiplicando. La ecuación correspondiente es 24 × 3 = ?.

alt.en: Question 30. A team of volunteers collected a total of $5,144 selling T-shirts at a charity concert. Each T-shirt was sold for $8. What was the total number of T-shirts the volunteers sold? Choices: A: 632; B: 643; C: 655; D: 668.

text.en: Divide the total money by the price of each shirt: 5,144 ÷ 8 = 643. This can be checked because 643 × 8 = 5,144.
text.es: Se divide el dinero total entre el precio de cada camiseta: 5,144 ÷ 8 = 643. Se puede comprobar porque 643 × 8 = 5,144.

alt.en: Question 31. Two fraction strips are each divided into 12 equal parts. The first strip has 3 parts shaded, and the second has 6 parts shaded. What is the sum of the two fractions? Choices: A: 3/12; B: 9/24; C: 9/12; D: 15/12.

text.en: The strips use the same 12 equal parts. The first has 3/12 shaded and the second has 6/12 shaded, so their sum is 3/12 + 6/12 = 9/12.
text.es: Las tiras usan las mismas 12 partes iguales. La primera tiene 3/12 sombreados y la segunda 6/12, así que la suma es 3/12 + 6/12 = 9/12.

alt.en: Question 32. What is the value of the expression below? 28 x 42 Choices: A: 420; B: 816; C: 1166; D: 1,176.

text.en: Use the distributive property: 28 × 42 = 28 × (40 + 2) = 1,120 + 56 = 1,176.
text.es: Se usa la propiedad distributiva: 28 × 42 = 28 × (40 + 2) = 1,120 + 56 = 1,176.

alt.en: Question 33. A line plot titled Lengths of String is marked in eighths of a foot from 0 to 1. It shows 1 X at 1/8, 1 at 2/8, 1 at 3/8, 2 at 4/8, 2 at 7/8, and 1 at 1. What was the total length, in feet, of string that Mario used? Choices: A: 25/8; B: 36/8; C: 48/8; D: 64/8.

text.en: Add the plotted lengths in eighths: (1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 4 + 7 + 7 + 8)/8 = 36/8 feet. Each X contributes one of those lengths.
text.es: Se suman las longitudes de la gráfica en octavos: (1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 4 + 7 + 7 + 8)/8 = 36/8 pies. Cada X aporta una de esas longitudes.

alt.en: Question 34. Which set of models is equivalent to the expression 2 × 4/6? Choices: A: 8 rectangles arranged in 2 rows of 4, each divided into 6 equal parts with 1 part shaded; B: 6 rectangles arranged in 2 rows of 3, each divided into 4 equal parts with the top 2 parts shaded; C: 3 rectangles divided into 6 equal parts, with the first 2 rectangles fully shaded and 4 parts of the third shaded; D: 8 rectangles arranged in 2 rows of 4, each divided into 12 equal parts with 1 part shaded.

text.en: The expression 2 × 4/6 means two groups of four sixths, for a total of 8/6. Model A shows 8 separate one-sixth pieces shaded, arranged as two groups of 4.
text.es: La expresión 2 × 4/6 significa dos grupos de cuatro sextos, para un total de 8/6. El modelo A muestra 8 piezas de un sexto sombreadas, organizadas en dos grupos de 4.

alt.en: Question 38. Dani has 45 marbles. She has 5 times as many marbles as Joe has. How many marbles does Joe have? Choices: A: 50; B: 40; C: 9; D: 5.

text.en: Dani's 45 marbles are 5 times Joe's amount, so divide to undo the multiplication: 45 ÷ 5 = 9. Joe has 9 marbles.
text.es: Las 45 canicas de Dani son 5 veces la cantidad de Joe, así que se divide para deshacer la multiplicación: 45 ÷ 5 = 9. Joe tiene 9 canicas.

alt.en: Question 39. When a certain number is divided by 4, the answer is 160 with a remainder of 2. What is the number? Choices: A: 38; B: 42; C: 638; D: 642.

text.en: A quotient of 160 with remainder 2 means the number is 4 × 160 + 2. Calculating gives 640 + 2 = 642.
text.es: Un cociente de 160 con residuo 2 significa que el número es 4 × 160 + 2. Al calcular se obtiene 640 + 2 = 642.

title.en: New York Grade 5 Math — 2016 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 5 — preguntas publicadas de 2016

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2016 New York State Grade 5 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 5.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016.

sourceTitle.en: 2016 NYS Grade 5 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 5.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016

alt.en: Question 1. Pax wants to make fruit punch for a party using this recipe: Fruit Punch—1.25 L orange juice, 2.5 L cranberry juice, and 1 L ginger ale. He will make three times the amount of fruit punch listed in the recipe. What is the total amount of fruit punch, in liters, that Pax will make? Choices: A: 4.53. B: 4.75. C: 12.90. D: 14.25.

text.en: One recipe makes 1.25 + 2.5 + 1 = 4.75 liters of punch. Making three times that amount gives 4.75 × 3 = 14.25 liters.
text.es: Una receta produce 1.25 + 2.5 + 1 = 4.75 litros de ponche. Hacer tres veces esa cantidad da 4.75 × 3 = 14.25 litros.

alt.en: Question 2. What is the value of the vertical subtraction expression 3 1/4 − 1 7/8? Choices: A: 1 1/4. B: 1 3/8. C: 2 3/8. D: 3 1/2.

text.en: To subtract 3 1/4 − 1 7/8, write 3 1/4 as 3 2/8 and regroup it as 2 10/8. Then 2 10/8 − 1 7/8 = 1 3/8.
text.es: Para restar 3 1/4 − 1 7/8, se escribe 3 1/4 como 3 2/8 y se reagrupa como 2 10/8. Entonces 2 10/8 − 1 7/8 = 1 3/8.

alt.en: Question 3. What is the value of the expression 3,972 ÷ 12? Choices: A: 372. B: 336. C: 331. D: 306.

text.en: Dividing 3,972 by 12 gives 331. The quotient is confirmed by the inverse operation because 331 × 12 = 3,972.
text.es: Al dividir 3,972 entre 12 se obtiene 331. El cociente se comprueba con la operación inversa porque 331 × 12 = 3,972.

alt.en: Question 4. A sign at the start of Pinecone Trail lists the distances from the sign to three points of interest along the trail: Nature Center, 1 1/2 miles; Giant Boulder, 4 1/4 miles; Lookout Point, 8 3/4 miles. Sage hiked from the start of the trail to Lookout Point. She then hiked back along the trail to Giant Boulder to camp for the night. What was the total distance, in miles, that Sage hiked? Choices: A: 21 3/4. B: 13 1/4. C: 4 1/2. D: 4 1/4.

text.en: Sage first hikes 8 3/4 miles to Lookout Point. The return distance to Giant Boulder is 8 3/4 − 4 1/4 = 4 1/2 miles, so the total is 8 3/4 + 4 1/2 = 13 1/4 miles.
text.es: Sage primero camina 8 3/4 millas hasta Lookout Point. La distancia de regreso a Giant Boulder es 8 3/4 − 4 1/4 = 4 1/2 millas, así que el total es 8 3/4 + 4 1/2 = 13 1/4 millas.

alt.en: Question 5. Which type of quadrilateral can have exactly 1 pair of parallel sides? Choices: A: rectangle. B: rhombus. C: square. D: trapezoid.

text.en: A trapezoid can have exactly one pair of parallel sides. Therefore it fits the condition, while rectangles, rhombuses, and squares each have two pairs of parallel sides.
text.es: Un trapecio puede tener exactamente un par de lados paralelos. En cambio, los rectángulos, rombos y cuadrados tienen dos pares de lados paralelos; por lo tanto, la respuesta es trapecio.

alt.en: Question 9. What is the value of the expression 56 ÷ 1/17? Choices: A: 1/952. B: 17/56. C: 3 5/17. D: 952.

text.en: Dividing by 1/17 is the same as multiplying by its reciprocal, 17. Therefore, 56 ÷ 1/17 = 56 × 17 = 952.
text.es: Dividir entre 1/17 equivale a multiplicar por su recíproco, 17. Por lo tanto, 56 ÷ 1/17 = 56 × 17 = 952.

alt.en: Question 10. In her math class, Carla used unit cubes to build a right rectangular prism with a volume of 24 cubic units. The height of the prism was two units. Which figure could be the bottom layer of the prism? Each choice is a one-cube-high rectangular array of unit cubes. Choices: A: An array 6 cubes long and 2 cubes deep, containing 12 cubes. B: An array 6 cubes long and 4 cubes deep, containing 24 cubes. C: An array 8 cubes long and 3 cubes deep, containing 24 cubes. D: An array 6 cubes long and 1 cube deep, containing 6 cubes.

text.en: With volume 24 cubic units and height 2 units, the bottom layer must have area 24 ÷ 2 = 12 square units. Figure A has 2 × 6 = 12 unit squares in its layer.
text.es: Con un volumen de 24 unidades cúbicas y una altura de 2 unidades, la capa inferior debe tener un área de 24 ÷ 2 = 12 unidades cuadradas. La figura A tiene 2 × 6 = 12 cuadrados unitarios.

alt.en: Question 13. Bettina spent $75 on 5 shirts that each cost the same price. Three of the shirts were red. Which expression represents the total cost of the red shirts? Choices: A: 75 × 3/5. B: 75 × 5/3. C: 75/5 × 1/3. D: 75/3 × 1/5.

text.en: Three of the 5 equally priced shirts were red, so the red shirts account for 3/5 of the $75 total. Their cost is represented by 75 × 3/5.
text.es: Tres de las 5 camisas del mismo precio eran rojas, así que representan 3/5 del total de $75. Su costo se representa con 75 × 3/5.

alt.en: Question 14. The two right rectangular prisms below have different volumes. Each small cube represents 1 cubic foot. The left prism has a 3-by-3 front face and is 3 cubes deep. The right prism has a 2-by-4 front face and is 3 cubes deep. What is the difference in volume, in cubic feet, of the two prisms? Choices: A: 1 B: 3 C: 6 D: 9

text.en: The first prism has volume 3 × 3 × 3 = 27 cubic feet. The second has volume 2 × 4 × 3 = 24 cubic feet, so the difference is 27 − 24 = 3 cubic feet.
text.es: El primer prisma tiene un volumen de 3 × 3 × 3 = 27 pies cúbicos. El segundo tiene un volumen de 2 × 4 × 3 = 24 pies cúbicos, así que la diferencia es 27 − 24 = 3 pies cúbicos.

alt.en: Question 15. The line plot shows the weights of ten eggs laid by one hen. It is titled “EGGS LAID BY ONE HEN,” and its horizontal axis is labeled “Weight (ounces).” The axis has five equally spaced tick marks from 1 1/2 through 2 1/2 ounces, representing 1 1/2, 1 3/4, 2, 2 1/4, and 2 1/2. There are 2 X marks at 1 1/2, 4 X marks at 1 3/4, 2 X marks at 2, 1 X mark at 2 1/4, and 1 X mark at 2 1/2. What is the total weight, in ounces, of the four heaviest eggs? Choices: A: 4 B: 7 C: 8 1/2 D: 8 3/4

text.en: The four greatest plotted weights are 2 1/2, 2 1/4, 2, and 2 ounces. Their sum is 2 1/2 + 2 1/4 + 2 + 2 = 8 3/4 ounces.
text.es: Los cuatro pesos mayores de la gráfica son 2 1/2, 2 1/4, 2 y 2 onzas. Su suma es 2 1/2 + 2 1/4 + 2 + 2 = 8 3/4 onzas.

alt.en: Question 16. The table below lists the capacity, in quarts, of four different fish tanks at a pet store. The table is titled “FISH TANK CAPACITY” and has columns “Fish Tank” and “Capacity (quarts).” Its rows are: Pacific, 240; Fresh, 15; Tropic, 120; Bahama, 60. Which fish tank has a capacity of 60 gallons? Choices: A: Pacific B: Fresh C: Tropic D: Bahama

text.en: Each gallon equals 4 quarts, so 60 gallons equals 60 × 4 = 240 quarts. The table lists the Pacific tank at 240 quarts.
text.es: Cada galón equivale a 4 cuartos, así que 60 galones equivalen a 60 × 4 = 240 cuartos. La tabla muestra que el tanque Pacific tiene 240 cuartos.

alt.en: Question 17. Which number sentence is true? Choices: A: 0.35 > 0.36 B: 0.3 < 0.04 C: 0.3 > 0.20 D: 0.75 < 0.7

text.en: Write both decimals to the hundredths place: 0.3 = 0.30 and 0.20 stays 0.20. Since 30 hundredths is greater than 20 hundredths, 0.3 > 0.20 is true.
text.es: Se escriben ambos decimales hasta las centésimas: 0.3 = 0.30 y 0.20 permanece igual. Como 30 centésimas es mayor que 20 centésimas, 0.3 > 0.20 es verdadero.

alt.en: Question 18. Mr. Hinckley owns 83 acres of land. He divides the land into eight equal sections to sell to eight buyers. Which phrase describes how much land, in acres, each buyer will receive? Choices: A: more than 9 and less than 10 B: more than 10 and less than 11 C: more than 11 and less than 12 D: more than 12 and less than 13

text.en: Divide the 83 acres equally among 8 buyers: 83 ÷ 8 = 10 3/8. This amount is more than 10 and less than 11 acres.
text.es: Se dividen los 83 acres por igual entre 8 compradores: 83 ÷ 8 = 10 3/8. Esta cantidad es mayor que 10 y menor que 11 acres.

alt.en: Question 23. What is the value of the expression below? 8 + 24 ÷ (2 × 6) − 4. Choices: A: 92 B: 76 C: 11 D: 6

text.en: Follow the order of operations: 2 × 6 = 12, then 24 ÷ 12 = 2. Finally, 8 + 2 − 4 = 6.
text.es: Se sigue el orden de las operaciones: 2 × 6 = 12 y luego 24 ÷ 12 = 2. Finalmente, 8 + 2 − 4 = 6.

alt.en: Question 24. Parallelograms always belong to which category of shapes? Choices: A: squares B: rectangles C: rhombuses D: quadrilaterals

text.en: A parallelogram is defined as a quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides. Therefore, every parallelogram always belongs to the category of quadrilaterals.
text.es: Un paralelogramo se define como un cuadrilátero con dos pares de lados paralelos. Por lo tanto, todo paralelogramo siempre pertenece a la categoría de los cuadriláteros.

alt.en: Question 25. Which decimal best represents the location of point X on the number line below? The horizontal number line has arrows at both ends and runs from a tick labeled 70/100 to a tick labeled 80/100. These labeled ticks and the ticks between them form 10 equal intervals. Point X is on the sixth tick to the right of 70/100. Choices: A: 0.076 B: 0.077 C: 0.76 D: 0.77

text.en: The interval from 70/100 to 80/100 is divided into ten equal steps, so each step is 1/100, or 0.01. Point X is at the sixth step after 0.70, which is 0.76.
text.es: El intervalo de 70/100 a 80/100 está dividido en diez pasos iguales, así que cada paso es 1/100, o 0.01. El punto X está en el sexto paso después de 0.70, lo que da 0.76.

alt.en: Question 26. What is 1,748 ÷ 38? Choices: A: 41 B: 43 C: 46 D: 48

text.en: Dividing 1,748 by 38 gives 46. Check the quotient by multiplying: 38 × 46 = 38 × (40 + 6) = 1,520 + 228 = 1,748.
text.es: Al dividir 1,748 entre 38 se obtiene 46. Se comprueba multiplicando: 38 × 46 = 38 × (40 + 6) = 1,520 + 228 = 1,748.

alt.en: Question 27. The table shows the number of computers donated to a school by each of 4 companies. The table is titled “COMPUTERS DONATED TO A SCHOOL.” Its columns are “Company” and “Number of Computers.” The rows are: Company A, 25; Company B, 40; Company C, 25; Company D, 30. All the donated computers were shared equally by 5 classrooms. Which expression represents the number of computers each classroom received? Choices: A: 120 × 5/4 B: 120 × 1/4 C: 120 × 4/5 D: 120 × 1/5

text.en: The companies donated 25 + 40 + 25 + 30 = 120 computers. Sharing 120 equally among 5 classrooms is 120 ÷ 5, which can be written as 120 × 1/5.
text.es: Las compañías donaron 25 + 40 + 25 + 30 = 120 computadoras. Repartir 120 por igual entre 5 salones es 120 ÷ 5, lo que se puede escribir como 120 × 1/5.

alt.en: Question 28. Which expression is equivalent to 83,120 in expanded form using powers of 10? Choices: A: (8 × 10⁵) + (3 × 10⁴) + (1 × 10³) + (2 × 10²) B: (8 × 10⁴) + (3 × 10³) + (1 × 10³) + (2 × 10²) C: (8 × 10⁴) + (3 × 10³) + (1 × 10²) + (2 × 10¹) D: (8 × 10¹) + (3 × 10¹) + (1 × 10¹) + (2 × 10¹)

text.en: In 83,120, the 8 is in the 10⁴ place, the 3 in the 10³ place, the 1 in the 10² place, and the 2 in the 10¹ place. Thus it equals (8 × 10⁴) + (3 × 10³) + (1 × 10²) + (2 × 10¹).
text.es: En 83,120, el 8 está en la posición de 10⁴, el 3 en la de 10³, el 1 en la de 10² y el 2 en la de 10¹. Por lo tanto, equivale a (8 × 10⁴) + (3 × 10³) + (1 × 10²) + (2 × 10¹).

alt.en: Question 29. Which measurement is equivalent to 3 meters? Choices: A: 9 centimeters B: 36 centimeters C: 100 centimeters D: 300 centimeters

text.en: One meter equals 100 centimeters. Multiplying 3 meters by 100 centimeters per meter gives 3 × 100 = 300 centimeters.
text.es: Un metro equivale a 100 centímetros. Al multiplicar 3 metros por 100 centímetros por metro se obtiene 3 × 100 = 300 centímetros.

alt.en: Question 40. Which model shows one way to determine the area of a rectangle that is 7/10 meter long and 3/5 meter wide? Each choice is a 1 m by 1 m square grid divided into 10 equal columns and 5 equal rows. Choices: A: The rectangular region formed by the leftmost 7 columns and the top 3 rows is shaded, for a total of 21 shaded small rectangles. B: All 10 columns in the top 3 rows are shaded, for a total of 30 shaded small rectangles. C: The rectangular region formed by the leftmost 6 columns and the top 2 rows is shaded, for a total of 12 shaded small rectangles. D: The leftmost 7 columns in only the top row are shaded, for a total of 7 shaded small rectangles.

text.en: Model A shades 7 of the 10 columns and 3 of the 5 rows. Their overlap represents 7/10 × 3/5 = 21/50 square meter, the rectangle's area.
text.es: El modelo A sombrea 7 de las 10 columnas y 3 de las 5 filas. Su superposición representa 7/10 × 3/5 = 21/50 de metro cuadrado, el área del rectángulo.

alt.en: Question 41. A swimming pool is shaped like a right rectangular prism. The pool is 36 feet long and 20 feet wide. What is the total amount of water, in cubic feet, needed to fill the pool to a depth of 4 feet? Choices: A: 800 B: 864 C: 2,880 D: 5,760

text.en: The pool's volume is length × width × depth. Substituting the dimensions gives 36 × 20 × 4 = 2,880 cubic feet.
text.es: El volumen de la piscina es largo × ancho × profundidad. Al sustituir las dimensiones se obtiene 36 × 20 × 4 = 2,880 pies cúbicos.

alt.en: Question 42. Kim’s class voted on a location for a field trip. 3/4 of the class voted for the museum. 1/8 of the class voted for the zoo. The rest of the class voted for the nature park. What fraction of the class voted for the nature park? Choices: A: 1/8 B: 1/2 C: 5/8 D: 7/8

text.en: Convert 3/4 to eighths: 3/4 = 6/8. The museum and zoo received 6/8 + 1/8 = 7/8 of the votes, leaving 1 − 7/8 = 1/8 for the nature park.
text.es: Se convierte 3/4 a octavos: 3/4 = 6/8. El museo y el zoológico recibieron 6/8 + 1/8 = 7/8 de los votos, por lo que queda 1 − 7/8 = 1/8 para el parque natural.

alt.en: Question 43. Jen determined the masses of 7 rocks. She recorded the mass of each rock on the line plot titled “MASS OF ROCKS.” The horizontal number line, labeled “Mass (kilograms),” has arrows at both ends and tick marks labeled 0, 1/4, 2/4, 3/4, and 1 from left to right. There are 2 X marks above 1/4, 3 X marks above 2/4, and 2 X marks above 3/4. There are no X marks above 0 or 1. What is the total mass, in kilograms, of the 7 rocks? Choices: A: 1 5/8 B: 1 1/2 C: 3 1/4 D: 3 1/2

text.en: The plot shows two rocks at 1/4 kilogram, three at 2/4, and two at 3/4. Their total is 2(1/4) + 3(2/4) + 2(3/4) = 14/4 = 3 1/2 kilograms.
text.es: La gráfica muestra dos rocas de 1/4 de kilogramo, tres de 2/4 y dos de 3/4. El total es 2(1/4) + 3(2/4) + 2(3/4) = 14/4 = 3 1/2 kilogramos.

alt.en: Question 44. To feed his plants, Logan creates a mixture that requires 3/8 cup of plant food for every gallon of water. If he uses 10 1/2 gallons of water, what is the total amount of plant food he needs? Choices: A: 3 15/16 cups. B: 6 3/10 cups. C: 10 3/16 cups. D: 10 7/8 cups.

text.en: Logan needs 3/8 cup per gallon and uses 10 1/2 = 21/2 gallons. Multiplying gives 3/8 × 21/2 = 63/16 = 3 15/16 cups.
text.es: Logan necesita 3/8 de taza por galón y usa 10 1/2 = 21/2 galones. Al multiplicar se obtiene 3/8 × 21/2 = 63/16 = 3 15/16 tazas.

alt.en: Question 45. Which expression is equivalent to 4 + [4 × (5 − 2)] ÷ 2? Choices: A: 4 + 12 ÷ 2. B: 4 + 18 ÷ 2. C: 8 × 3 ÷ 2. D: 8 × 5 − 1.

text.en: Evaluate inside the parentheses first: 5 − 2 = 3. Then 4 × 3 = 12, while the division by 2 remains, so the expression is equivalent to 4 + 12 ÷ 2.
text.es: Primero se evalúa dentro de los paréntesis: 5 − 2 = 3. Luego, 4 × 3 = 12, mientras que la división entre 2 permanece, así que la expresión equivale a 4 + 12 ÷ 2.

title.en: New York Grade 6 Math — 2016 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 6 — preguntas publicadas de 2016

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2016 New York State Grade 6 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 6.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016.

sourceTitle.en: 2016 NYS Grade 6 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 6.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016

alt.en: Question 11. Jason has a coupon for $2.50 off any electronic book from an online book store. If the original price, in dollars, of an electronic book is p and the discounted price, in dollars, is d, which table shows the relationship between p and d? Choices: A: Table: p = 3.00, 4.00, 5.00, 6.00; corresponding d = 0.50, 1.50, 2.50, 3.50. B: Table: p = 3.00, 4.00, 5.00, 6.00; corresponding d = 5.50, 6.50, 7.50, 8.50. C: Table: p = 3.00, 4.00, 5.00, 6.00; corresponding d = 2.50, 2.50, 2.50, 2.50. D: Table: p = 3.00, 4.00, 5.00, 6.00; corresponding d = 7.50, 10.00, 12.50, 15.00.

text.en: The coupon subtracts $2.50 from every original price, so d = p − 2.50. For example, $3.00 becomes $0.50 and $6.00 becomes $3.50, exactly the pairs in table A.
text.es: El cupón resta $2.50 de cada precio original, así que d = p − 2.50. Por ejemplo, $3.00 se convierte en $0.50 y $6.00 en $3.50, exactamente los pares de la tabla A.

alt.en: Question 12. Which pair of expressions below are equivalent? Choices: A: 7(2x) and 9x. B: 3x + 5x and 15x. C: 4(2x − 6) and 8x − 24. D: x + x + x + x and x⁴.

text.en: Apply the distributive property: 4(2x − 6) = 4(2x) − 4(6) = 8x − 24. Therefore, the two expressions in choice C have the same value for every x.
text.es: Aplica la propiedad distributiva: 4(2x − 6) = 4(2x) − 4(6) = 8x − 24. Por lo tanto, las dos expresiones de la opción C tienen el mismo valor para todo x.

alt.en: Question 13. An art teacher had 2/3 gallon of paint to pour into containers. If he poured 1/8 gallon of paint into each container until he ran out of paint, how many containers had paint in them, including the one that was partially filled? Choices: A: 1. B: 3. C: 5. D: 6.

text.en: Divide the total paint by the amount for each container: (2/3) ÷ (1/8) = (2/3)(8) = 16/3 = 5 1/3. Five containers are full and a sixth is partially filled, so 6 containers have paint.
text.es: Divide la pintura total entre la cantidad para cada recipiente: (2/3) ÷ (1/8) = (2/3)(8) = 16/3 = 5 1/3. Cinco recipientes quedan llenos y un sexto queda parcialmente lleno, así que 6 recipientes tienen pintura.

alt.en: Question 14. The coordinates of point F are (1, 0.5) and the coordinates of point G are (−1, −0.5). Which coordinate plane below correctly shows the locations of points F and G? Each plane has a horizontal x-axis, positive to the right, and a vertical y-axis, positive upward. Choices: A: F is plotted at (1, 0.5), and G is plotted at (−1, −0.5). B: F is plotted at (0.5, 1), and G is plotted at (−0.5, −1). C: F is plotted at (1, −0.5), and G is plotted at (−1, 0.5). D: F is plotted at (−1, 0.5), and G is plotted at (1, −0.5).

text.en: Point F is at (1, 0.5), one unit right and one-half unit up from the origin. Point G is at (−1, −0.5), one unit left and one-half unit down; plane A shows both locations.
text.es: El punto F está en (1, 0.5), una unidad a la derecha y media unidad arriba del origen. El punto G está en (−1, −0.5), una unidad a la izquierda y media unidad abajo; el plano A muestra ambas ubicaciones.

alt.en: Question 15. Last year, Chesa made 32 one-cup servings of soup for a school party. This year, she will make two times the amount of soup that she made last year. How many gallons of soup will Chesa make this year? Choices: A: 64. B: 16. C: 4. D: 2.

text.en: Twice last year’s soup is 2 × 32 = 64 one-cup servings. Since 1 gallon contains 16 cups, 64 ÷ 16 = 4 gallons, which is choice C.
text.es: El doble de la sopa del año pasado es 2 × 32 = 64 porciones de una taza. Como 1 galón contiene 16 tazas, 64 ÷ 16 = 4 galones, que corresponde a la opción C.

alt.en: Question 16. A shelf has four books on it. The weight, in pounds, of each of the four books on the shelf is listed below: 2.5, 3.2, 2.7, 2.3. Which inequality represents the weight, w, of any book chosen from the shelf? Choices: A: w > 2.3. B: w < 2.4. C: w > 3.2. D: w < 3.3.

text.en: The greatest listed book weight is 3.2 pounds. Every listed weight is therefore less than 3.3 pounds, so w < 3.3 describes any book on the shelf.
text.es: El mayor peso de los libros es 3.2 libras. Por lo tanto, todos los pesos son menores que 3.3 libras, así que w < 3.3 describe cualquier libro del estante.

alt.en: Question 17. The area of the triangle below is 2/5 square foot. The diagram is a right triangle with a horizontal base and a vertical height of 6/5 ft; a right-angle marker identifies the perpendicular base and height. What is the length, in feet, of the base of the triangle? Choices: A: 24/25. B: 25/24. C: 2/3. D: 3/2.

text.en: Use the triangle area formula with base b: (1/2)b(6/5) = 2/5. This simplifies to (3/5)b = 2/5, and multiplying by 5/3 gives b = 2/3 foot.
text.es: Usa la fórmula del área del triángulo con base b: (1/2)b(6/5) = 2/5. Esto se simplifica a (3/5)b = 2/5, y al multiplicar por 5/3 se obtiene b = 2/3 de pie.

alt.en: Question 18. Point M represents the opposite of −1/2, and point N represents the opposite of 5/2. Which number line correctly shows points M and N? Each horizontal number line increases from left to right, has arrows at both ends, and has integer ticks from −3 through 3; points halfway between consecutive integers are marked with filled dots. Choices: A: M is at −1/2, and N is at 5/2. B: N is at −5/2, and M is at 1/2. C: M is at −2, and N is at 1/2. D: N is at −1/2, and M is at 2.

text.en: The opposite of −1/2 is 1/2, so M belongs halfway between 0 and 1. The opposite of 5/2 is −5/2, so N belongs halfway between −3 and −2; number line B shows both.
text.es: El opuesto de −1/2 es 1/2, así que M va a mitad de camino entre 0 y 1. El opuesto de 5/2 es −5/2, así que N va entre −3 y −2; la recta B muestra ambos puntos.

alt.en: Question 19. The weight of an object on the moon, m, is about 1/6 of the object’s weight on Earth, e. Which equation represents the approximate weight of an object on the moon in terms of the object’s weight on Earth? Choices: A: m = 1/6 + e. B: m = e/6. C: m = 6 + e. D: m = 6e.

text.en: The moon weight is one sixth of the Earth weight. Multiplying e by 1/6 gives m = (1/6)e, which is equivalently written m = e/6.
text.es: El peso en la Luna es una sexta parte del peso en la Tierra. Al multiplicar e por 1/6 se obtiene m = (1/6)e, que también se escribe m = e/6.

alt.en: Question 20. The table titled “Possible Baseball Games Won” shows different possibilities for the number of games a team would need to win to maintain a certain percentage of wins. The columns are “Number of Games Won” and “Number of Games Played.” The rows, written as games won to games played, are 6 to 10, 24 to 40, 36 to 60, and 42 to 70. Which ratio of the number of games won to the number of games played could also be included in this table? Choices: A: 18:20. B: 30:20. C: 18:30. D: 50:30.

text.en: Each table pair has games-won to games-played ratio 6/10 = 3/5. The ratio 18/30 also simplifies to 3/5, so 18:30 can be added to the table.
text.es: Cada par de la tabla tiene la razón de juegos ganados a juegos jugados 6/10 = 3/5. La razón 18/30 también se simplifica a 3/5, así que 18:30 puede incluirse en la tabla.

alt.en: Question 21. A table titled “Coordinates” lists the coordinates of four points. Its columns are x and y. The four rows are x = 1, y = 5; x = 2, y = 7; x = 3, y = 9; and x = 4, y = 11. If x represents any number in the first column, which expression can always be used to find the value of y in the second column? Choices: A: 5x. B: x + 2. C: x + 4. D: 2x + 3.

text.en: The output increases by 2 whenever x increases by 1, so the rule has coefficient 2. Substitution confirms y = 2x + 3: x = 1 gives 5 and x = 4 gives 11.
text.es: El valor de salida aumenta 2 cuando x aumenta 1, así que la regla tiene coeficiente 2. La sustitución confirma y = 2x + 3: x = 1 da 5 y x = 4 da 11.

alt.en: Question 22. Which expression is represented by the phrase “the square of y decreased by the quotient of 28 and 7”? Choices: A: 28/7 − y². B: y² − 28/7. C: 28/(7 − y²). D: 28/(y² − 7).

text.en: The square of y is y², and the quotient of 28 and 7 is 28/7. Decreasing the first quantity by the second gives y² − 28/7, which is choice B.
text.es: El cuadrado de y es y² y el cociente de 28 y 7 es 28/7. Disminuir la primera cantidad por la segunda da y² − 28/7, que es la opción B.

alt.en: Question 27. A recipe for lemonade calls for 1 cup of sugar and 5 cups of water. How much sugar is used per cup of water? Choices: A: 1/6 cup. B: 1/5 cup. C: 1/4 cup. D: 5/1 cup.

text.en: The unit rate compares 1 cup of sugar with 5 cups of water: 1 ÷ 5 = 1/5. Therefore, the recipe uses 1/5 cup of sugar per cup of water.
text.es: La tasa unitaria compara 1 taza de azúcar con 5 tazas de agua: 1 ÷ 5 = 1/5. Por lo tanto, la receta usa 1/5 de taza de azúcar por cada taza de agua.

alt.en: Question 28. Sam paid $8.28 for 18 stamps. At this rate, how much would it cost Sam to buy 12 stamps? Choices: A: $2.19. B: $2.28. C: $3.72. D: $5.52.

text.en: First find the price per stamp: $8.28 ÷ 18 = $0.46. At that same rate, 12 stamps cost 12 × $0.46 = $5.52.
text.es: Primero calcula el precio por estampilla: $8.28 ÷ 18 = $0.46. A esa misma tasa, 12 estampillas cuestan 12 × $0.46 = $5.52.

alt.en: Question 29. John’s friend told him that he could earn $49 for handing out flyers at a local concert. John wants to calculate the hourly rate. If he works a total of 3.5 hours, the equation 3.5x = 49 can be used to determine his hourly rate. What would John’s hourly rate be, in dollars? Choices: A: $1.40. B: $14.00. C: $45.50. D: $171.50.

text.en: Solve 3.5x = 49 by dividing both sides by 3.5: x = 49 ÷ 3.5 = 14. John’s hourly rate is therefore $14.00.
text.es: Resuelve 3.5x = 49 dividiendo ambos lados entre 3.5: x = 49 ÷ 3.5 = 14. Por lo tanto, la tarifa por hora de John es $14.00.

alt.en: Question 30. Theo made sails for a model boat. He cut along the diagonal of a rectangular piece of cloth to make two sails. The rectangle is 4 ft tall and 1 1/6 ft wide. A dashed diagonal cut runs from the upper-left corner to the lower-right corner, dividing the rectangle into two congruent triangular sails. A right-angle marker appears at the lower-left corner of the rectangle. What was the area, in square feet, of one sail? Choices: A: 9 1/3. B: 4 2/3. C: 2 7/12. D: 2 1/3.

text.en: The rectangle has area 4 × 1 1/6 = 4 × 7/6 = 14/3 square feet. A diagonal divides it into two equal triangles, so one sail has area (14/3) ÷ 2 = 7/3 = 2 1/3 square feet.
text.es: El rectángulo tiene un área de 4 × 1 1/6 = 4 × 7/6 = 14/3 pies cuadrados. La diagonal lo divide en dos triángulos iguales, así que un velamen tiene (14/3) ÷ 2 = 7/3 = 2 1/3 pies cuadrados.

alt.en: Question 34. The area of an airplane’s wings is related to the airplane’s lifting force, which holds the airplane in the air. The table below lists several wing areas and the corresponding lifting forces. A table titled “AIRPLANE LIFTING FORCE” has columns “Area of Wings (square feet)” and “Lifting Force (pounds).” Its rows are: 125 and 1,875; 150 and 2,250; 175 and 2,625; 250 and 3,750; x and 5,625; 420 and y. The ratio of lifting force to area is equivalent for all pairs in the table. What are the values of x and y? Choices: A: x = 375 square feet and y = 7,500 pounds. B: x = 335 square feet and y = 7,500 pounds. C: x = 375 square feet and y = 6,300 pounds. D: x = 335 square feet and y = 6,300 pounds.

text.en: The constant lifting-force rate is 1,875 ÷ 125 = 15 pounds per square foot. Thus x = 5,625 ÷ 15 = 375 square feet and y = 420 × 15 = 6,300 pounds.
text.es: La tasa constante de fuerza de sustentación es 1,875 ÷ 125 = 15 libras por pie cuadrado. Entonces x = 5,625 ÷ 15 = 375 pies cuadrados y y = 420 × 15 = 6,300 libras.

alt.en: Question 36. Simon used 3 pears and 9 apples to make a fruit salad. What was the ratio of the number of pears to the number of apples in the fruit salad? Choices: A: 1:3. B: 1:4. C: 1:6. D: 1:9.

text.en: The pears-to-apples ratio is 3:9. Dividing both parts by their common factor 3 gives 1:3, so there is one pear for every three apples.
text.es: La razón de peras a manzanas es 3:9. Al dividir ambas partes entre el factor común 3 se obtiene 1:3, es decir, una pera por cada tres manzanas.

alt.en: Question 37. The summit of a volcano is 10 kilometers (km) above the ocean floor. A vertical cross-section diagram shows the volcano rising from the ocean floor through sea level to the summit. The ocean floor is labeled “Ocean Floor (5 km below sea level),” a horizontal line across the volcano is labeled “Sea Level,” and the top is labeled “Summit.” If the ocean floor has an elevation of −5 kilometers, which statement describes the elevation of sea level and the summit? Choices: A: The elevation of sea level is 0 km and the elevation of the summit is 5 km. B: The elevation of sea level is 5 km and the elevation of the summit is 5 km. C: The elevation of sea level is 0 km and the elevation of the summit is 10 km. D: The elevation of sea level is 5 km and the elevation of the summit is 10 km.

text.en: Sea level is defined as 0 kilometers. The ocean floor is at −5 kilometers, and the summit is 10 kilometers above it, so −5 + 10 = 5; the summit is at 5 kilometers.
text.es: El nivel del mar se define como 0 kilómetros. El fondo oceánico está a −5 kilómetros y la cumbre está 10 kilómetros más arriba, así que −5 + 10 = 5; la cumbre está a 5 kilómetros.

alt.en: Question 38. The Frenchtown Roller Rink charges a $5 entrance fee and an hourly rate for roller skating. The total cost for roller skating depends on the number of hours a person skates. A table titled “ROLLER SKATING COST” represents the total cost of skating for different numbers of hours. Its columns are “Number of Hours (h)” and “Total Cost in Dollars (c).” Its rows are: h = 0, c = 5; h = 1, c = 8; h = 2, c = 11; h = 3, c = 14; h = 4, c = 17. Which equation represents the relationship between the cost, c, and the number of hours, h? Choices: A: c = 8h. B: c = 5h + 3. C: c = 2h + 7. D: c = 3h + 5.

text.en: The total cost rises by $3 for each additional hour, so the hourly rate is 3. The $5 entrance fee is the value when h = 0, giving c = 3h + 5.
text.es: El costo total aumenta $3 por cada hora adicional, así que la tarifa por hora es 3. La entrada de $5 es el valor cuando h = 0, lo que da c = 3h + 5.

alt.en: Question 39. Fei Yen’s dog eats 8 ounces of dog food each day. Fei Yen bought a 28-pound bag of dog food. How many 8-ounce servings are in a 28-pound bag of dog food? Choices: A: 14. B: 56. C: 224. D: 448.

text.en: Convert the bag to ounces: 28 × 16 = 448 ounces. Dividing by 8 ounces per serving gives 448 ÷ 8 = 56 servings.
text.es: Convierte la bolsa a onzas: 28 × 16 = 448 onzas. Al dividir entre 8 onzas por porción se obtiene 448 ÷ 8 = 56 porciones.

alt.en: Question 40. Point A and point B are placed on a number line. Point A is located at −20 and point B is 5 less than point A. Which statement about point B is true? Choices: A: It is located at −25 and is to the right of point A on the number line. B: It is located at −15 and is to the right of point A on the number line. C: It is located at −25 and is to the left of point A on the number line. D: It is located at −15 and is to the left of point A on the number line.

text.en: Five less than −20 is −20 − 5 = −25. Since −25 is smaller than −20, point B is located to the left of point A.
text.es: Cinco menos que −20 es −20 − 5 = −25. Como −25 es menor que −20, el punto B está a la izquierda del punto A.

alt.en: Question 41. Chakan worked at the warehouse after school. He earned $9.25 per hour stacking boxes. Which equation correctly relates Chakan’s total earnings, d, to the number of hours he worked, h? Choices: A: d = 9.25h. B: h = 9.25d. C: d = 9.25/h. D: h = 9.25/d.

text.en: Each hour earns $9.25, so multiplying the number of hours h by 9.25 gives the total dollars earned: d = 9.25h.
text.es: Cada hora produce $9.25, así que al multiplicar el número de horas h por 9.25 se obtienen los dólares totales: d = 9.25h.

alt.en: Question 42. Bronson is using a coordinate plane to design a rectangular swimming pool. He will plot points on the coordinate plane to mark the vertices of the rectangular pool bottom. If Bronson plots the first three points at (5, 3), (5, 13), and (30, 13), what would be the coordinates of the fourth point? A blank square coordinate grid is provided for working out the problem; it has evenly spaced horizontal and vertical grid lines and no visible axis labels or plotted points. Choices: A: (30, 5). B: (20, 13). C: (5, 28). D: (30, 3).

text.en: A rectangle’s opposite vertices share coordinates. The missing vertex must have x = 30 like (30, 13) and y = 3 like (5, 3), so its coordinates are (30, 3).
text.es: Los vértices opuestos de un rectángulo comparten coordenadas. El vértice faltante debe tener x = 30 como (30, 13) y y = 3 como (5, 3), así que sus coordenadas son (30, 3).

alt.en: Question 43. Residents of a small city voted on whether to allow a developer to build a shopping center. The number of votes in favor of the shopping center was 4,400. The number of votes against the shopping center was 17,600. What percent of the voters were in favor of building the shopping center? Choices: A: 20% B: 25% C: 40% D: 44%

text.en: There were 4,400 + 17,600 = 22,000 total votes. The fraction in favor is 4,400/22,000 = 0.20, and 0.20 × 100% = 20%.
text.es: Hubo 4,400 + 17,600 = 22,000 votos en total. La fracción a favor es 4,400/22,000 = 0.20, y 0.20 × 100% = 20%.

alt.en: Question 44. Maddy had a piece of ribbon that was 3 1/2 yards long. She used this ribbon to make bows. Each bow was made from a piece of the ribbon that was 3/4 yard long. This situation can be represented by the equation 3 1/2 ÷ 3/4 = 4 2/3. Which statement best describes what the quotient 4 2/3 represents in this situation? Choices: A: Maddy had bows that were each 4 2/3 yards long. B: Maddy had 4 2/3 yards of ribbon left after making the bows. C: Maddy made 4 bows from the piece of ribbon and had 2/3 of a yard left. D: Maddy made 4 bows from the piece of ribbon and had enough left for 2/3 of a bow.

text.en: The quotient is 3 1/2 ÷ 3/4 = 7/2 × 4/3 = 14/3 = 4 2/3. This means 4 complete bows can be made, with enough ribbon left for 2/3 of another bow.
text.es: El cociente es 3 1/2 ÷ 3/4 = 7/2 × 4/3 = 14/3 = 4 2/3. Esto significa que se pueden hacer 4 lazos completos y queda cinta suficiente para 2/3 de otro lazo.

alt.en: Question 45. The figure is the net of a triangular pyramid. The net consists of four equilateral triangles: one central, downward-pointing triangle outlined with dashed lines, with one outward-pointing triangle attached to each of its three sides. One side of each equilateral triangle is 5 cm, and the perpendicular height of each triangle is 4.33 cm; the given height is rounded to the nearest hundredth. What is the surface area of the pyramid in square centimeters? Choices: A: 86.6 B: 43.3 C: 32.48 D: 10.83

text.en: The net contains 4 equilateral triangles. Each has area (1/2)(5)(4.33) = 10.825 square centimeters, so the total surface area is 4 × 10.825 = 43.3 square centimeters.
text.es: La red contiene 4 triángulos equiláteros. Cada uno tiene un área de (1/2)(5)(4.33) = 10.825 centímetros cuadrados, así que el área total es 4 × 10.825 = 43.3 centímetros cuadrados.

alt.en: Question 46. Zelma buys p pounds of bananas for 40 cents per pound. She pays the clerk with a twenty-dollar bill. The clerk subtracts the total cost of the bananas from the twenty-dollar bill to determine the amount of change to give Zelma. Which expression represents the amount of change Zelma should receive? Choices: A: p − 20 B: 20 − 40p C: 20 − 0.40p D: 0.40p − 20

text.en: At $0.40 per pound, p pounds of bananas cost 0.40p dollars. The change from a $20 bill is the amount paid minus that cost, or 20 − 0.40p.
text.es: A $0.40 por libra, p libras de bananas cuestan 0.40p dólares. El cambio de un billete de $20 es el monto pagado menos ese costo, es decir, 20 − 0.40p.

alt.en: Question 47. A table titled “Masses and Corresponding Weights” lists four masses and their corresponding approximate weights on Earth. The columns are Mass (kilograms) and Weight (Newtons). The data rows are: 20 kilograms, 196 Newtons; 50 kilograms, 490 Newtons; x kilograms, 1078 Newtons; 130 kilograms, 1274 Newtons; and 140 kilograms, 1372 Newtons. The ratio of weight to mass is constant. Which statement describes the ratio of weight to mass and the value of x in the table? Choices: A: The ratio is 98/10; x = 90. B: The ratio is 98/10; x = 110. C: The ratio is 10/98; x = 90. D: The ratio is 10/98; x = 110.

text.en: The weight-to-mass ratio is 196/20 = 9.8 = 98/10 newtons per kilogram. Using that rate, x = 1,078 ÷ 9.8 = 110 kilograms.
text.es: La razón de peso a masa es 196/20 = 9.8 = 98/10 newtons por kilogramo. Usando esa tasa, x = 1,078 ÷ 9.8 = 110 kilogramos.

alt.en: Question 48. What value of y makes the equation below true? y + 2.9 = 11. Choices: A: 8.1 B: 8.9 C: 9.1 D: 13.9

text.en: Subtract 2.9 from both sides of y + 2.9 = 11. This gives y = 11 − 2.9 = 8.1.
text.es: Resta 2.9 de ambos lados de y + 2.9 = 11. Esto da y = 11 − 2.9 = 8.1. Por lo tanto, y es 8.1.

alt.en: Question 49. A scientist studied the migration patterns of two types of whales. The humpback whales traveled 2,240 miles in 28 days. The gray whales traveled 2,368 miles in 32 days. If the humpback whales had traveled at the same rate for 32 days, how many more miles would they have traveled than the gray whales? Choices: A: 128 B: 192 C: 280 D: 408

text.en: The humpback rate is 2,240 ÷ 28 = 80 miles per day, so in 32 days they would travel 80 × 32 = 2,560 miles. The difference from 2,368 is 2,560 − 2,368 = 192 miles.
text.es: La tasa de las ballenas jorobadas es 2,240 ÷ 28 = 80 millas por día, así que en 32 días recorrerían 80 × 32 = 2,560 millas. La diferencia con 2,368 es 2,560 − 2,368 = 192 millas.

title.en: New York Grade 7 Math — 2016 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 7 — preguntas publicadas de 2016

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2016 New York State Grade 7 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 7.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016.

sourceTitle.en: 2016 NYS Grade 7 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 7.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016

alt.en: Question 1. The drawing of a building has a scale of 1 inch to 30 feet. The building is represented by a three-dimensional rectangular structure. Its vertical height in the drawing is labeled 1.25 in., and the width of its front face is labeled 0.75 in. What is the actual height, in feet, of the building? Choices: A: 22.5 B: 24 C: 37.5 D: 40

text.en: The drawing height is 1.25 inches and each inch represents 30 feet. Multiplying 1.25 × 30 = 37.5 gives an actual building height of 37.5 feet.
text.es: La altura del dibujo es 1.25 pulgadas y cada pulgada representa 30 pies. Al multiplicar 1.25 × 30 = 37.5 se obtiene una altura real de 37.5 pies.

alt.en: Question 2. What is the value of the expression below? −0.75 − (−2/5) + 0.4 + (−3/4) Choices: A: −1.5 B: −0.7 C: 0.8 D: 2.3

Question 7. Lehana and Marty each opened a savings account with a deposit of $100. Lehana earned 2.5% simple interest per year. Marty earned 2% simple interest per year.
