Neither of them made additional deposits or withdrawals. How much more did Lehana receive in interest than Marty after three years? Choices: A: $0.50 B: $1.50 C: $5.00 D: $15.00

text.en: Lehana earns $100(0.025)(3) = $7.50 in simple interest, while Marty earns $100(0.02)(3) = $6.00. The difference is $7.50 − $6.00 = $1.50.
text.es: Lehana gana $100(0.025)(3) = $7.50 de interés simple, mientras que Marty gana $100(0.02)(3) = $6.00. La diferencia es $7.50 − $6.00 = $1.50.

alt.en: Question 8. Addison wants to ride her bicycle more than 80 miles this week. She has already ridden her bicycle 18 miles. Which inequality could be used to determine the mean number of miles, m, she would need to ride her bicycle each day for six more days to achieve her goal? Choices: A: 6m + 18 < 80 B: 6m − 18 < 80 C: 6m + 18 > 80 D: 6m − 18 > 80

text.en: Riding an average of m miles for each of 6 days adds 6m miles to the 18 already ridden. To exceed 80 miles, the total must satisfy 6m + 18 > 80.
text.es: Recorrer un promedio de m millas durante cada uno de 6 días agrega 6m a las 18 millas ya recorridas. Para superar 80 millas, el total debe cumplir 6m + 18 > 80.

alt.en: Question 9. An electronic sign that showed the speed of motorists was installed on a road. Two line plots show the speeds of some motorists before and after the sign was installed. Both horizontal axes are labeled Speed (miles per hour), have arrows in both directions, and contain tick marks at every 1 mile per hour from 20 through 40, with numbered labels at 20, 25, 30, 35, and 40. Before sign installation, the plotted speeds are: one motorist at 20, one at 24, four at 25, two at 30, two at 35, one at 38, and one at 40 miles per hour. After sign installation, the plotted speeds are: one motorist at 20, one at 22, four at 25, one at 28, three at 30, and two at 35 miles per hour. Based on these data, which statement is true about the speeds of motorists after the sign was installed? Choices: A: The mean speed and the range of the speeds of the motorists decreased. B: The median speed and the range of the speeds of the motorists increased. C: The mean speed of the motorists decreased and the range of the speeds increased. D: The median speed of the motorists increased and the range of the speeds decreased.

text.en: Before installation, the 12 speeds total 352, so the mean is 352/12 = 29 1/3 mph and the range is 40 − 20 = 20. Afterward, the total is 330, so the mean is 27.5 mph and the range is 35 − 20 = 15; both decreased.
text.es: Antes de instalar el letrero, las 12 velocidades suman 352, así que la media es 352/12 = 29 1/3 mph y el rango es 40 − 20 = 20. Después suman 330, así que la media es 27.5 mph y el rango es 35 − 20 = 15; ambos disminuyeron.

alt.en: Question 10. A number, n, is multiplied by −5/8. The product is −0.4. What is the value of n? Choices: A: −16/25 B: −1/4 C: 1/4 D: 16/25

Question 11. The perimeter of a certain pentagon is 10.5 centimeters. Four sides of this pentagon have the same length, in centimeters, h, and the other side has a length of 1.7 centimeters. The pentagon diagram labels four sides h and the remaining upper-left side 1.7 cm. What is the value of h? Choices: A: 2.2 B: 3.7 C: 4.8 D: 8.8

text.en: The four equal sides total 10.5 − 1.7 = 8.8 centimeters. Dividing equally gives h = 8.8 ÷ 4 = 2.2 centimeters.
text.es: Los cuatro lados iguales miden en total 10.5 − 1.7 = 8.8 centímetros. Al dividir en partes iguales se obtiene h = 8.8 ÷ 4 = 2.2 centímetros.

alt.en: Question 12. A school principal wants to determine which type of speaker the students prefer to invite to an assembly for the entire student population. Which survey method would produce the best representative sample? Choices: A: survey every fifth person who shops at a mall B: survey all of the students on the student council C: survey every tenth student entering the school one morning D: survey all of the students who went to the last basketball game

text.en: Selecting every tenth student entering school samples students from the population the principal wants to study and avoids choosing only one special-interest group. That systematic sample best represents the entire student body.
text.es: Seleccionar a cada décimo estudiante que entra a la escuela toma alumnos de la población que se quiere estudiar y evita elegir solo un grupo de interés particular. Esa muestra sistemática representa mejor a todo el alumnado.

alt.en: Question 13. Henry has a fair number pyramid with four faces and a spinner with three equal-sized colored sections. The possible outcomes are represented by branching diagrams. The Number Pyramid has four possible outcomes labeled 1, 2, 3, and 4. The Spinner has three possible outcomes labeled Red, Blue, and Green. What is the probability that the number pyramid will land on three and the spinner will stop on blue? Choices: A: 1/12. B: 3/12. C: 4/12. D: 7/12.

text.en: The probability of rolling a 3 is 1/4, and the probability of spinning blue is 1/3. The events are independent, so their joint probability is (1/4)(1/3) = 1/12.
text.es: La probabilidad de obtener 3 es 1/4 y la probabilidad de obtener azul es 1/3. Los sucesos son independientes, así que la probabilidad conjunta es (1/4)(1/3) = 1/12.

alt.en: Question 14. A company ordered 10 boxed lunches from a deli for $74.50. Each boxed lunch cost the same amount. Which equation represents the proportional relationship between y, the total cost of the boxed lunches, and x, the number of boxed lunches? Choices: A: 7.45x = y. B: 7.45/x = 10/y. C: 74.50x = y. D: 74.50/x = 10/y.

text.en: The constant cost per lunch is $74.50 ÷ 10 = $7.45. Multiplying $7.45 by x lunches gives the total cost, so the proportional equation is y = 7.45x.
text.es: El costo constante por almuerzo es $74.50 ÷ 10 = $7.45. Al multiplicar $7.45 por x almuerzos se obtiene el costo total, así que la ecuación proporcional es y = 7.45x.

alt.en: Question 15. What is the value of the expression (2/3 − 5/6) ÷ (3/4)? Choices: A: −2/9. B: −1/8. C: 1/8. D: 2/9.

Question 16. Which event is most likely to occur? Choices: A: flipping a fair coin, with sides labeled heads and tails, and the coin landing on tails. B: choosing a marble out of a bag, with nine blue marbles and one red marble, and the marble being red. C: rolling a fair number cube, with faces labeled one to six, and the cube landing on a number less than six. D: spinning the arrow on a spinner, with four equal sectors labeled one to four, and the arrow landing on a number greater than one.

text.en: Compare the event probabilities: tails is 1/2, red marble is 1/10, a number below 6 on the cube is 5/6, and a spinner number above 1 is 3/4. Since 5/6 is greatest, event C is most likely.
text.es: Compara las probabilidades: cruz es 1/2, una canica roja es 1/10, un número menor que 6 en el cubo es 5/6 y un número mayor que 1 en la ruleta es 3/4. El suceso C es el más probable porque 5/6 es la mayor.

alt.en: Question 20. A trailer will be used to transport several 40-kilogram crates to a store. The greatest amount of weight that can be loaded onto the trailer is 1,050 kilograms. An 82-kilogram crate has already been loaded onto the trailer. What is the greatest number of 40-kilogram crates that can also be loaded onto the trailer? Choices: A: 24. B: 25. C: 26. D: 27.

text.en: After the 82-kilogram crate, 1,050 − 82 = 968 kilograms remain. Since 968 ÷ 40 = 24.2, only 24 whole 40-kilogram crates can be added without exceeding the limit.
text.es: Después de cargar la caja de 82 kilogramos quedan 1,050 − 82 = 968 kilogramos. Como 968 ÷ 40 = 24.2, solo se pueden agregar 24 cajas completas de 40 kilogramos sin superar el límite.

alt.en: Question 21. What is the value of the expression 8/15 ÷ (−0.35)? Choices: A: −75/14. B: −32/21. C: −21/32. D: −14/75.

text.en: Convert −0.35 to −7/20, then divide by multiplying by the reciprocal: (8/15) ÷ (−7/20) = (8/15)(−20/7) = −160/105 = −32/21.
text.es: Convierte −0.35 en −7/20 y divide multiplicando por el recíproco: (8/15) ÷ (−7/20) = (8/15)(−20/7) = −160/105 = −32/21.

alt.en: Question 22. What is the value of the expression below? (3 1/2 − 9 3/4) ÷ (−2.5). Choices: A: −2.5. B: −2.3. C: 2.3. D: 2.5.

text.en: Convert the mixed numbers: 3 1/2 − 9 3/4 = 3.5 − 9.75 = −6.25. Dividing by −2.5 gives −6.25 ÷ −2.5 = 2.5.
text.es: Convierte los números mixtos: 3 1/2 − 9 3/4 = 3.5 − 9.75 = −6.25. Al dividir entre −2.5 se obtiene −6.25 ÷ −2.5 = 2.5.

alt.en: Question 27. Which expression is equivalent to 4 − (−7)? Choices: A: 7 + 4. B: 4 − 7. C: −7 − 4. D: −4 + 7.

text.en: Subtracting a negative is equivalent to adding its opposite: 4 − (−7) = 4 + 7. By the commutative property, 4 + 7 = 7 + 4.
text.es: Restar un número negativo equivale a sumar su opuesto: 4 − (−7) = 4 + 7. Por la propiedad conmutativa, 4 + 7 = 7 + 4.

alt.en: Question 28. The elevation at ground level is 0 feet. An elevator starts 90 feet below ground level. After traveling for 15 seconds, the elevator is 20 feet below ground level. Which statement describes the elevator’s rate of change in elevation during this 15-second interval? Choices: A: The elevator traveled upward at a rate of 6 feet per second. B: The elevator traveled upward at a rate of 4 2/3 feet per second. C: The elevator traveled downward at a rate of 6 feet per second. D: The elevator traveled downward at a rate of 4 2/3 feet per second.

text.en: The elevation changes from −90 to −20 feet, an increase of −20 − (−90) = 70 feet. Over 15 seconds, the upward rate is 70/15 = 14/3 = 4 2/3 feet per second.
text.es: La elevación cambia de −90 a −20 pies, un aumento de −20 − (−90) = 70 pies. En 15 segundos, la tasa ascendente es 70/15 = 14/3 = 4 2/3 pies por segundo.

alt.en: Question 29. Which expression represents the product of 3 and (5/4 n + 1.8)? Choices: A: 5.55n B: 9.15n C: 3.75n + 1.8 D: 3.75n + 5.4

text.en: Distribute 3 to both terms: 3((5/4)n + 1.8) = (15/4)n + 5.4. Since 15/4 = 3.75, the product is 3.75n + 5.4.
text.es: Distribuye 3 a ambos términos: 3((5/4)n + 1.8) = (15/4)n + 5.4. Como 15/4 = 3.75, el producto es 3.75n + 5.4.

alt.en: Question 33. Mike took a taxi from his home to the airport. The taxi driver charged an initial fee of $6 plus $3 per mile. The total fare was $24, not including the tip. How many miles did Mike travel by taxi on this ride? Choices: A: 2 B: 6 C: 8 D: 10

text.en: Subtract the $6 initial fee from the $24 fare to get $18 in mileage charges. At $3 per mile, the distance is $18 ÷ $3 = 6 miles.
text.es: Resta la tarifa inicial de $6 del total de $24 para obtener $18 de cargos por millaje. A $3 por milla, la distancia es $18 ÷ $3 = 6 millas.

alt.en: Question 34. The original selling price of a share of stock was d dollars. The selling price for a share of the same stock at a later date was represented by the expression 1.15(0.95d). Which description could explain what happened to the price of the share of stock? Choices: A: The price decreased by 5% and then increased by 0.15%. B: The price decreased by 95% and then increased by 0.15%. C: The price decreased by 5% and then increased by 15%. D: The price decreased by 95% and then increased by 15%.

text.en: Multiplying the original price by 0.95 keeps 95%, which means a 5% decrease. Multiplying that result by 1.15 then adds 15%, so description C matches 1.15(0.95d).
text.es: Multiplicar el precio original por 0.95 conserva el 95%, lo que significa una disminución del 5%. Multiplicar luego por 1.15 aumenta ese resultado un 15%, así que la descripción C corresponde a 1.15(0.95d).

alt.en: Question 35. A clothing store used a sign to advertise a discount on shirts. The rectangular sign reads: “DISCOUNT. Buy Two Shirts. Get 50% Off Third Shirt.” Ky wants to buy three shirts, which were originally priced $49.96 each. The store will discount the price of the third shirt and then apply a 7.1% tax to the total cost of all three shirts. Including the tax, what will be the mean cost of each shirt? Choices: A: $41.99 B: $42.70 C: $44.59 D: $45.18

text.en: The discounted third shirt costs $49.96(0.50) = $24.98, so the pretax total is $49.96 + $49.96 + $24.98 = $124.90. With tax, $124.90(1.071) = $133.7679; dividing by 3 and rounding gives a mean of $44.59.
text.es: La tercera camisa con descuento cuesta $49.96(0.50) = $24.98, así que el total antes de impuestos es $124.90. Con impuestos, $124.90(1.071) = $133.7679; al dividir entre 3 y redondear se obtiene un promedio de $44.59.

alt.en: Question 36. The results for a survey of 120 students who were selected randomly are listed: 60 students have a cell phone plan with company X; 36 students have a cell phone plan with company Y; and 24 students do not have a cell phone. The total population of students was 380. Based on the data, what is the best approximation for the total number of students who have a cell phone plan with company Y? Choices: A: 114 B: 127 C: 143 D: 163

text.en: In the sample, 36/120 = 0.30 of the students use company Y. Applying that proportion to 380 students gives 0.30 × 380 = 114 students.
text.es: En la muestra, 36/120 = 0.30 de los estudiantes usan la compañía Y. Al aplicar esa proporción a 380 estudiantes se obtiene 0.30 × 380 = 114 estudiantes.

alt.en: Question 37. Wallpaper was applied to one rectangular wall of a large room. A rectangular diagram labels the horizontal length as 42 feet and the vertical height as 25.5 feet. If the total cost of the wallpaper was $771.12, what was the cost, in dollars, of the wallpaper per square foot? Choices: A: $0.61 B: $0.72 C: $1.39 D: $1.65

text.en: The wall area is 42 × 25.5 = 1,071 square feet. Dividing the total wallpaper cost by the area gives $771.12 ÷ 1,071 = $0.72 per square foot.
text.es: El área de la pared es 42 × 25.5 = 1,071 pies cuadrados. Al dividir el costo total entre el área se obtiene $771.12 ÷ 1,071 = $0.72 por pie cuadrado.

alt.en: Question 38. Three friends own a landscaping business. The number of hours each friend spent on the same project is given in a table titled “HOURS WORKED ON LANDSCAPING PROJECT.” The columns are “Name” and “Hours Worked.” The rows are: Edgar, 17 1/4; Kelly, 18 1/4; Shawn, 14 1/2. In total, they earned $850 for the job. They put 15% of this amount into a joint savings account for future expenses. They then divided the rest proportionally based on the number of hours each one worked. How much money did Shawn receive? Choices: A: $209.53 B: $240.83 C: $283.48 D: $295.11

text.en: After saving 15%, the amount to divide is $850(0.85) = $722.50. The total time is 17.25 + 18.25 + 14.5 = 50 hours, so Shawn receives (14.5/50)($722.50) = $209.525, which rounds to $209.53.
text.es: Después de ahorrar el 15%, quedan $850(0.85) = $722.50 para repartir. El tiempo total es 17.25 + 18.25 + 14.5 = 50 horas, así que Shawn recibe (14.5/50)($722.50) = $209.525, que se redondea a $209.53.

alt.en: Question 39. Line KN represents a proportional relationship. Point N lies at (18, 12), as represented on a coordinate plane. The x-axis is labeled x and marked from 0 to 20 in increments of 2. The y-axis is labeled y and marked from 0 to 14 in increments of 2; the grid extends above 14. Point N is plotted at x = 18 and y = 12. Which ordered pair could represent the coordinates of point K? Choices: A: (6, 0) B: (2, 3) C: (1.5, 0) D: (7.5, 5)

text.en: A proportional line through N has constant ratio y/x = 12/18 = 2/3. For point (7.5, 5), 5/7.5 = 2/3, so that point can lie at K on the same line.
text.es: Una recta proporcional que pasa por N tiene la razón constante y/x = 12/18 = 2/3. Para el punto (7.5, 5), 5/7.5 = 2/3, así que ese punto puede ser K en la misma recta.

alt.en: Question 40. Which expression is equivalent to the expression −3(4x − 2) − 2x? Choices: A: −8x B: −16x C: −14x − 2 D: −14x + 6

text.en: Distribute −3 and then combine like terms: −3(4x − 2) − 2x = −12x + 6 − 2x = −14x + 6.
text.es: Distribuye −3 y luego combina términos semejantes: −3(4x − 2) − 2x = −12x + 6 − 2x = −14x + 6.

alt.en: Question 41. Maya uses blue and orange fabric to make identical wall decorations. A coordinate graph titled “FABRIC AMOUNTS” represents the relationship between the amounts of blue and orange fabric used. The horizontal x-axis is labeled “Orange Fabric (yards)” and is numbered 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, and 1.0. The vertical y-axis is labeled “Blue Fabric (yards)” and is numbered 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, and 0.5. A straight ray begins at the origin (0, 0), rises to the right through the grid intersection (0.7, 0.3), and continues with an arrow. No individual data points are marked on the ray. What is the constant of proportionality as represented in the graph? Choices: A: 3/10 B: 2/5 C: 3/7 D: 1/2

text.en: The graph passes through the origin and the point (0.7, 0.3). The constant of proportionality is blue/orange = y/x = 0.3/0.7 = 3/7.
text.es: La gráfica pasa por el origen y por el punto (0.7, 0.3). La constante de proporcionalidad es azul/naranja = y/x = 0.3/0.7 = 3/7.

alt.en: Question 42. Lance bought n notebooks that cost $0.75 each and p pens that cost $0.55 each. A 6.25% sales tax will be applied to the total cost. Which expression represents the total amount Lance paid, including tax? Choices: A: 0.0625(n + p) + 0.0625(0.75n + 0.55p) B: (0.75n + 0.55p) + 0.0625(0.75n + 0.55p) C: 0.75(0.0625n) + 0.55(0.0625n) D: 0.75(1.0625n) + 0.55(1.0625n)

text.en: The pretax subtotal is 0.75n + 0.55p. The tax is 6.25% of that subtotal, or 0.0625(0.75n + 0.55p), so adding subtotal and tax gives the expression in choice B.
text.es: El subtotal antes de impuestos es 0.75n + 0.55p. El impuesto es el 6.25% de ese subtotal, o 0.0625(0.75n + 0.55p), así que sumar subtotal e impuesto da la expresión de la opción B.

alt.en: Question 43. A recycling plant processes an average of 1/3 ton of glass each minute. At approximately what rate does the recycling plant process glass, in tons per day? (1 day = 24 hours) Choices: A: 20 B: 180 C: 480 D: 4,320

text.en: One day has 24 × 60 = 1,440 minutes. At 1/3 ton each minute, the plant processes (1/3)(1,440) = 480 tons per day.
text.es: Un día tiene 24 × 60 = 1,440 minutos. A una tasa de 1/3 de tonelada por minuto, la planta procesa (1/3)(1,440) = 480 toneladas por día.

alt.en: Question 44. When Keisha installed a fence along the 200-foot perimeter of her rectangular back yard, she left an opening for a gate. She used x to represent the length, in feet, of the gate. A rectangular diagram labeled “Back Yard” has a height of 30 ft. Along the bottom side, the gate opening at the left has length x, and the remaining segment from the right edge of the gate to the bottom-right corner has length 60 ft. Thus, the full horizontal side consists of the gate length x plus 60 ft. What is the value of x? Choices: A: 10 B: 20 C: 25 D: 30

text.en: The rectangle has height 30 and width x + 60. Its perimeter equation is 2(30) + 2(x + 60) = 200, so 60 + 2x + 120 = 200 and x = 10 feet.
text.es: El rectángulo tiene altura 30 y ancho x + 60. Su ecuación de perímetro es 2(30) + 2(x + 60) = 200, así que 60 + 2x + 120 = 200 y x = 10 pies.

alt.en: Question 45. Last year 950 people attended a town’s annual parade. This year 1,520 people attended. What was the percent increase in attendance from last year to this year? Choices: A: 37.5% B: 57.0% C: 60.0% D: 62.5%

text.en: Attendance increased by 1,520 − 950 = 570 people. Relative to last year, the increase is 570/950 = 0.60, and 0.60 × 100% = 60.0%.
text.es: La asistencia aumentó en 1,520 − 950 = 570 personas. En relación con el año pasado, el aumento es 570/950 = 0.60, y 0.60 × 100% = 60.0%.

title.en: New York Grade 8 Math — 2016 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 8 — preguntas publicadas de 2016

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2016 New York State Grade 8 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 8.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016.

sourceTitle.en: 2016 NYS Grade 8 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 8.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2016

alt.en: Question 1. Mr. Thomsen is buying two types of gift cards to give as prizes to employees at a company meeting. He will buy restaurant gift cards that each cost $50. He will also buy movie theater gift cards that each cost $20. He has $450 to buy a total of 15 gift cards. How many of each type of gift card can Mr. Thomsen buy? Choices: A: He can buy 5 restaurant gift cards and 10 movie theater gift cards. B: He can buy 8 restaurant gift cards and 7 movie theater gift cards. C: He can buy 10 restaurant gift cards and 5 movie theater gift cards. D: He can buy 12 restaurant gift cards and 3 movie theater gift cards.

text.en: Five restaurant cards and 10 movie cards make 5 + 10 = 15 cards, and their cost is 5($50) + 10($20) = $450.
text.es: Cinco tarjetas de restaurante y 10 de cine suman 5 + 10 = 15 tarjetas, y cuestan 5($50) + 10($20) = $450.

alt.en: Question 2. The relationship between temperature in degrees Fahrenheit and degrees Celsius is represented by a coordinate graph titled “TEMPERATURE CONVERSION.” The horizontal x-axis represents degrees Celsius and is labeled from 0 through 5 in increments of 1. The vertical y-axis represents degrees Fahrenheit and is labeled from 26 through 42 in increments of 2. An increasing straight line crosses the y-axis at (0, 32) and continues upward and to the right with an arrow, reaching approximately (5.5, 42). What is the meaning of the y-intercept? Choices: A: the change in degrees Fahrenheit for every change of one degree Celsius. B: the change in degrees Celsius for every change of one degree Fahrenheit. C: the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit when the temperature is zero degrees Celsius. D: the temperature in degrees Celsius when the temperature is zero degrees Fahrenheit.

text.en: The graph crosses the Fahrenheit axis at 32 when Celsius is 0. Thus the y-intercept represents 32°F at 0°C.
text.es: La gráfica cruza el eje Fahrenheit en 32 cuando Celsius es 0. Por eso, la intersección con y representa 32°F cuando hay 0°C.

alt.en: Question 3. Kevin moved from a city to a small town. The population of the city is 6 × 10⁵, which is about 15 times as great as the small town. Which expression could represent the approximate population of the small town? Choices: A: 4 × 10³. B: 4 × 10⁴. C: 9 × 10⁵. D: 9 × 10⁶.

text.en: Divide the city population by 15: (6 × 10^5) ÷ 15 = 0.4 × 10^5 = 4 × 10^4 people.
text.es: Se divide la población de la ciudad entre 15: (6 × 10^5) ÷ 15 = 0.4 × 10^5 = 4 × 10^4 personas.

alt.en: Question 4. Pentagon P and pentagon Q are congruent and are drawn on a coordinate plane. Both axes are marked in increments of 1. Pentagon P has vertices (1, 2), (1, 5), (2, 5), (2, 4), and (3, 2), connected in that order and then back to (1, 2). Pentagon Q has vertices (-3, -5), (-2, -3), (-2, -2), (-1, -2), and (-1, -5), connected in that order and then back to (-3, -5). Which sequence could be used to transform pentagon P to pentagon Q? Choices: A: a 180° clockwise rotation about the origin. B: a translation four units left and then a reflection over the x-axis. C: a reflection over the y-axis and then a translation seven units down. D: a translation seven units down and then a 90° clockwise rotation about the origin.

text.en: Reflecting over the y-axis sends (x, y) to (−x, y), and moving down 7 sends it to (−x, y − 7). For example, (1, 5) maps to (−1, −2) and (3, 2) maps to (−3, −5), matching Q.
text.es: Reflejar sobre el eje y transforma (x, y) en (−x, y), y bajar 7 unidades da (−x, y − 7). Por ejemplo, (1, 5) pasa a (−1, −2) y (3, 2) pasa a (−3, −5), que coinciden con Q.

alt.en: Question 5. The graph of a system of equations is drawn on a coordinate plane whose numbered tick marks increase by 2. One decreasing line crosses the y-axis at (0, 10) and the x-axis at (5, 0), then continues with arrows upward to the left and downward to the right. The other decreasing line passes through the origin (0, 0) and approximately through (-12, 6) and (12, -6), continuing with arrows in both directions. The two lines intersect at approximately (6.7, -3.3). What system of equations represents the graph? Choices: A: y = -2x + 10 and y = -(1/3)x. B: y = -2x + 10 and y = -(1/2)x. C: y = -(1/2)x + 10 and y = -2x. D: y = -(1/3)x + 10 and y = -2x.

text.en: The steep line has y-intercept 10 and slope −2, so it is y = −2x + 10. The other line passes through the origin with slope −1/2, so it is y = −(1/2)x.
text.es: La recta inclinada tiene intersección 10 y pendiente −2, así que y = −2x + 10. La otra pasa por el origen con pendiente −1/2, así que y = −(1/2)x.

alt.en: Question 6. A cylinder and a cone have congruent heights and radii. What is the ratio of the volume of the cone to the volume of the cylinder? Choices: A: 1:1. B: 1:3. C: 1:6. D: 1:9.

text.en: With the same radius and height, V(cone) = (1/3)πr²h while V(cylinder) = πr²h. Dividing the volumes gives a ratio of 1/3 to 1, or 1:3.
text.es: Con el mismo radio y altura, V(cono) = (1/3)πr²h y V(cilindro) = πr²h. Al dividir los volúmenes, la razón es 1/3 a 1, es decir, 1:3.

alt.en: Question 7. Which of the equations listed below are linear equations? Equation I: C = 2πr. Equation II: A = πr². Equation III: V = (4/3)πr³. Choices: A: equation I only. B: equation II only. C: equations I and III. D: equations II and III.

text.en: In C = 2πr, the variable r has exponent 1, so the relationship is linear. The formulas A = πr² and V = (4/3)πr³ contain higher powers and are nonlinear.
text.es: En C = 2πr, la variable r tiene exponente 1, así que la relación es lineal. A = πr² y V = (4/3)πr³ tienen potencias mayores y no son lineales.

alt.en: Question 8. The scatter plot titled “POINTS SCORED AND ALLOWED” shows the points scored and the points allowed by the Bulldogs football team for several games. The horizontal x-axis represents Points Scored and runs from 0 to 30, with numbered labels every 2 points. The vertical y-axis represents Points Allowed and runs from 0 to 30, with numbered labels every 2 points. The plotted points are (2, 14), (7, 21), (10, 17), (13, 20), (14, 10), (16, 4), (17, 13), (20, 24), (21, 17), (24, 28), (24, 10), (27, 3), and (28, 24). Which association (correlation) best describes the data? Choices: A: no association (correlation). B: positive association (correlation). C: negative association (correlation). D: nonlinear association (correlation).

text.en: As points scored increase, the points allowed are sometimes high and sometimes low. The scattered points show no consistent upward, downward, or curved pattern.
text.es: Cuando aumentan los puntos anotados, los puntos permitidos a veces son altos y a veces bajos. Por lo tanto, no muestran un patrón ascendente, descendente ni curvo.

alt.en: Question 9. The graph below shows the relationship between the distances run and the time for three people in a 100-yard race. The graph is titled “RUNNERS IN 100-YARD RACE.” The horizontal x-axis is labeled “Time (seconds),” and the vertical y-axis is labeled “Distance (yards).” Three rays begin at the origin. Bella’s ray is the steepest, Kofi’s ray has a middle slope, and Elsie’s ray is the least steep. The relationship between the distance run and the time for Kofi can be represented by the equation y = 15.55x, where he ran y yards in x seconds. Which two equations could be used to represent this relationship for Bella and Elsie? Choices: A: Bella: y = 15.15x; Elsie: y = 15.85x. B: Bella: y = 15.85x; Elsie: y = 15.65x. C: Bella: y = 15.45x; Elsie: y = 15.15x. D: Bella: y = 15.85x; Elsie: y = 15.15x.

text.en: Because Bella's line is steeper than Kofi's rate of 15.55 yards per second, her rate must be larger, while Elsie's must be smaller. The matching rates are 15.85 for Bella and 15.15 for Elsie.
text.es: La tasa de Bella debe ser mayor que 15.55 porque su recta es más inclinada, y la de Elsie debe ser menor porque su recta es menos inclinada. Las tasas son 15.85 para Bella y 15.15 para Elsie.

alt.en: Question 10. Which table of values represents a linear function? Choices: A: A table with columns x and y and rows (0, 0), (1, 1), (4, 16), and (9, 81). B: A table with columns x and y and rows (0, 1), (1, 3), (4, 9), and (9, 20). C: A table with columns x and y and rows (0, 0), (1, 2), (4, 8), and (9, 18). D: A table with columns x and y and rows (0, 0), (1, 2), (4, 4), and (9, 6).

text.en: Every pair in table C satisfies y = 2x: 0 = 2(0), 2 = 2(1), 8 = 2(4), and 18 = 2(9). The constant rate makes the function linear.
text.es: Cada par de la tabla C satisface y = 2x: 0 = 2(0), 2 = 2(1), 8 = 2(4) y 18 = 2(9). La tasa constante hace que la función sea lineal.

alt.en: Question 11. Simplify 5⁻⁸ × 5⁴. Choices: A: 1/5⁴. B: 1/5³². C: −5². D: −5¹².

text.en: Multiplying powers with base 5 means adding exponents: 5^(−8) × 5^4 = 5^(−4). A negative exponent gives 5^(−4) = 1/5^4.
text.es: Al multiplicar potencias de base 5 se suman los exponentes: 5^(−8) × 5^4 = 5^(−4). El exponente negativo da 5^(−4) = 1/5^4.

alt.en: Question 12. What is the value of t that satisfies the equation below? 3(t + 4) − 2(2t + 3) = −4. Choices: A: −11/3. B: −4/5. C: 10. D: 11.

text.en: Distribute and combine terms: 3t + 12 − 4t − 6 = −4, so −t + 6 = −4. Subtracting 6 gives −t = −10, hence t = 10.
text.es: Al distribuir y combinar términos: 3t + 12 − 4t − 6 = −4, entonces −t + 6 = −4. Al restar 6 queda −t = −10, por lo tanto t = 10.

alt.en: Question 19. Ellentown College has approximately 3 × 10³ students, and Pengrove University has approximately 30,000 students. How many times as much is the number of students at Pengrove University as the number of students at Ellentown College? Choices: A: 1. B: 10. C: 30. D: 100.

text.en: Ellentown has 3 × 10^3 = 3,000 students. Dividing Pengrove's 30,000 by 3,000 gives 30,000 ÷ 3,000 = 10 times as many.
text.es: Ellentown tiene 3 × 10^3 = 3,000 estudiantes. Al dividir 30,000 entre 3,000 se obtiene 30,000 ÷ 3,000 = 10 veces esa cantidad.

alt.en: Question 20. A series of transformations on quadrilateral S resulted in quadrilateral T. The angle measures of quadrilateral T are congruent to those of quadrilateral S. The side lengths of quadrilateral T are twice as long as those of quadrilateral S. Which transformation on quadrilateral S must be included to result in quadrilateral T? Choices: A: dilation. B: rotation. C: reflection. D: translation.

text.en: Rotations, reflections, and translations preserve side lengths. Doubling every side while preserving angles requires a dilation with scale factor 2.
text.es: Las rotaciones, reflexiones y traslaciones conservan las longitudes. Duplicar cada lado (×2) y conservar los ángulos requiere una dilatación de factor 2.

alt.en: Question 21. Function 1 is represented by the equation y = −4/5x − 2, and function 2 is represented by a coordinate graph. The graph, titled “FUNCTION 2,” has an x-axis and a y-axis with a one-unit square grid. Function 2 is a downward-opening parabola symmetric about the y-axis. Its vertex is at (0, −2), its greatest output value. The curve passes through approximately (−1, −3), (1, −3), (−2, −6), and (2, −6), and both branches continue downward, as indicated by arrows near y = −9. For which of the functions are all the output values less than −1? Choices: A: both functions. B: only function 1. C: only function 2. D: neither function.

text.en: Function 1 can produce values above −1; for example, x = −5 gives y = 2. Function 2 is a downward-opening parabola whose maximum is y = −2, so only function 2 always stays below −1.
text.es: La función 1 puede dar valores mayores que −1; por ejemplo, x = −5 da y = 2. La función 2 es una parábola hacia abajo cuyo máximo es y = −2, así que solo ella permanece siempre debajo de −1.

alt.en: Question 22. Rectangle FGHJ is translated 6 units right and 1 unit up to produce rectangle F′G′H′J′. On the coordinate plane, the original rectangle has vertices F(−5, 2), G(−2, 2), H(−2, −3), and J(−5, −3). Its horizontal side FG has length 3 units, and its vertical side GH has length 5 units. Which statement about the side lengths of rectangle F′G′H′J′ is true? Choices: A: F′G′ = 3 and G′H′ = 5. B: F′G′ = 3 and G′H′ = 6. C: F′G′ = 9 and G′H′ = 5. D: F′G′ = 9 and G′H′ = 6.

text.en: The original rectangle has FG = 3 units and GH = 5 units. A translation preserves lengths, so F'G' = 3 and G'H' = 5.
text.es: El rectángulo original tiene FG = 3 unidades y GH = 5 unidades. Una traslación conserva las longitudes, por lo que F'G' = 3 y G'H' = 5.

alt.en: Question 27. Solve the system of equations below: 2x + 4y = 10 and 2x + 4y = −10. Choices: A: x = 3, y = 1. B: x = 6, y = −4. C: No solution. D: Infinitely many solutions.

text.en: The equations have the same left side but different constants. Subtracting them gives 0 = 20, a contradiction, so no ordered pair satisfies both equations.
text.es: Las ecuaciones tienen el mismo lado izquierdo pero constantes distintas. Al restarlas se obtiene 0 = 20, una contradicción, así que ningún par satisface ambas.

alt.en: Question 28. Mia enlarged a plan for an outdoor stage. The original plan is a rectangle on a coordinate plane with vertices (−6, 4), (8, 4), (8, −2), and (−6, −2). Its sides are horizontal along y = 4 and y = −2 and vertical along x = −6 and x = 8. She dilated the outdoor stage by a scale factor of four with the center of dilation at the origin. Which ordered pair will be the coordinates of one of the new vertices? Choices: A: (2, 1). B: (8, 16). C: (32, 4). D: (32, 16).

text.en: A dilation centered at the origin with factor 4 multiplies both coordinates by 4. The vertex (8, 4) maps to (4·8, 4·4) = (32, 16).
text.es: Una dilatación con centro en el origen y factor 4 multiplica ambas coordenadas por 4. El vértice (8, 4) pasa a (4·8, 4·4) = (32, 16).

alt.en: Question 29. Bianca and Nick are both musicians who sell their songs online. During the same year, Bianca sold 8 × 10⁵ downloads of her songs and Nick sold 4 × 10⁶ downloads of his songs. How many times as much is the number of songs that Nick sold than the number of songs that Bianca sold? Choices: A: 2. B: 5. C: 20. D: 40.

text.en: Compare the downloads by division: (4 × 10^6) ÷ (8 × 10^5) = (4/8) × 10 = 0.5 × 10 = 5.
text.es: Se comparan las descargas mediante división: (4 × 10^6) ÷ (8 × 10^5) = (4/8) × 10 = 0.5 × 10 = 5.

alt.en: Question 30. Which table represents a relation that is not a function? Choices: A: A two-column Input–Output table contains the rows (1, 1), (2, 1), (3, 1), and (4, 1). B: A two-column Input–Output table contains the rows (2, 0), (4, 1), (6, 2), and (8, 0). C: A two-column Input–Output table contains the rows (−1, −7), (−2, 11), (−3, 13), and (−4, 105). D: A two-column Input–Output table contains the rows (3, 0), (5, 2), (7, 1), and (3, −4).

text.en: In table D, the input 3 is paired with output 0 and also with output −4. One input having two outputs violates the definition of a function.
text.es: En la tabla D, la entrada 3 se relaciona con la salida 0 y también con −4. Una entrada con dos salidas no representa una función.

alt.en: Question 34. The Ecology Club was planning to take a field trip either to the seacoast or the mountains. The club president surveyed all of the members to determine the preferred trip. The results are displayed in a table titled “FIELD TRIP SURVEY.” The columns are Students, Seacoast, Mountains, and Total. The Seventh-Grade row contains 42, 28, and 70, respectively. The Eighth-Grade row contains 30, 50, and 80, respectively. The Total row contains 72, 78, and 150, respectively. Which statement is true about the results of the survey? Choices: A: 20% of eighth-grade students preferred the seacoast. B: 32% of seventh-grade students preferred the mountains. C: 40% of students preferred the mountains. D: 48% of students preferred the seacoast.

text.en: Overall, 72 of the 150 students preferred the seacoast. The proportion is 72 ÷ 150 = 0.48, which is 48%.
text.es: En total, 72 de los 150 estudiantes prefirieron la costa. La proporción es 72 ÷ 150 = 0.48, que equivale al 48%.

alt.en: Question 35. A cylinder has a radius of 3 inches and a height of 4 3/4 inches. A sphere has a radius of 3 inches. What is the difference between the volumes, to the nearest tenth of a cubic inch, of the cylinder and the sphere? Choices: A: 21.2. B: 51.8. C: 68.3. D: 96.6.

text.en: The cylinder volume is π(3²)(4.75) = 42.75π, and the sphere volume is (4/3)π(3³) = 36π. Their difference is 6.75π ≈ 21.2 cubic inches.
text.es: El cilindro tiene volumen π(3²)(4.75) = 42.75π y la esfera (4/3)π(3³) = 36π. La diferencia es 6.75π ≈ 21.2 pulgadas cúbicas.

alt.en: Question 36. Two friends work at different companies, P and S. Both companies use the number of hours that an employee works to calculate that employee’s vacation hours. The relationship between the number of hours worked and the number of vacation hours for employees at each company is shown in a table and graph, respectively. The COMPANY P table has columns Hours Worked and Vacation Hours and contains the rows (10, 0.4), (20, 0.8), (30, 1.2), (40, 1.6), and (50, 2.0). The COMPANY S graph has Hours Worked on the x-axis and Vacation Hours on the y-axis. The labeled x-axis values are 0, 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, and 120, with grid lines continuing to 140; the labeled y-axis values are 1 through 9, with grid lines continuing to 10. A straight ray begins at the origin, rises to the right, passes through approximately (80, 5), and continues with an arrow to approximately (140, 8.3). Which statement describes the difference in each friend’s vacation hours if both work 2,080 hours? Choices: A: The friend at company S will have about 42 more vacation hours than the friend at company P. B: The friend at company S will have about 46 more vacation hours than the friend at company P. C: The friend at company P will have about eight more vacation hours than the friend at company S. D: The friend at company P will have about nine more vacation hours than the friend at company S.

text.en: Company P earns 0.4 ÷ 10 = 0.04 vacation hour per work hour, while the graph for S shows 0.06. At 2,080 hours, the difference is (0.06 − 0.04)(2,080) = 41.6, about 42 hours in favor of S.
text.es: La compañía P da 0.4 ÷ 10 = 0.04 hora de vacaciones por hora trabajada, y S da 0.06. Con 2,080 horas, la diferencia es (0.06 − 0.04)(2,080) = 41.6, unas 42 horas más en S.

alt.en: Question 37. Which equation represents a nonlinear function? Choices: A: y = −3x + 1. B: y = x² + 1. C: y = x/2 + 1. D: y = 2x + 1/2.

text.en: The equation y = x² + 1 contains an x² term, so its rate of change is not constant and its graph is a parabola. The other equations have x only to the first power.
text.es: La ecuación y = x² + 1 contiene x², por lo que su tasa de cambio no es constante y su gráfica es una parábola. Las demás tienen x solo a la primera potencia.

alt.en: Question 38. What is the value of the expression below? (4.8 × 10⁸)/(1.2 × 10⁴) × (2.2 × 10⁻⁶). Choices: A: 0.88. B: 0.088. C: 0.0088. D: 0.00088.

Question 39. A crane is lowering a concrete block from a height of 270 feet above the ground at a constant rate of 2.5 feet per second. Which function can be used to determine h, the height, in feet, above the ground of the concrete block after s seconds? Choices: A: h = 270s + 2.5. B: h = 2.5s + 270. C: h = 270 − 2.5s. D: h = 2.5s − 270.

text.en: The block begins at 270 feet and loses 2.5 feet of height each second. After s seconds its height is h = 270 − 2.5s.
text.es: El bloque empieza a 270 pies y pierde 2.5 pies de altura cada segundo. Después de s segundos, su altura es h = 270 − 2.5s.

alt.en: Question 40. Function P is a linear function with a y-intercept of 5. Function Q is defined by the equation y = −(1/3)x + 4. Which statement must be true about functions P and Q? Choices: A: Both functions have the same slope. B: Both functions have a negative slope. C: The functions will have the same input when y = 0. D: The functions will have different outputs when x = 0.

text.en: At x = 0, a function's output is its y-intercept. Function P outputs 5, while Q outputs −(1/3)(0) + 4 = 4, so their outputs differ.
text.es: Cuando x = 0, la salida es la intersección con y. La función P da 5 y Q da −(1/3)(0) + 4 = 4, así que sus salidas son distintas.

alt.en: Question 41. Line k is the line of best fit for a set of data on a scatter plot. The data show a strong linear association. Which scatter plot best represents these data and line k? Each choice uses an x-y coordinate grid marked in increments of 1, with numbered values shown at even integers from 0 through 10. Choices: A: A scatter plot with an overall positive association but points spread noticeably around the trend. The visible points are approximately (1,4), (1,1), (2,4), (2,1), (3,5), (3,2), (3,3), (4,7), (4,3), (5,7), (5,5), (6,5), (6,9), (7,7), (7,10), (8,7), (8,8), and (9,10). Line k rises from about (0,6) toward (7,12), lying above most of the data rather than through their center. B: A scatter plot with a strong negative association. The visible points descend from the upper left toward the lower right, including points near (1,10), (1,7), (2,10), (2,7), (3,9), (3,6), (4,9), (4,4), (5,8), (5,4), (6,6), (6,4), (7,6), (7,5), (8,4), and (9,4). Line k descends from about (0,10) toward the lower right and lies above much of the lower-right data. C: A scatter plot with a strong positive association. The visible points rise from the lower left toward the upper right, including points near (0,2), (1,1), (1,3), (2,4), (2,2), (3,5), (3,3), (4,5), (5,7), (5,4), (6,8), (6,5), (7,9), (7,7), (8,8), (8,10), and (9,8). Line k rises from about (2,0) to about (11,10), lying below most of the data rather than through their center. D: A scatter plot with a strong negative association. The visible points cluster closely around a descending line from the upper left toward the lower right, including points near (0,10), (0,8), (1,10), (1,9), (2,7), (2,9), (3,8), (3,6), (4,7), (4,5), (5,5), (5,4), and (6,5). Line k descends through the center of the cluster from about (0,10) toward the lower right.

text.en: A strong linear association places the points close to the line of best fit. Plot D qualifies because its points cluster around the downward-sloping line k, indicating a strong negative linear association.
text.es: Una asociación lineal fuerte coloca los puntos cerca de la recta de ajuste. En la gráfica D, los puntos se agrupan alrededor de la recta descendente k, por lo que la asociación es fuerte y negativa.

alt.en: Question 47. In the coordinate-plane diagram, lines MN and JK are parallel and are intersected by line QT. The axes are marked in units of 1. Line MN is horizontal at y = 2 and contains M(−5,2), R(−2,2), and N(5,2). Line JK is horizontal at y = −2 and contains J(−5,−2), S(2,−2), and K(5,−2). Transversal QT descends from upper left to lower right, passes through Q(−5,5), R(−2,2), the origin (0,0), S(2,−2), and T(5,−5), and continues with arrows in both directions. Which transformation could be used to show that ∠MRS is congruent to ∠JST? Choices: A: reflect ∠MRS over the x-axis. B: rotate ∠MRS about the origin. C: translate ∠MRS down and to the right. D: dilate ∠MRS by a scale factor of two with the center at point R.

text.en: Translating by 4 units right and 4 units down maps vertex R(−2, 2) to S(2, −2). The horizontal ray stays on the parallel line and the diagonal ray stays on transversal QT, so the angles coincide.
text.es: Trasladar 4 unidades a la derecha y 4 hacia abajo lleva R(−2, 2) a S(2, −2). El rayo horizontal queda en la paralela y el diagonal en la transversal QT, así que los ángulos coinciden.

alt.en: Question 48. What is the equation of the line that passes through points (−3, 0.5) and (3, −0.5)? Choices: A: y = −(1/6)x. B: y = −6x. C: y = −(1/6)x + 1. D: y = −6x − 17.5.

text.en: The slope is (−0.5 − 0.5)/(3 − (−3)) = −1/6. The midpoint of the two points is (0, 0), so the y-intercept is 0 and the equation is y = −(1/6)x.
text.es: La pendiente es (−0.5 − 0.5)/(3 − (−3)) = −1/6. El punto medio es (0, 0), así que la intersección es 0 y la ecuación es y = −(1/6)x.

alt.en: Question 49. Function J is graphed on a coordinate grid whose x- and y-axes are marked in units of 1 from −5 through 5. The graph is a straight line with arrows at both ends, rising steeply from lower left to upper right. It crosses the y-axis at (0,−1) and passes through (1,2) and (2,5). If the y-intercept of Function R is 3/2 greater than the y-intercept of Function J, which equation could represent Function R? Choices: A: y = −x + 4.5. B: y = 0.5x + 3. C: y = 3x + 0.5. D: y = 4.5x − 1.

text.en: The graph of J crosses the y-axis at −1. Adding 3/2 gives −1 + 3/2 = 1/2 = 0.5, so function R must have y-intercept 0.5; y = 3x + 0.5 does.
text.es: La gráfica de J cruza el eje y en −1. Al sumar 3/2 se obtiene −1 + 3/2 = 1/2 = 0.5, así que R debe tener intersección 0.5; y = 3x + 0.5 la tiene.

title.en: New York Grade 3 Math — 2015 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 3 — preguntas publicadas de 2015

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2015 New York State Grade 3 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 3.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015.

sourceTitle.en: 2015 NYS Grade 3 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 3.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015

alt.en: Question 1. A model shows 2 rows of 10 circles. Which expression could be used to find the total number of circles? Choices: A: 2 + 20; B: 2 × 20; C: 2 + 10; D: 2 × 10.

text.en: There are 2 rows with 10 circles in each row, so 2 × 10 = 20 circles. The matching expression is 2 × 10.
text.es: Hay 2 filas con 10 círculos en cada fila, así que 2 × 10 = 20 círculos. La expresión correspondiente es 2 × 10.

alt.en: Question 2. Amar and his friends made a flag for their clubhouse. They divided the flag into 8 equal vertical sections and colored 3 of the sections gray. What fraction of the flag is gray? Choices: A: 1/3; B: 3/8; C: 3/5; D: 5/8.

text.en: The flag has 8 equal sections, and 3 of those sections are gray. Therefore, the gray part represents 3/8 of the flag.
text.es: La bandera tiene 8 secciones iguales y 3 de ellas están sombreadas de gris. Por lo tanto, la parte gris representa 3/8 de la bandera.

alt.en: Question 3. Alexis read 63 pages of a book in seven days. She read an equal number of pages each day. The equation 63 ÷ 7 = □ can be used to find the total number of pages she read each day. What is the total number of pages Alexis read each day? Choices: A: 8; B: 9; C: 56; D: 70.

text.en: Divide the 63 pages equally among 7 days: 63 ÷ 7 = 9. Alexis read 9 pages each day.
text.es: Se dividen las 63 páginas en partes iguales entre 7 días: 63 ÷ 7 = 9. Alexis leyó 9 páginas cada día.

alt.en: Question 4. Selena had 204 stamps in her collection. She bought 47 more stamps. If she gave 38 stamps to her brother, how many stamps does Selena have now? Choices: A: 119; B: 195; C: 213; D: 289.

text.en: First add the 47 new stamps: 204 + 47 = 251. Then subtract the 38 stamps Selena gave away: 251 − 38 = 213.
text.es: Primero se suman las 47 estampillas nuevas: 204 + 47 = 251. Luego se restan las 38 que Selena regaló: 251 − 38 = 213.

alt.en: Question 5. A tally chart titled Our Favorite Seasons shows: Spring, 4 students; Summer, 6 students; Fall, 2 students; Winter, 8 students. Which picture graph correctly shows the data? In each choice, one balloon symbol represents 2 students. Choices: A: Spring 4 symbols, Summer 6, Fall 2, Winter 8; B: Spring 2 symbols, Summer 3, Fall 2, Winter 4; C: Spring 3 symbols, Summer 3, Fall 1, Winter 4; D: Spring 2 symbols, Summer 3, Fall 1, Winter 4.

text.en: The tallies give 4, 6, 2, and 8 students for spring through winter. Since each picture represents 2 students, the graph needs 2, 3, 1, and 4 pictures, which is graph D.
text.es: Las marcas indican 4, 6, 2 y 8 estudiantes de primavera a invierno. Como cada dibujo representa 2 estudiantes, se necesitan 2, 3, 1 y 4 dibujos, que corresponden a la gráfica D.

alt.en: Question 6. What is 637 rounded to the nearest ten? Choices: A: 600; B: 630; C: 640; D: 647.

text.en: The ones digit in 637 is 7, so the tens digit rounds up. Thus, 637 rounded to the nearest ten is 640.
text.es: El dígito de las unidades de 637 es 7, así que se aumenta la decena. Por lo tanto, 637 redondeado a la decena más cercana es 640.

alt.en: Question 7. Nadia was asked to shade parts of a figure so that 5/6 of it was shaded. The figure is divided into 6 equal vertical parts, and 1 part is already shaded. What should Nadia do to complete the math problem? Choices: A: Nadia should shade two more parts of the figure; B: Nadia should shade three more parts of the figure; C: Nadia should shade four more parts of the figure; D: Nadia should shade five more parts of the figure.

text.en: One of the 6 equal parts is already shaded, and the target is 5 shaded parts. Nadia must shade 5 − 1 = 4 more parts.
text.es: Ya está sombreada 1 de las 6 partes iguales y la meta es tener 5 partes sombreadas. Nadia debe sombrear 5 − 1 = 4 partes más.

alt.en: Question 8. What is the area, in square units, of the shaded shape on the grid? The shaded shape contains a rectangle 7 unit squares wide and 3 unit squares high, plus 2 additional shaded unit squares centered directly below it. The key says each small square equals 1 square unit. Choices: A: 22; B: 23; C: 28; D: 72.

text.en: The shaded shape contains three rows of 7 unit squares plus 2 more squares below them. Its area is 3 × 7 + 2 = 23 square units.
text.es: La figura sombreada contiene tres filas de 7 cuadrados unitarios y 2 cuadrados más debajo. Su área es 3 × 7 + 2 = 23 unidades cuadradas.

alt.en: Question 9. A balance scale shows that one box is heavier than 5 identical cans: the pan holding the box is lower than the pan holding the cans. The box has a mass of 40 kilograms. What could be the mass, in kilograms, of 1 can? Choices: A: 40; B: 10; C: 8; D: 6.

text.en: Because the 40-kilogram box is heavier than 5 identical cans, each can must weigh less than 40 ÷ 5 = 8 kilograms. A mass of 6 works since 5 × 6 = 30 < 40.
text.es: Como la caja de 40 kilogramos pesa más que 5 latas idénticas, cada lata debe pesar menos de 40 ÷ 5 = 8 kilogramos. Una masa de 6 sirve porque 5 × 6 = 30 < 40.

alt.en: Question 10. A picture graph titled Students’ Favorite Hobbies shows 2 symbols for Drawing, 3 for Reading, and 4 for Sports. The key says each symbol represents 2 students. Which table represents the same data as the picture graph? Choices: A: Drawing 4, Reading 6, Sports 8; B: Drawing 8, Reading 6, Sports 4; C: Drawing 2, Reading 3, Sports 4; D: Drawing 4, Reading 5, Sports 6.

text.en: The graph shows 2 drawing symbols, 3 reading symbols, and 4 sports symbols. Multiplying each by 2 students gives 4, 6, and 8, matching table A.
text.es: La gráfica muestra 2 símbolos para dibujo, 3 para lectura y 4 para deportes. Al multiplicar cada cantidad por 2 estudiantes se obtienen 4, 6 y 8, que coinciden con la tabla A.

alt.en: Question 11. Which expression has the same value as (8 ×5) + (8 × 3)? Choices: A: 8 × 8; B: 8 ×15; C: 16 + 8; D: 13 + 11.

text.en: Factor out the common 8: (8 × 5) + (8 × 3) = 8 × (5 + 3) = 8 × 8. Therefore, 8 × 8 has the same value.
text.es: Se saca el factor común 8: (8 × 5) + (8 × 3) = 8 × (5 + 3) = 8 × 8. Por lo tanto, 8 × 8 tiene el mismo valor.

alt.en: Question 12. Alex sorted 20 toy cars into 4 groups with the same number of cars in each group. Which expression represents the number of toy cars in each group? Choices: A: 20 × 4; B: 20 + 4; C: 20 ÷ 4; D: 20 − 4.

text.en: Sorting 20 cars into 4 equal groups requires division. The expression 20 ÷ 4 gives 5 cars in each group.
text.es: Repartir 20 autos en 4 grupos iguales requiere una división. La expresión 20 ÷ 4 da 5 autos en cada grupo.

alt.en: Question 13. A certain dance class has 42 dancers. The teacher wants to place the class into six equal groups. Which number sentence could be used to find the number of dancers that will be in each group? Choices: A: 6 × □ = 42; B: 6 ÷ □ = 42; C: 42 + 6 = □; D: 42 − 6 = □.

text.en: Six equal groups with the same unknown number of dancers must total 42, so 6 × ? = 42. Solving gives ? = 7 dancers per group.
text.es: Seis grupos iguales con la misma cantidad desconocida de bailarines deben sumar 42, así que 6 × ? = 42. Al resolver, ? = 7 bailarines por grupo.

alt.en: Question 14. Noel read 90 minutes each day for 6 days. Tyra read 60 minutes each day for 8 days. What is the difference, in minutes, between the total amount of time Noel read and the total amount of time Tyra read? Choices: A: 30; B: 40; C: 60; D: 80.

text.en: Noel read 90 × 6 = 540 minutes, while Tyra read 60 × 8 = 480 minutes. The difference is 540 − 480 = 60 minutes.
text.es: Noel leyó 90 × 6 = 540 minutos, mientras que Tyra leyó 60 × 8 = 480 minutos. La diferencia es 540 − 480 = 60 minutos.

alt.en: Question 15. Mr. Bachu bought 24 pounds of potting soil. Which sentence could describe the potting soil Mr. Bachu bought? Choices: A: He bought 6 bags that weigh 4 pounds each; B: He bought 5 bags that weigh 4 pounds each; C: He bought 4 bags that weigh 20 pounds each; D: He bought 10 bags that weigh 14 pounds each.

text.en: Six bags weighing 4 pounds each have a total weight of 6 × 4 = 24 pounds. The other bag-and-weight combinations do not equal 24.
text.es: Seis bolsas de 4 libras cada una pesan en total 6 × 4 = 24 libras. Las otras combinaciones de bolsas y pesos no suman 24.

alt.en: Question 16. Pedro left home this morning at the time shown on an analog clock. The minute hand points to 7, and the hour hand is between 8 and 9, showing 8:35. Tina left home 20 minutes after Pedro left. Carlos left home 18 minutes after Tina left. At what time did Carlos leave home this morning? Choices: A: 7:57 a.m.; B: 8:13 a.m.; C: 8:38 a.m.; D: 9:13 a.m.

text.en: The clock shows 8:35 a.m. Adding Tina's 20 minutes gives 8:55, and adding Carlos's 18 more minutes gives 9:13 a.m.
text.es: El reloj marca las 8:35 a. m. Al sumar los 20 minutos de Tina se llega a las 8:55, y al sumar 18 minutos más se llega a las 9:13 a. m.

alt.en: Question 17. Mrs. Allen made 8 pitchers of fruit punch for a party. She used 2 liters of water to make each pitcher of fruit punch. How many liters of water did Mrs. Allen use in all? Choices: A: 4; B: 6; C: 10; D: 16.

text.en: There are 8 pitchers and each uses 2 liters of water. Multiplying 8 × 2 = 16 shows that Mrs. Allen used 16 liters in all.
text.es: Hay 8 jarras y cada una usa 2 litros de agua. Al multiplicar 8 × 2 = 16, se obtiene que la señora Allen usó 16 litros en total.

alt.en: Question 18. The table below shows the number of tickets for the school play that were sold each day. SCHOOL PLAY TICKETS Number of Day Tickets Sold Thursday 238 Friday 361 Saturday 249 Sunday 328 On which day does the number of tickets sold round to 300, when rounded to the nearest hundred? Choices: A: Thursday; B: Friday; C: Saturday; D: Sunday.

text.en: Sunday's 328 tickets round to 300 because 328 is closer to 300 than to 400. The other counts round to either 200 or 400.
text.es: Las 328 entradas del domingo se redondean a 300 porque 328 está más cerca de 300 que de 400. Las otras cantidades se redondean a 200 o a 400.

alt.en: Question 19. Paul’s rectangular patio is shown as a grid 8 unit squares wide and 10 unit squares high. The top 7 rows are gray stones, and the bottom 3 rows are white stones. The key says each small square equals 1 square foot. Which equation can be used to find the total area, in square feet, of Paul’s patio? Choices: A: (8 × 7) + (8 × 3) = □; B: (8 + 7) × (8 + 3) = □; C: (10 × 7) + (10 × 3) = □; D: (10 + 7) × (10 + 3) = □.

text.en: The patio is 8 stones wide, with 7 rows of gray stones and 3 rows of white stones. Adding the two rectangular areas gives (8 × 7) + (8 × 3) = 80 square feet.
text.es: El patio tiene 8 piedras de ancho, con 7 filas de piedras grises y 3 filas de piedras blancas. Al sumar las dos áreas rectangulares se obtiene (8 × 7) + (8 × 3) = 80 pies cuadrados.

alt.en: Question 20. A number line from 0 to 1 is divided into 3 equal intervals, and point R is at the second tick mark after 0. Which fraction represents the location of point R? Choices: A: 1/3; B: 2/4; C: 2/3; D: 3/4.

text.en: The interval from 0 to 1 is divided into 3 equal parts. Point R is at the second division, so its location is 2/3.
text.es: El intervalo de 0 a 1 está dividido en 3 partes iguales. El punto R está en la segunda división, así que su ubicación es 2/3.

alt.en: Question 21. The school store sells pencils in packages of 4. Zoe bought enough packages to have 28 pencils. The equation 4 × □ = 28 can be used to determine the number of packages Zoe bought. What is the total number of packages that Zoe bought? Choices: A: 6; B: 7; C: 24; D: 32.

text.en: Each package contains 4 pencils, so the number of packages is 28 ÷ 4 = 7. Indeed, 4 × 7 = 28 pencils.
text.es: Cada paquete contiene 4 lápices, así que la cantidad de paquetes es 28 ÷ 4 = 7. En efecto, 4 × 7 = 28 lápices.

alt.en: Question 22. Rick has 15 muffins. He will give each of his three children one muffin each day. The equation 15 ÷ □ = 3 can be used to find the total number of days he can give the muffins to his children before they are gone. What is the total number of days Rick can give the muffins to his children? Choices: A: 4; B: 5; C: 12; D: 18.

text.en: Rick gives out 3 muffins each day, one to each child. Dividing 15 ÷ 3 = 5 shows that the muffins last 5 days.
text.es: Rick reparte 3 panecillos cada día, uno a cada hijo. Al dividir 15 ÷ 3 = 5, se obtiene que los panecillos duran 5 días.

alt.en: Question 23. Which number sentence could also be used to find the missing number in the equation 10 × □ = 60? Choices: A: 60 × 10 = □; B: 60 − 10 = □; C: 60 + 10 = □; D: 60 ÷ 10 = □.

text.en: Division undoes multiplication, so the missing factor in 10 × ? = 60 is found by 60 ÷ 10. This gives ? = 6.
text.es: La división deshace la multiplicación, así que el factor que falta en 10 × ? = 60 se obtiene con 60 ÷ 10. Esto da ? = 6.

alt.en: Question 24. Which number sentence is true? Choices: A: 2/2 = 2/6; B: 4/6 = 2/2; C: 1/2 = 3/6; D: 2/6 = 1/2.

text.en: Multiplying the numerator and denominator of 1/2 by 3 gives 3/6. Therefore, 1/2 = 3/6 is the true number sentence.
text.es: Al multiplicar por 3 el numerador y el denominador de 1/2 se obtiene 3/6. Por lo tanto, 1/2 = 3/6 es la igualdad verdadera.

title.en: New York Grade 4 Math — 2015 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 4 — preguntas publicadas de 2015

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2015 New York State Grade 4 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 4.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015.

sourceTitle.en: 2015 NYS Grade 4 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 4.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015

text.en: Compare 376,425 and 367,419 from left to right. The hundred-thousands digits match, but 7 ten-thousands is greater than 6 ten-thousands, so 376,425 > 367,419.
text.es: Se comparan 376,425 y 367,419 de izquierda a derecha. Las centenas de millar son iguales, pero 7 decenas de millar es mayor que 6 decenas de millar, así que 376,425 > 367,419.

alt.en: Question 2. Taylor is measuring leaves for an art project. A leaf is shown above a horizontal measurement line whose endpoints align with the leaf’s left and right ends; the line measures 3 1/2 inches. What is the length of this leaf? Choices: A: 3 1/4 inches; B: 3 1/2 inches; C: 4 1/4 inches; D: 4 1/2 inches.

text.en: The marked span measures 3 whole inches plus 2 quarter-inch intervals. Since 2/4 = 1/2, the leaf is 3 1/2 inches long.
text.es: El tramo marcado mide 3 pulgadas completas más 2 intervalos de un cuarto de pulgada. Como 2/4 = 1/2, la hoja mide 3 1/2 pulgadas.

alt.en: Question 3. Ryan folded a sheet of paper into 12 equal-sized sections arranged in 2 rows and 6 columns. He shaded the 2 sections in the last column. Which fraction is equivalent to the one represented by the shaded part of the sheet of paper? Choices: A: 1/12; B: 1/6; C: 1/5; D: 6/5.

text.en: The model has 2 shaded sections out of 12 equal sections, representing 2/12. Dividing numerator and denominator by 2 gives 2/12 = 1/6.
text.es: El modelo tiene 2 secciones sombreadas de 12 secciones iguales, por lo que representa 2/12. Al dividir el numerador y el denominador entre 2 se obtiene 2/12 = 1/6.

alt.en: Question 4. A diagram has horizontal line ACE through C, vertical ray CB, and diagonal ray CD rising to the right. Angle DCE is the acute angle between diagonal ray CD and right-pointing ray CE; it is visibly smaller than half of the right angle BCE. What is the measure of angle DCE? Choices: A: 40°; B: 50°; C: 90°; D: 130°.

text.en: Using ray CE as the protractor's 0-degree baseline, ray CD meets the 40-degree mark. Therefore, angle DCE measures 40°.
text.es: Al usar el rayo CE como línea de 0 grados del transportador, el rayo CD llega a la marca de 40 grados. Por lo tanto, el ángulo DCE mide 40°.

alt.en: Question 5. Estelle had 28 coins in her collection. Fred had 4 times as many coins as Estelle had. Which equation can be used to determine the number of coins Fred had? Choices: A: 28 × 4 = □; B: 28 ÷ 4 = □; C: 28 + 4 = □; D: 28 − 4 = □.

text.en: Fred has 4 times Estelle's 28 coins, so his amount is represented by 28 × 4. The product is 112 coins.
text.es: Fred tiene 4 veces las 28 monedas de Estelle, así que su cantidad se representa con 28 × 4. El producto es 112 monedas.

alt.en: Question 6. Which number would be 16,000 when rounded to the nearest thousand? Choices: A: 15,472; B: 15,518; C: 16,511; D: 16,739.

text.en: The number 15,518 is past the halfway point of 15,500, so it rounds up to 16,000. The other choices round to either 15,000 or 17,000.
text.es: El número 15,518 supera el punto medio de 15,500, así que se redondea hacia arriba a 16,000. Las otras opciones se redondean a 15,000 o a 17,000.

alt.en: Question 7. Students shaded circles to represent fractions. Britney’s circle has 4 of 6 equal sections shaded. Keisha’s has 2 of 4 equal sections shaded. Ivan’s has 3 of 4 equal sections shaded. Walter’s has 2 of 3 equal sections shaded. Which two students’ shapes represent equivalent fractions? Choices: A: Britney’s fraction is equivalent to Walter’s fraction; B: Keisha’s fraction is equivalent to Walter’s fraction; C: Ivan’s fraction is equivalent to Keisha’s fraction; D: Ivan’s fraction is equivalent to Britney’s fraction.

text.en: Britney shaded 4 of 6 equal parts, and Walter shaded 2 of 3 equal parts. Since 4/6 simplifies to 2/3, their fractions are equivalent.
text.es: Britney sombreó 4 de 6 partes iguales y Walter sombreó 2 de 3 partes iguales. Como 4/6 = 2/3 al simplificar, sus fracciones son equivalentes.

alt.en: Question 8. Carolina moved the hand of a spinner 270 degrees. A diagram shows a three-quarter counterclockwise turn from an upward-pointing position to a left-pointing position. What fraction of a complete turn through a circle is 270 degrees? Choices: A: 3/360; B: 27/360; C: 90/360; D: 270/360.

text.en: A complete turn is 360°, and the spinner moves 270°. The fraction of the full turn is therefore 270/360, which also equals 3/4.
text.es: Una vuelta completa mide 360° y la flecha gira 270°. Por lo tanto, la fracción de la vuelta completa es 270/360, que también equivale a 3/4.

alt.en: Question 9. Which model represents 3 × 1/2? Choices: A: 3 fully shaded squares and a fourth square with 1/2 shaded; B: 3 squares, each divided diagonally into 2 equal triangles with 1 triangle shaded; C: 2 rectangles, each divided into 3 equal vertical parts with 1 part shaded; D: 3 rectangles, each divided into thirds with 2/3 shaded.

text.en: The product 3 × 1/2 means three groups of one-half. Model B shows three separate squares with exactly one-half of each square shaded.
text.es: El producto 3 × 1/2 significa tres grupos de un medio. El modelo B muestra tres cuadrados separados con exactamente la mitad de cada uno sombreada.

alt.en: Question 10. What is the missing number in the equation 5,600 ÷ 8 = □? Choices: A: 7; B: 70; C: 700; D: 7,000.

text.en: Dividing 5,600 by 8 gives 700 because 8 × 700 = 5,600. Thus, the missing number is 700.
text.es: Al dividir 5,600 entre 8 se obtiene 700 porque 8 × 700 = 5,600. Por lo tanto, el número que falta es 700.

alt.en: Question 11. A fraction strip is divided into 5 equal parts, and the first 3 parts are shaded. Which expression represents the amount of the fraction strip that is shaded? Choices: A: 1/5 + 1/5; B: 1/5 + 1/5 + 1/5; C: 3/5 + 3/5 + 3/5; D: 3/5 + 2/5.

text.en: Three of the 5 equal parts are shaded, so the shaded amount is 3/5. Adding one fifth for each shaded part gives 1/5 + 1/5 + 1/5 = 3/5.
text.es: Tres de las 5 partes iguales están sombreadas, así que la cantidad sombreada es 3/5. Al sumar un quinto por cada parte se obtiene 1/5 + 1/5 + 1/5 = 3/5.

alt.en: Question 12. Rose uses instant lemonade powder to make 7 pitchers of lemonade. She uses 2/8 cup of powder for each pitcher. What is the total amount of powder that Rose uses? Choices: A: 2/56 cups; B: 14/56 cups; C: 9/8 cups; D: 14/8 cups.

text.en: Rose uses 2/8 cup for each of 7 pitchers. Multiplying 7 × 2/8 gives 14/8 cups of powder in total.
text.es: Rose usa 2/8 de taza para cada una de las 7 jarras. Al multiplicar 7 × 2/8 se obtienen 14/8 tazas de polvo en total.

alt.en: Question 13. A store ordered 28 boxes holding 12 banana muffins each and 5 boxes holding 6 blueberry muffins each. What was the total number of muffins the store ordered? Choices: A: 51; B: 366; C: 440; D: 10,080.

text.en: The banana muffins total 28 × 12 = 336, and the blueberry muffins total 5 × 6 = 30. Adding them gives 336 + 30 = 366 muffins.
text.es: Los panecillos de banana suman 28 × 12 = 336 y los de arándano suman 5 × 6 = 30. Al sumarlos se obtiene 336 + 30 = 366 panecillos.

alt.en: Question 14. A regular-looking octagon is shown with horizontal top and bottom sides, vertical left and right sides, and four diagonal corner sides. Which statement best classifies the figure? Choices: A: The figure has opposite sides that are perpendicular and angles that measure 135°; B: The figure has opposite sides that are perpendicular and angles that measure 45°; C: The figure has opposite sides that are parallel and angles that measure 135°; D: The figure has opposite sides that are parallel and angles that measure 45°.

text.en: The octagon's opposite sides run in the same directions, so they are parallel. Each exterior turn is 45°, making each interior angle 180° − 45° = 135°.
text.es: Los lados opuestos del octágono siguen las mismas direcciones, así que son paralelos. Cada giro exterior mide 45°, por lo que cada ángulo interior mide 180° − 45° = 135°.

alt.en: Question 15. The price of a board game is $24. The price of the board game is 2 times as much as the price of a jigsaw puzzle. What is the price of the jigsaw puzzle? Choices: A: $12; B: $22; C: $26; D: $48.

text.en: The $24 board game costs twice as much as the puzzle. Dividing $24 ÷ 2 = $12 gives the price of the puzzle.
text.es: El juego de mesa de $24 cuesta el doble que el rompecabezas. Al dividir $24 ÷ 2 = $12 se obtiene el precio del rompecabezas.

alt.en: Question 16. A rectangular model is divided into 4 equal vertical parts with 3 shaded. Which figure is shaded to show a fraction equivalent to the model? Choices: A: a rectangle divided into 8 equal vertical parts with 6 shaded; B: a rectangle divided into 3 equal vertical parts with 2 shaded; C: a rectangle divided into 4 equal vertical parts with 1 shaded; D: a rectangle divided into 8 equal vertical parts with 3 shaded.

text.en: The original model has 3 of 4 equal parts shaded, or 3/4. Figure A has 6 of 8 parts shaded, and 6/8 simplifies to 3/4.
text.es: El modelo original tiene sombreadas 3 de 4 partes iguales, es decir, 3/4. La figura A tiene 6 de 8 partes sombreadas, y 6/8 = 3/4 al simplificar.

alt.en: Question 17. The value of the 7 in 507,264 is 10 times the value of the 7 in which number? Choices: A: 493,725; B: 587,921; C: 672,439; D: 714,093.

text.en: The 7 in 507,264 is worth 7,000. In 493,725 the 7 is worth 700, and 10 × 700 = 7,000.

alt.en: Question 18. A gardener ordered 46 flowering bushes to plant in a park. Each bush cost $27. What was the total cost of the bushes? Choices: A: $1,102; B: $1,142; C: $1,202; D: $1,242.

text.es: Se multiplican los 46 arbustos por $27 cada uno: 46 × 27 = 46 × 20 + 46 × 7 = 920 + 322 = $1,242.

alt.en: Question 19. Dennis wants to buy carpet for the rectangular floor of his living room. The room is 12 feet long and 16 feet wide. What is the area, in square feet, of the living room floor? Choices: A: 28; B: 56; C: 182; D: 192.

text.en: A rectangle's area is length times width. For this floor, 12 × 16 = 192, so its area is 192 square feet.
text.es: El área de un rectángulo es largo por ancho. Para este piso, 12 × 16 = 192, así que el área es 192 pies cuadrados.

alt.en: Question 20. A rectangle has a width of 9 inches. The area of the rectangle is 648 square inches. What is the length, in inches, of the rectangle? Choices: A: 36; B: 72; C: 162; D: 315.

text.en: Use area = length × width: 648 = length × 9. Dividing 648 ÷ 9 = 72 gives a length of 72 inches.
text.es: Se usa área = largo × ancho: 648 = largo × 9. Al dividir 648 ÷ 9 = 72 se obtiene un largo de 72 pulgadas.

alt.en: Question 21. The number below has the digit 6 in two different places. 916,672 How many times greater is the value represented by the 6 in the thousands place than the value represented by the 6 in the hundreds place? Choices: A: 10; B: 100; C: 1,000; D: 10,000.

text.en: In 916,672, the 6 in the thousands place is worth 6,000 and the 6 in the hundreds place is worth 600. Since 6,000 ÷ 600 = 10, the first value is 10 times greater.
text.es: En 916,672, el 6 de los millares vale 6,000 y el 6 de las centenas vale 600. Como 6,000 ÷ 600 = 10, el primer valor es 10 veces mayor.

title.en: New York Grade 5 Math — 2015 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 5 — preguntas publicadas de 2015

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2015 New York State Grade 5 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 5.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015.

sourceTitle.en: 2015 NYS Grade 5 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 5.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015

alt.en: Question 1. Which expression represents the phrase “4 times the sum of 9 and 6”? Choices: A: 4 × (9 + 6) B: 4 × 9 + 6 C: 9 + 6 × 4 D: 9 + (6 × 4)

text.en: The phrase first requires finding the sum 9 + 6, then multiplying that sum by 4. Thus, 4 × (9 + 6) = 4 × 15 = 60 represents the phrase.
text.es: La frase exige primero hallar la suma 9 + 6 y luego multiplicar esa suma por 4. Por lo tanto, 4 × (9 + 6) = 4 × 15 = 60 representa la frase.

alt.en: Question 2. A water tank in the shape of a right rectangular prism is 11 feet deep. The top of the water tank has an area of 220 square feet. What is the volume, in cubic feet, of the water tank? Choices: A: 20 B: 231 C: 1,331 D: 2,420

text.en: For a right rectangular prism, volume equals base area times depth. The tank's volume is 220 × 11 = 2,420 cubic feet.
text.es: Para un prisma rectangular recto, el volumen es el área de la base por la profundidad. El volumen del tanque es 220 × 11 = 2,420 pies cúbicos.

alt.en: Question 3. Members of the Garner High School yearbook committee need to put 1,344 student photos on 24 pages in the yearbook. They want to put the same number of student photos on each page. How many student photos will they put on each page in the yearbook? Choices: A: 51 B: 52 C: 56 D: 61

text.en: Divide the 1,344 photos equally among 24 pages. Since 24 × 56 = 1,344, each page will contain 56 photos.
text.es: Se dividen las 1,344 fotos en partes iguales entre 24 páginas. Como 24 × 56 = 1,344, cada página tendrá 56 fotos.

alt.en: Question 4. Which decimal makes the number sentence true? 0.27 > ?. Choices: A: 0.4 B: 0.26 C: 0.3 D: 0.28

text.en: For 0.27 to be greater than the missing decimal, the missing value must be below 27 hundredths. Since 0.26 < 0.27, 0.26 makes the sentence true.
text.es: Para que 0.27 sea mayor que el decimal que falta, ese valor debe ser menor que 27 centésimos. Como 0.26 < 0.27, el número 0.26 hace verdadera la desigualdad.

alt.en: Question 5. Juliette made a jewelry box shaped like a right rectangular prism. A perspective drawing labels the prism’s height as 5 cm, its front horizontal length as 8 cm, and its depth from front to back as 4 cm. Dashed lines represent the three hidden back and interior edges. What was the volume, in cubic centimeters, of the jewelry box? Choices: A: 17 B: 37 C: 160 D: 184

text.en: Multiply the prism's three dimensions to find its volume: 5 × 4 × 8 = 160. The jewelry box holds 160 cubic centimeters.
text.es: Se multiplican las tres dimensiones del prisma para hallar el volumen: 5 × 4 × 8 = 160. El joyero tiene un volumen de 160 centímetros cúbicos.

alt.en: Question 6. The fifth-grade classes at Brookfield School used five identical buses to go on a field trip. There were a total of 40 seats on each bus. All of the seats on four buses were filled. The fifth bus had 4/5 of the seats filled. Of all the passengers on the buses, 1/8 were adults. How many adults went on the field trip with the fifth-grade classes? Choices: A: 20 B: 24 C: 25 D: 32

text.en: The four full buses carry 4 × 40 = 160 people, and the fifth carries 4/5 × 40 = 32, for 192 passengers. One eighth of 192 is 192 ÷ 8 = 24 adults.
text.es: Los cuatro autobuses llenos llevan 4 × 40 = 160 personas y el quinto lleva 4/5 × 40 = 32, para un total de 192 pasajeros. Un octavo de 192 es 192 ÷ 8 = 24 adultos.

alt.en: Question 7. Which expression is equivalent to 32? Choices: A: (30 + 6) ÷ 3 B: 2 × (9 + 7) C: 9 × (3 + 5) D: 6 + 2 × 4

text.en: Evaluate inside the parentheses first: 2 × (9 + 7) = 2 × 16 = 32. The other expressions evaluate to 12, 72, and 14, not 32.
text.es: Primero se evalúa lo que está dentro de los paréntesis: 2 × (9 + 7) = 2 × 16 = 32. Las otras expresiones dan 12, 72 y 14, no 32.

alt.en: Question 8. Josie has a 1,364-page book to read over summer vacation. She wants to read the same number of pages each day for 62 days. What is the total number of pages Josie will need to read each day? Choices: A: 28 B: 27 C: 22 D: 17

text.en: Divide 1,364 pages by 62 days. Because 62 × 22 = 1,364, Josie must read 22 pages each day.
text.es: Se dividen 1,364 páginas entre 62 días. Como 62 × 22 = 1,364, Josie debe leer 22 páginas cada día.

alt.en: Question 9. Equation 1: 3/10 + 15/100 = 18/100. Equation 2: 4/10 + 32/100 = 72/100. Equation 3: 7/10 + 2/100 = 27/100. Equation 4: 6/10 + 27/100 = 87/100. Which equation or equations are true? Choices: A: equation 1 only. B: equation 2 only. C: equations 3 and 4 only. D: equations 2 and 4 only.

text.en: Equation 2 gives 40/100 + 32/100 = 72/100, and equation 4 gives 60/100 + 27/100 = 87/100. Equations 1 and 3 instead total 45/100 and 72/100, so only equations 2 and 4 are true.
text.es: La ecuación 2 da 40/100 + 32/100 = 72/100, y la ecuación 4 da 60/100 + 27/100 = 87/100. Las ecuaciones 1 y 3 suman 45/100 y 72/100, así que solo son verdaderas la 2 y la 4.

alt.en: Question 10. What is the product of 5/8 × 3/4? Choices: A: 8/32. B: 15/32. C: 8/12. D: 15/12.

text.en: Multiply numerators and denominators: 5/8 × 3/4 = (5 × 3)/(8 × 4) = 15/32. The fraction is already in simplest form.
text.es: Se multiplican los numeradores y los denominadores: 5/8 × 3/4 = (5 × 3)/(8 × 4) = 15/32. La fracción ya está en su forma más simple.

alt.en: Question 11. Barbara filled a box with layers of unit cubes. The box had a volume of 125 cubic units. Which sentence about the box must be true? Choices: A: There were 125 unit cubes in the bottom layer. B: The box was filled with exactly 125 unit cubes. C: There were 125 unit cubes in each layer. D: The box was filled with less than 125 unit cubes.

text.en: Each unit cube has a volume of 1 cubic unit. Therefore, filling a volume of 125 cubic units requires exactly 125 unit cubes.
text.es: Cada cubo unitario tiene un volumen de 1 unidad cúbica. Por lo tanto, para llenar 125 unidades cúbicas se necesitan exactamente 125 cubos unitarios.

alt.en: Question 12. Rashad is filling a toy box with wooden blocks that are each a unit cube in size. He filled the bottom layer of a toy box with 15 wooden blocks. He then stacked two more wooden blocks on top of the bottom layer. The partially filled rectangular toy box has a bottom layer arranged 5 blocks across and 3 blocks deep. At the back-left corner, two additional blocks are stacked above the bottom block, reaching the full height of the box; this indicates that the box is 3 blocks high. What was the total volume, in cubic units, of the toy box? Choices: A: 15. B: 17. C: 30. D: 45.

text.en: The bottom layer holds 15 cubes, and the two cubes stacked above it show that the box is 3 layers high. A full box therefore has 15 × 3 = 45 cubic units of volume.
text.es: La capa inferior contiene 15 cubos y los dos cubos apilados encima muestran que la caja tiene 3 capas de altura. Por lo tanto, una caja llena tiene 15 × 3 = 45 unidades cúbicas de volumen.

alt.en: Question 13. Which expression represents the phrase “triple the sum of 24 and 9”? Choices: A: 3 + (24 + 9). B: 3 × (24 + 9). C: 3 + 24 + 9. D: 3 × 24 + 9.

text.en: “Triple” means multiply by 3, and the sum is 24 + 9. Thus, the expression is 3 × (24 + 9), which equals 99.
text.es: “El triple” significa multiplicar por 3, y la suma es 24 + 9. Por lo tanto, la expresión es 3 × (24 + 9), que equivale a 99.

alt.en: Question 14. Which equation correctly shows the relationship between the numbers 2,560 and 256? Choices: A: 2,560 = 1,000 × (2 + 5 + 6). B: 2,560 = 10 × (2 + 5 + 6). C: 2,560 = 10 × (200 + 50 + 6). D: 2,560 = 1/10 × (200 + 50 + 6).

text.en: The expanded form 200 + 50 + 6 equals 256. Multiplying by 10 shifts every digit one place left, so 10 × 256 = 2,560.
text.es: La forma desarrollada 200 + 50 + 6 equivale a 256. Al multiplicar por 10, cada dígito se desplaza un lugar a la izquierda, así que 10 × 256 = 2,560.

alt.en: Question 15. The distance from Greg’s house to Tanya’s house is 7/10 of a mile. A horizontal line segment connects a closed dot labeled Greg’s house on the left to a closed dot labeled Tanya’s house on the right. The segment is labeled 7/10 mile. Which number correctly shows the number of miles as a decimal? Choices: A: 0.07. B: 0.7. C: 7.0. D: 7.10.

text.en: The fraction 7/10 means seven tenths. Writing seven tenths in decimal form gives 0.7 mile.
text.es: La fracción 7/10 significa siete décimos. Al escribir siete décimos en forma decimal se obtiene 0.7 milla.

alt.en: Question 16. Movie tickets cost $9.25 each, and a large order of popcorn costs $7.75. What is the total cost of 5 movie tickets and a large order of popcorn? Choices: A: $22.00. B: $48.00. C: $54.00. D: $85.00.
