text.en: Five tickets cost 5 × $9.25 = $46.25. Adding the $7.75 popcorn gives $46.25 + $7.75 = $54.00.
text.es: Cinco boletos cuestan 5 × $9.25 = $46.25. Al sumar las palomitas de $7.75 se obtiene $46.25 + $7.75 = $54.00.

alt.en: Question 17. Jack used cubes to make a right rectangular prism. The prism is 8 cubes long, 4 cubes high, and 2 cubes deep. A key states that each cube equals 1 cubic inch. He then made a smaller right rectangular prism using 1/4 of the number of cubes. What was the volume, in cubic inches, of the smaller right rectangular prism? Choices: A: 8 B: 13 C: 16 D: 64

text.en: The prism measures 8 cubes long, 4 cubes high, and 2 cubes deep, so it contains 8 × 4 × 2 = 64 cubes. One fourth of 64 is 64 ÷ 4 = 16 cubic inches.
text.es: El prisma mide 8 cubos de largo, 4 de alto y 2 de profundidad, así que contiene 8 × 4 × 2 = 64 cubos. La cuarta parte de 64 es 64 ÷ 4 = 16 pulgadas cúbicas.

alt.en: Question 18. What is the value of the expression below? 1/4 ÷ 8. Choices: A: 1/32 B: 1/2 C: 2 D: 32

text.en: Dividing by 8 is the same as multiplying by 1/8. Therefore, 1/4 ÷ 8 = 1/4 × 1/8 = 1/32.
text.es: Dividir entre 8 equivale a multiplicar por 1/8. Por lo tanto, 1/4 ÷ 8 = 1/4 × 1/8 = 1/32.

alt.en: Question 19. Nellie has a watering can that contains 20 cups of water. She pours one quart of water on each plant in her yard. If Nellie uses all of the water in the watering can, how many plants does she water? Choices: A: 4 B: 5 C: 10 D: 80

text.en: One quart equals 4 cups, so each plant receives 4 cups. Dividing 20 ÷ 4 = 5 shows that Nellie waters 5 plants.
text.es: Un cuarto de galón equivale a 4 tazas, así que cada planta recibe 4 tazas. Al dividir 20 ÷ 4 = 5 se obtiene que Nellie riega 5 plantas.

alt.en: Question 20. Christopher wants to buy a notebook for $2.15, a pack of glue sticks for $5.08, and a pack of pens for $3.08. What is the total cost of the three items Christopher wants to buy? Choices: A: $10.75 B: $10.31 C: $10.23 D: $10.11

text.en: Add the three prices by place value: $2.15 + $5.08 = $7.23, and $7.23 + $3.08 = $10.31.
text.es: Se suman los tres precios por valor posicional: $2.15 + $5.08 = $7.23, y $7.23 + $3.08 = $10.31.

alt.en: Question 21. Min wants to make 100 name tags with ribbons attached to them. Each name tag requires five centimeters of ribbon. She has 3.25 meters of ribbon. Exactly how many more centimeters of ribbon does Min still need to make 100 name tags? Choices: A: 175 B: 305 C: 325 D: 825

text.en: The tags require 100 × 5 = 500 centimeters of ribbon. Min has 3.25 meters = 325 centimeters, so she still needs 500 − 325 = 175 centimeters.
text.es: Las etiquetas requieren 100 × 5 = 500 centímetros de cinta. Min tiene 3.25 metros = 325 centímetros, así que todavía necesita 500 − 325 = 175 centímetros.

alt.en: Question 22. A park sign reads “Sunrise Trail, 3 1/4 miles.” What is the length, in feet, of the trail? Choices: A: 5,280 B: 5,720 C: 15,840 D: 17,160

text.en: Three miles equal 3 × 5,280 = 15,840 feet, and one fourth of a mile is 5,280 ÷ 4 = 1,320 feet. Together, the trail is 15,840 + 1,320 = 17,160 feet.
text.es: Tres millas equivalen a 3 × 5,280 = 15,840 pies, y un cuarto de milla es 5,280 ÷ 4 = 1,320 pies. En total, el sendero mide 15,840 + 1,320 = 17,160 pies.

alt.en: Question 23. In a shipment of new books for a library, 5/12 of the books were poetry and 2/5 were biographies. The remainder of the books in the shipment were mysteries. What fraction of the books in the shipment were mysteries? Choices: A: 2/12 B: 11/60 C: 7/17 D: 49/60

text.en: Using denominator 60, 5/12 = 25/60 and 2/5 = 24/60, so poetry and biographies use 49/60 of the books. The remainder is 60/60 − 49/60 = 11/60.
text.es: Con denominador 60, 5/12 = 25/60 y 2/5 = 24/60, así que poesía y biografías ocupan 49/60 de los libros. El resto es 60/60 − 49/60 = 11/60.

alt.en: Question 24. In a math game, a player chooses two numbers, as described below. First number: a mixed number between 2 and 10. Second number: 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5. Which statement describes the product of the two numbers a player chooses? Choices: A: The product must be a whole number less than the second number. B: The product must be a value less than the second number. C: The product must be a whole number greater than the second number. D: The product must be a value greater than the second number.

text.en: The first factor is a mixed number greater than 2, so it is greater than 1. Multiplying any positive second number by a factor greater than 1 makes the product greater than that second number, though not necessarily a whole number.
text.es: El primer factor es un número mixto mayor que 2, así que es mayor que 1. Al multiplicar cualquier segundo número positivo por un factor mayor que 1, el producto resulta mayor que ese segundo número, aunque no tiene que ser entero.

title.en: New York Grade 6 Math — 2015 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 6 — preguntas publicadas de 2015

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2015 New York State Grade 6 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 6.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015.

sourceTitle.en: 2015 NYS Grade 6 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 6.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015

alt.en: Question 1. What is the x-coordinate of point P on the coordinate grid? The coordinate plane has horizontal x- and vertical y-axes, with grid lines every 1/2 unit. The labeled integer values on each axis are −2, −1, 1, and 2. Point P is located at (−1 1/2, 1/2). Choices: A: −1 1/2. B: −1/2. C: 1/2. D: 1 1/2.

text.en: Each small grid interval represents 1/2 unit. Point P is three half-unit intervals left of the y-axis, so its x-coordinate is −3/2 = −1 1/2.
text.es: Cada intervalo pequeño de la cuadrícula representa 1/2 unidad. El punto P está tres medios a la izquierda del eje y, así que su coordenada x es −3/2 = −1 1/2.

alt.en: Question 2. Arnold’s entire workout consisted of 10 minutes of warm-up exercises, 25 minutes of lifting weights, and 15 minutes on the treadmill. What was the ratio of the number of minutes he lifted weights to the total number of minutes of his entire workout? Choices: A: 1 : 1. B: 1 : 2. C: 3 : 10. D: 5 : 8.

text.en: The entire workout lasted 10 + 25 + 15 = 50 minutes. The lifting-to-total ratio is 25:50, which simplifies to 1:2.
text.es: El entrenamiento completo duró 10 + 25 + 15 = 50 minutos. La razón entre levantamiento de pesas y tiempo total es 25:50, que se simplifica a 1:2.

alt.en: Question 3. A rectangular prism is drawn as a tall box whose height is greater than the side lengths of its rectangular base. Hidden back and bottom edges are dashed. Which figure represents the net of the rectangular prism? Choices: A: A horizontal strip of four tall rectangles with alternating narrow and wide widths; two congruent small base rectangles are attached above and below the same narrow rectangle in the strip. B: A horizontal strip made of one narrow tall rectangle between two wide tall rectangles; congruent narrow rectangles extend above and below the central rectangle, forming a vertical column. C: A horizontal strip of four short rectangles with alternating wide and narrow widths; two congruent tall rectangles are attached above and below the same narrow rectangle in the strip. D: A cross made from a small central rectangle, tall narrow rectangles directly above and below it, and short wide rectangles directly to its left and right.

text.en: A rectangular prism needs four rectangular side faces in a strip and two congruent rectangular bases. Net A works because its six faces fold along the dashed lines to enclose the prism without overlapping.
text.es: Un prisma rectangular necesita cuatro caras laterales rectangulares en una tira y dos bases rectangulares congruentes. La red A funciona porque sus seis caras se pliegan por las líneas punteadas y cierran el prisma sin superponerse.

alt.en: Question 4. Wyatt hiked 6 miles in 2 hours. At this same rate, what is the total number of miles Wyatt could hike in 9 hours? Choices: A: 3. B: 7. C: 21. D: 27.

text.en: Wyatt's rate is 6 ÷ 2 = 3 miles per hour. At that rate, in 9 hours he hikes 3 × 9 = 27 miles.
text.es: La rapidez de Wyatt es 6 ÷ 2 = 3 millas por hora. A esa rapidez, en 9 horas recorre 3 × 9 = 27 millas.

alt.en: Question 5. A punch recipe requires 2 cups of cranberry juice to make 3 gallons of punch. Using the same recipe, what is the amount of cranberry juice needed for 1 gallon of punch? Choices: A: 3 cups. B: 1 1/2 cups. C: 1 cup. D: 2/3 cup.

text.en: Divide the 2 cups of cranberry juice equally among 3 gallons of punch. The unit rate is 2 ÷ 3 = 2/3 cup per gallon.
text.es: Se dividen las 2 tazas de jugo de arándano en partes iguales entre 3 galones de ponche. La razón unitaria es 2 ÷ 3 = 2/3 de taza por galón.

alt.en: Question 6. Omar has 2 3/4 cups of dough to make dumplings. If he uses 3/16 cup of dough for each dumpling, how many whole dumplings can Omar make? Choices: A: 13. B: 14. C: 15. D: 16.

text.en: Convert 2 3/4 cups to 44/16 cup, then divide by 3/16: 44/16 ÷ 3/16 = 44/3 = 14 2/3. Therefore, Omar can make 14 whole dumplings.
text.es: Se convierten 2 3/4 tazas en 44/16 de taza y se divide entre 3/16: 44/16 ÷ 3/16 = 44/3 = 14 2/3. Por lo tanto, Omar puede hacer 14 empanadillas completas.

alt.en: Question 7. What is the solution of the equation below? x + 8.63 = 11.001. Choices: A: x = 19.631. B: x = 10.138. C: x = 3.471. D: x = 2.371.

text.en: Subtract 8.63 from both sides of x + 8.63 = 11.001. This gives x = 11.001 − 8.630 = 2.371.
text.es: Se resta 8.63 de ambos lados de x + 8.63 = 11.001. Así se obtiene x = 11.001 − 8.630 = 2.371.

alt.en: Question 8. A high-speed elevator can rise 480 feet in 30 seconds. Which expression represents the rate, in feet per minute, of the elevator? Choices: A: 480 × 30. B: 480 ÷ 30. C: 480 × 2. D: 480 ÷ 2.

text.en: Thirty seconds is one-half of a minute, so the elevator travels twice 480 feet in one minute. The required expression is 480 × 2, which equals 960 feet per minute.
text.es: Treinta segundos es medio minuto, así que el ascensor recorre dos veces 480 pies en un minuto. La expresión necesaria es 480 × 2, que equivale a 960 pies por minuto.

alt.en: Question 9. Erica drew a parallelogram. Its horizontal base is 5 1/4 in., its slanted left side is 4 1/2 in., and its perpendicular height is 4 1/5 in. The height is represented by a vertical dashed segment from the upper-right vertex to the base, with a right-angle marker where it meets the base. Which expression can Erica use to find the area of the parallelogram? Choices: A: 5 1/4 × 4 1/5. B: 1/2(5 1/4 × 4 1/5). C: 2 × (5 1/4 + 4 1/2). D: 5 1/4 × 4 1/2.

text.en: A parallelogram's area is base times perpendicular height. The base is 5 1/4 inches and the dashed height is 4 1/5 inches, so the expression is 5 1/4 × 4 1/5.
text.es: El área de un paralelogramo es base por altura perpendicular. La base mide 5 1/4 pulgadas y la altura punteada mide 4 1/5 pulgadas, así que la expresión es 5 1/4 × 4 1/5.

alt.en: Question 10. What is the value of 5/6 ÷ 3/7? Choices: A: 15/42. B: 18/35. C: 35/18. D: 42/15.

text.en: Divide by a fraction by multiplying by its reciprocal: 5/6 ÷ 3/7 = 5/6 × 7/3 = 35/18.
text.es: Para dividir entre una fracción, se multiplica por su recíproco: 5/6 ÷ 3/7 = 5/6 × 7/3 = 35/18.

alt.en: Question 11. The points plotted on the coordinate grid represent different locations in a city, and the grid lines represent the city’s streets. The x-axis increases to the right and the y-axis increases upward. Both axes begin at 0, and the grid marks integer coordinates from 0 through 9. The locations are Theater at (3, 9), Library at (5, 4), Museum at (7, 2), and Post Office at (8, 6). The city plans to build a parking lot at the location represented by the coordinates (8, 4). Which building is the shortest driving distance from the parking lot when travel follows the horizontal and vertical grid streets? Choices: A: theater. B: library. C: museum. D: post office.

text.en: From the lot at (8, 4), the post office at (8, 6) is only 2 street blocks away. It is closest because the museum and library are each 3 blocks away, and the theater is farther.
text.es: Desde el estacionamiento en (8, 4), la oficina de correos en (8, 6) está a solo 2 cuadras. Es la más cercana porque el museo y la biblioteca están a 3 cuadras cada uno y el teatro queda más lejos.

alt.en: Question 12. Solve the equation 0.3r = 2.1. Choices: A: r = 0.7. B: r = 1.8. C: r = 7. D: r = 18.

text.en: Divide both sides of 0.3r = 2.1 by 0.3. The result is r = 2.1 ÷ 0.3 = 7.
text.es: Se dividen ambos lados de 0.3r = 2.1 entre 0.3. El resultado es r = 2.1 ÷ 0.3 = 7.

alt.en: Question 13. A group of students organized a car wash to raise money for a local charity. The students charged $5.00 for each car they washed. In 3 hours, they washed 12 cars. At that rate, how much money could they earn from washing cars for eight hours? Choices: A: $40.00. B: $60.00. C: $85.00. D: $160.00.

text.en: The students wash 12 ÷ 3 = 4 cars per hour, so in 8 hours they wash 4 × 8 = 32 cars. At $5 per car, they earn 32 × $5 = $160.
text.es: Los estudiantes lavan 12 ÷ 3 = 4 autos por hora, así que en 8 horas lavan 4 × 8 = 32 autos. A $5 por auto, ganan 32 × $5 = $160.

alt.en: Question 14. Madison and Pedro each created a number pattern that began with the number 0. Madison used the rule “Add 4.” Pedro used the rule “Add 12.” Which statement is true about each corresponding pair of numbers in the two patterns? Choices: A: Each number in Pedro’s pattern is 8 less than the corresponding number in Madison’s pattern. B: Each number in Pedro’s pattern is 8 more than the corresponding number in Madison’s pattern. C: Each number in Pedro’s pattern is 3 times less than the corresponding number in Madison’s pattern. D: Each number in Pedro’s pattern is 3 times more than the corresponding number in Madison’s pattern.

text.en: At step n, Madison's number is 4n and Pedro's is 12n. Since 12n = 3(4n), every Pedro number is three times its corresponding Madison number.
text.es: En el paso n, el número de Madison es 4n y el de Pedro es 12n. Como 12n = 3(4n), cada número de Pedro es tres veces el número correspondiente de Madison.

alt.en: Question 15. Kelly saves $5 every week. Which expression represents the amount of money, in dollars, Kelly will save in w weeks? Choices: A: 5 + w. B: 5 − w. C: 5w. D: 5/w.

text.en: Kelly saves $5 for each of w weeks, so repeated addition is represented by multiplication. Her total savings are 5 × w = 5w dollars.
text.es: Kelly ahorra $5 durante cada una de w semanas, así que la suma repetida se representa con una multiplicación. Su ahorro total es 5 × w = 5w dólares.

alt.en: Question 16. What is the greatest common factor of 56 and 92? Choices: A: 2. B: 4. C: 7. D: 8.

text.en: The prime factorizations are 56 = 2³ × 7 and 92 = 2² × 23. Their greatest shared factor is 2² = 4.
text.es: Las factorizaciones primas son 56 = 2³ × 7 y 92 = 2² × 23. Su mayor factor común es 2² = 4.

alt.en: Question 17. A museum has an aquarium in the shape of a right rectangular prism that is 22.9 meters long, 7.5 meters wide, and 4.6 meters high. What is the volume, rounded to the nearest cubic meter, of the aquarium? Choices: A: 280. B: 623. C: 790. D: 1,288.

text.en: Multiply the aquarium's dimensions: 22.9 × 7.5 × 4.6 = 790.05 cubic meters. Rounded to the nearest cubic meter, the volume is 790.
text.es: Se multiplican las dimensiones del acuario: 22.9 × 7.5 × 4.6 = 790.05 metros cúbicos. Redondeado al metro cúbico más cercano, el volumen es 790.

alt.en: Question 18. The right rectangular prism described below is made of equal-sized cubes. The side length of each cube is 2 1/2 inches. The prism is 5 cubes wide, 4 cubes high, and 2 cubes deep, for a total arrangement of 5 by 4 by 2 cubes. What is the volume, in cubic inches, of the right rectangular prism? Choices: A: 50. B: 100. C: 250. D: 625.

text.en: The prism is 5 cubes long, 4 cubes high, and 2 cubes deep, so it contains 5 × 4 × 2 = 40 cubes. Each 2 1/2-inch cube has volume 2.5³ = 15.625, giving 40 × 15.625 = 625 cubic inches.
text.es: El prisma mide 5 cubos de largo, 4 de alto y 2 de profundidad, así que contiene 5 × 4 × 2 = 40 cubos. Cada cubo de 2 1/2 pulgadas tiene volumen 2.5³ = 15.625, y 40 × 15.625 = 625 pulgadas cúbicas.

alt.en: Question 19. The water level in an ocean bay changes at an average rate of 3 meters per hour. At this rate, how many hours would it take for the water level to change 12 meters? Choices: A: 1/4. B: 1/3. C: 4. D: 36.

text.en: At 3 meters per hour, the time for a 12-meter change is distance divided by rate. Thus, 12 ÷ 3 = 4 hours.
text.es: A una razón de 3 metros por hora, el tiempo para un cambio de 12 metros es distancia entre rapidez. Por lo tanto, 12 ÷ 3 = 4 horas.

alt.en: Question 20. A company paid $48 for 2 cases of printer paper. Each case contained 12 packages of paper. Next month the company’s office manager needs to order 180 packages of the same paper. If the price per package does not change, what would be the total cost of next month’s order? Choices: A: $90. B: $360. C: $720. D: $1,140.

text.en: Two cases contain 2 × 12 = 24 packages, so each package costs $48 ÷ 24 = $2. Ordering 180 packages therefore costs 180 × $2 = $360.
text.es: Dos cajas contienen 2 × 12 = 24 paquetes, así que cada paquete cuesta $48 ÷ 24 = $2. Por lo tanto, pedir 180 paquetes cuesta 180 × $2 = $360.

alt.en: Question 21. Which two expressions are equivalent for any value of y? Choices: A: 3(3y + 3) and 6y + 6. B: 3(3y + 3) and 9y + 6. C: 9(y + 3) and 12 + 9y. D: 9(y + 3) and 27 + 9y.

text.en: Distribute 9 across y + 3: 9(y + 3) = 9y + 27. By commutativity, this is the same as 27 + 9y.
text.es: Se distribuye 9 sobre y + 3: 9(y + 3) = 9y + 27. Por la propiedad conmutativa, esto equivale a 27 + 9y.

alt.en: Question 22. There are five houses on Maple Street and five houses on Oak Street, arranged from left to right. The first house on Maple Street is numbered 6. Each house number on Maple Street is six more than the house number to its left. The first house on Oak Street is numbered 4. Each house number on Oak Street is eight more than the house number to its left. How much greater is the house number of the last house on Oak Street than the house number of the last house on Maple Street? Choices: A: 2. B: 6. C: 8. D: 10.

text.en: Maple's fifth number is 6 + 4(6) = 30, while Oak's fifth number is 4 + 4(8) = 36. The last Oak number is 36 − 30 = 6 greater.
text.es: El quinto número de Maple es 6 + 4(6) = 30, mientras que el quinto de Oak es 4 + 4(8) = 36. El último número de Oak es 36 − 30 = 6 mayor.

alt.en: Question 23. Which equation has the solution x = 2? Choices: A: 2x − 3 = 19. B: 3x + 2 = 8. C: 4x − 4 = −4. D: 5x + 1 = 10.

text.en: Substitute x = 2 into 3x + 2 = 8: 3(2) + 2 = 6 + 2 = 8. Thus, x = 2 satisfies that equation.
text.es: Se sustituye x = 2 en 3x + 2 = 8: 3(2) + 2 = 6 + 2 = 8. Por lo tanto, x = 2 satisface esa ecuación.

alt.en: Question 24. At a concert, 20% of the audience members were teenagers. If the number of teenagers at the concert was 360, what was the total number of audience members? Choices: A: 432. B: 450. C: 1,800. D: 7,200.

text.en: If 360 is 20% of the audience, then 0.20 × total = 360. Dividing gives total = 360 ÷ 0.20 = 1,800 people.
text.es: Si 360 es el 20% del público, entonces 0.20 × total = 360. Al dividir se obtiene total = 360 ÷ 0.20 = 1,800 personas.

alt.en: Question 25. The net below represents a three-dimensional object. The net consists of three rectangles arranged vertically in a single strip. A triangle is attached to the left side of the middle rectangle, and a congruent triangle is attached to its right side. Dashed fold lines separate the middle rectangle from the rectangles above and below it and from the two triangles. Which three-dimensional object can be formed from the net? Choices: A: A square pyramid with one quadrilateral base and four triangular faces meeting at one apex. B: A triangular prism with two parallel triangular bases joined by three rectangular faces. C: A rectangular prism with six rectangular faces. D: A triangular pyramid with four triangular faces.

text.en: The net has three rectangular faces that wrap around and two congruent triangular ends. It forms the solid in B because those are exactly the faces of a triangular prism.
text.es: La red tiene tres caras rectangulares que forman los lados y dos extremos triangulares congruentes. Forma el sólido B porque esas son exactamente las caras de un prisma triangular.

alt.en: Question 26. The trapezoid below has an area of 21.66 square inches. Its horizontal top base is 4.6 in. long. Perpendicular dashed segments from both endpoints of the top base to the bottom base show a height of 3.8 in. These segments form a central unshaded rectangle. The bottom base extends 1.1 in. beyond the rectangle on each side, forming two shaded right-triangular sections. The diagram is not drawn to scale. What is the total area of the shaded sections of the trapezoid? Choices: A: 2.09 square inches B: 4.18 square inches C: 4.86 square inches D: 8.74 square inches

text.en: Each shaded end is a triangle with base 1.1 inches and height 3.8 inches. Their combined area is 2 × (1/2 × 1.1 × 3.8) = 4.18 square inches.
text.es: Cada extremo sombreado es un triángulo de base 1.1 pulgadas y altura 3.8 pulgadas. Su área total es 2 × (1/2 × 1.1 × 3.8) = 4.18 pulgadas cuadradas.

alt.en: Question 27. Which expression is equivalent to the expression below? g + g + g + g + g + g Choices: A: 6 + g B: g⁶ C: 6g D: g/6

text.en: The expression adds six identical terms g. Repeated addition gives g + g + g + g + g + g = 6g.
text.es: La expresión suma seis términos iguales a g. La suma repetida da g + g + g + g + g + g = 6g.

alt.en: Question 28. Keith wants to plot −8 and −9 on a number line. Which statement is true? Choices: A: Keith should plot −8 to the left of −9 because −8 < −9. B: Keith should plot −8 to the left of −9 because −8 > −9. C: Keith should plot −9 to the left of −8 because −9 < −8. D: Keith should plot −9 to the left of −8 because −9 > −8.

text.en: On a number line, smaller numbers lie farther left. Since −9 < −8, −9 must be plotted to the left of −8.
text.es: En una recta numérica, los números menores quedan más a la izquierda. Como −9 < −8, se debe ubicar −9 a la izquierda de −8.

title.en: New York Grade 7 Math — 2015 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 7 — preguntas publicadas de 2015

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2015 New York State Grade 7 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 7.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015.

sourceTitle.en: 2015 NYS Grade 7 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 7.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015

alt.en: Question 1. Yesterday, the temperature at noon was 11.4°F. By midnight, the temperature had decreased by 15.7 degrees. What was the temperature at midnight? Choices: A: −4.3°F B: −11.4°F C: −15.7°F D: −27.1°F

text.en: A decrease of 15.7 degrees means subtracting from 11.4. The midnight temperature was 11.4 − 15.7 = −4.3°F.
text.es: Una disminución de 15.7 grados significa restar esa cantidad de 11.4. La temperatura a medianoche fue 11.4 − 15.7 = −4.3°F.

alt.en: Question 2. Jared surveyed the students in his class to determine how they scored in a game. He displayed his results in a table titled “GAME SCORES.” The table has columns “Score (points)” and “Number of Students.” The rows are: 0 to 4 points, 5 students; 5 to 9 points, 10 students; 10 to 14 points, 3 students; and 15 to 19 points, 7 students. Which histogram represents the data in the table? Each choice is a histogram titled “GAME SCORES,” with horizontal axis “Score (points),” intervals 0–4, 5–9, 10–14, and 15–19, and vertical axis “Number of Students.” Adjacent bars represent the four intervals in that order. Choices: A: The bar heights are 7, 3, 10, and 5. B: The bar heights are 5, 10, 3, and 7. C: The bar heights are 5, 6, 3, and 7. D: The bar heights are 10, 5, 3, and 7.

text.en: The table requires consecutive bar heights of 5, 10, 3, and 7 for the four score intervals. Histogram B represents the data because its bars have exactly those heights.
text.es: La tabla requiere alturas consecutivas de 5, 10, 3 y 7 para los cuatro intervalos de puntaje. El histograma B representa los datos porque sus barras tienen exactamente esas alturas.

alt.en: Question 3. Altitude above sea level is given in positive values and below sea level is given in negative values. Which situation describes a hiker in Death Valley stopping at an altitude of 0 feet? Choices: A: The hiker starts at −10 feet then increases altitude by 10 feet. B: The hiker starts at −10 feet then decreases altitude by 10 feet. C: The hiker starts at 10 feet then increases altitude by 10 feet. D: The hiker starts at 0 feet then decreases altitude by 10 feet.

text.en: Starting at −10 feet and increasing altitude by 10 feet gives −10 + 10 = 0 feet. That situation ends exactly at sea level.
text.es: Comenzar a −10 pies y aumentar la altitud 10 pies da −10 + 10 = 0 pies. Esa situación termina exactamente al nivel del mar.

alt.en: Question 4. A cereal company puts a colored ring in each box of cereal. There are 6 different ring colors. The colors of the rings in each of 50 cereal boxes are listed in a table titled “RING COLORS IN CEREAL BOXES.” The table has columns “Color” and “Number of Rings.” The rows are: Red, 7; Blue, 15; Green, 8; Purple, 10; Yellow, 5; Orange, 5. Based on the data, what is the probability that the next cereal box will contain a blue or a yellow ring? Choices: A: 1/6 B: 2/5 C: 3/5 D: 2/3

text.en: There are 15 blue rings and 5 yellow rings, so 15 + 5 = 20 of the 50 rings qualify. The experimental probability is 20/50 = 2/5.
text.es: Hay 15 anillos azules y 5 amarillos, así que 15 + 5 = 20 de los 50 anillos cumplen la condición. La probabilidad experimental es 20/50 = 2/5.

alt.en: Question 5. The three steps below were used to find an expression equivalent to (2/5)(15x − 30y) + 10x. Step 1 is unknown. Step 2 is 16x − 12y. Step 3 is 4(4x − 3y). Which expression could be used as Step 1? Choices: A: (2/5)(25x − 30y) B: 6x − 12y + 10x C: 6x − 30y + 10x D: 15(x − 2y) + 10x

text.en: Distribute 2/5 over 15x − 30y to get 6x − 12y. Keeping the remaining +10x gives Step 1 as 6x − 12y + 10x.
text.es: Se distribuye 2/5 sobre 15x − 30y para obtener 6x − 12y. Al conservar el término +10x, el paso 1 queda como 6x − 12y + 10x.

alt.en: Question 6. A graph titled “Circus Admission” shows the relationship between the number of people in a group and the total cost of admission tickets for a circus. The horizontal x-axis is labeled “Number of People” and is marked from 0 through 10 in increments of 1. The vertical y-axis is labeled “Total Cost (dollars)” and is marked from 0 through 135 in increments of 15. A straight ray begins at (0, 0), passes through the closed points (1, 15), (2, 30), (3, 45), (4, 60), (5, 75), (6, 90), (7, 105), and (8, 120), and continues upward and to the right with an arrow. What point on the graph represents the unit rate? Choices: A: (0, 0) B: (1, 15) C: (15, 1) D: (8, 120)

text.en: The graph rises $15 for each additional person, so the unit rate is $15 per 1 person. A unit rate appears where x = 1, at the point (1, 15).
text.es: La gráfica aumenta $15 por cada persona adicional, así que la razón unitaria es $15 por 1 persona. La razón unitaria aparece cuando x = 1, en el punto (1, 15).

alt.en: Question 8. A recipe requires 1/3 cup of milk for each 1/4 cup of water. How many cups of water are needed for each cup of milk? Choices: A: 1/12 B: 3/4 C: 11/12 D: 1 1/3

text.en: The water-to-milk ratio is (1/4) ÷ (1/3). Multiplying by the reciprocal gives 1/4 × 3/1 = 3/4 cup of water per cup of milk.
text.es: La razón de agua a leche es (1/4) ÷ (1/3). Al multiplicar por el recíproco se obtiene 1/4 × 3/1 = 3/4 de taza de agua por taza de leche.

alt.en: Question 9. During a sale, a store offered a 40% discount on a particular camera that was originally priced at $450. After the sale, the discounted price of the camera was increased by 40%. What was the price of the camera after this increase? Choices: A: $252 B: $360 C: $378 D: $450

text.en: A 40% discount leaves 60% of $450, so the sale price is 0.60 × 450 = $270. Increasing that by 40% gives 1.40 × 270 = $378.
text.es: Un descuento del 40% deja el 60% de $450, así que el precio rebajado es 0.60 × 450 = $270. Al aumentarlo un 40% se obtiene 1.40 × 270 = $378.

alt.en: Question 10. What is the product of (−1/4) × (−3/7)? Choices: A: −7/12 B: −3/28 C: 3/28 D: 7/12

text.en: The product of two negative numbers is positive. Multiplying numerators and denominators gives (−1/4)(−3/7) = 3/28.
text.es: El producto de dos números negativos es positivo. Al multiplicar numeradores y denominadores se obtiene (−1/4)(−3/7) = 3/28.

alt.en: Question 11. Gary buys a 3 1/2-pound bag of cat food every 3 weeks. Gary feeds his cat the same amount of food each day. Which expression can Gary use to determine the number of pounds of cat food his cat eats each year? (1 year = 52 weeks) Choices: A: 7/2 × 52/3 B: 7/2 × 3/52 C: 3(1/2 × 3/52) D: 3(1/2 × 52/3)

text.en: The 3 1/2-pound bag is 7/2 pounds, and there are 52/3 three-week periods in a year. Therefore, the yearly amount is represented by 7/2 × 52/3.
text.es: La bolsa de 3 1/2 libras equivale a 7/2 libras, y hay 52/3 períodos de tres semanas en un año. Por lo tanto, la cantidad anual se representa con 7/2 × 52/3.

alt.en: Question 12. What is the decimal equivalent of 7/8? Choices: A: 0.780 B: 0.870 C: 0.875 D: 0.885

text.en: Dividing 7 by 8 gives 0.875 because 0.875 × 8 = 7. Thus, 7/8 has decimal form 0.875.
text.es: Al dividir 7 entre 8 se obtiene 0.875 porque 0.875 × 8 = 7. Por lo tanto, la forma decimal de 7/8 es 0.875.

alt.en: Question 13. What is the value of (−1/4 − 1/2) ÷ (−4/7)? Choices: A: −1 5/16 B: −3/7 C: 3/7 D: 1 5/16

text.en: First, −1/4 − 1/2 = −3/4. Then (−3/4) ÷ (−4/7) = (−3/4)(−7/4) = 21/16 = 1 5/16; the two negative factors make the result positive.
text.es: Primero, −1/4 − 1/2 = −3/4. Luego, (−3/4) ÷ (−4/7) = (−3/4)(−7/4) = 21/16 = 1 5/16; los dos factores negativos hacen positivo el resultado.

alt.en: Question 14. The school bus Evie rides is scheduled to arrive at her stop at 8:20 a.m. each day. The table below shows the actual arrival times of the bus for several days that were randomly selected over the past few months. The table is titled “BUS ARRIVAL TIMES (a.m.)” and has four rows and five columns. From left to right, row 1 contains 8:21, 8:21, 8:19, 8:20, 8:23. Row 2 contains 8:22, 8:20, 8:18, 8:20, 8:18. Row 3 contains 8:21, 8:20, 8:19, 8:17, 8:25. Row 4 contains 8:20, 8:20, 8:18, 8:19, 8:24. Based on these data, what is the probability that the bus will arrive at Evie’s stop before 8:20 a.m. tomorrow? Choices: A: 3/10 B: 1/3 C: 7/20 D: 13/20

text.en: Seven of the 20 recorded arrival times are earlier than 8:20: three at 8:19, three at 8:18, and one at 8:17. Therefore, the estimated probability is 7/20 because 7 of 20 observations qualify.
text.es: Siete de las 20 llegadas registradas son anteriores a las 8:20: tres a las 8:19, tres a las 8:18 y una a las 8:17. Por lo tanto, la probabilidad es 7/20 porque cumplen 7 de 20 observaciones.

alt.en: Question 15. What is the radius, in centimeters, of a circle that has a circumference of 16π centimeters? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: 8 B: 16 C: 32 D: 64

text.en: Use the circumference formula C = 2πr. Substituting 16π gives 16π = 2πr, so r = 8 centimeters.
text.es: Se usa la fórmula de la circunferencia C = 2πr. Al sustituir 16π se obtiene 16π = 2πr, así que r = 8 centímetros.

alt.en: Question 16. Each sales associate at an electronics store has a choice of the two salary options below: $115 per week plus 9.5% commission on the associate’s total sales; or $450 per week with no commission. The average of the total sales amount for each associate last year was $125,000. Based on this average, what is the difference between the two salary options each year? (52 weeks = 1 year) Calculators allowed. Choices: A: $4,262.11 B: $5,545.00 C: $10,956.90 D: $11,525.00

text.en: The commission plan pays 52($115) + 0.095($125,000) = $17,855 per year. The fixed plan pays 52($450) = $23,400, a difference of $23,400 − $17,855 = $5,545.
text.es: El plan con comisión paga 52($115) + 0.095($125,000) = $17,855 al año. El plan fijo paga 52($450) = $23,400, una diferencia de $23,400 − $17,855 = $5,545.

alt.en: Question 17. Travis, Jessica, and Robin are collecting donations for the school band. Travis wants to collect 20% more than Jessica, and Robin wants to collect 35% more than Travis. If the students meet their goals and Travis collects $43, how much money did they collect in all? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: $106.78 B: $128.60 C: $136.88 D: $144.99

text.en: Travis's $43 is 120% of Jessica's goal, so Jessica collects 43 ÷ 1.20 = $35.833…. Robin collects 1.35 × 43 = $58.05; together they collect $35.833… + $43 + $58.05 = $136.88 to the nearest cent.
text.es: Los $43 de Travis son el 120% de la meta de Jessica, así que Jessica reúne 43 ÷ 1.20 = $35.833…. Robin reúne 1.35 × 43 = $58.05; juntos reúnen $35.833… + $43 + $58.05 = $136.88 al centavo más cercano.

alt.en: Question 18. The mean radius of Earth is 6,371.0 kilometers, and the mean radius of Earth’s Moon is 1,737.5 kilometers. What is the approximate difference in the mean circumferences, in kilometers, of Earth and Earth’s Moon? Round your answer to the nearest tenth of a kilometer. Calculators allowed. Choices: A: 40,030.2 B: 29,113.1 C: 14,556.6 D: 10,917.0

text.en: Circumference is 2πr, so the difference is 2π(6,371.0 − 1,737.5) = 2π(4,633.5). This is approximately 29,113.1 kilometers.
text.es: La circunferencia es 2πr, así que la diferencia es 2π(6,371.0 − 1,737.5) = 2π(4,633.5). Esto es aproximadamente 29,113.1 kilómetros.

alt.en: Question 19. A dealer paid $10,000 for a boat at an auction. At the dealership, a salesperson sold the boat for 30% more than the auction price. The salesperson received a commission of 25% of the difference between the auction price and the dealership price. What was the salesperson’s commission? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: $750 B: $1,750 C: $3,250 D: $5,500

text.en: Selling for 30% more creates a $10,000 × 0.30 = $3,000 difference. The commission is 25% of that difference: 0.25 × $3,000 = $750.
text.es: Vender por un 30% más crea una diferencia de $10,000 × 0.30 = $3,000. La comisión es el 25% de esa diferencia: 0.25 × $3,000 = $750.

alt.en: Question 20. Which expression represents a factorization of 32m + 56mp? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: 8(4m + 7p) B: 8(4 + 7)mp C: 8p(4 + 7m) D: 8m(4 + 7p)

text.en: Both terms share a greatest common factor of 8m. Factoring gives 32m + 56mp = 8m(4 + 7p).
text.es: Ambos términos comparten el máximo factor común 8m. Al factorizar se obtiene 32m + 56mp = 8m(4 + 7p).

alt.en: Question 21. Amber determined that the expression (−1/2) ÷ (−41/15) is equivalent to 15/82. Which statement describes the process Amber could have used? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: She divided −1/2 by −15 and then divided the result by 41. B: She multiplied −1/2 by −15 and then divided the result by 41. C: She divided −1/2 by −15 and then multiplied the result by 41. D: She multiplied −1/2 by −15 and then multiplied the result by 41.

text.en: Dividing by −41/15 means multiplying by its reciprocal, −15/41. Thus, (−1/2)(−15) ÷ 41 = 15/82, which is the process in statement B.
text.es: Dividir entre −41/15 significa multiplicar por su recíproco, −15/41. Así, (−1/2)(−15) ÷ 41 = 15/82, que es el proceso de la afirmación B.

alt.en: Question 22. Ben earns $9 per hour and $6 for each delivery he makes. He wants to earn more than $155 in an 8-hour workday. What is the least number of deliveries he must make to reach his goal? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: 11 B: 12 C: 13 D: 14

text.en: Eight hours of hourly pay gives 8 × $9 = $72, so 72 + 6d > 155. Then 6d > 83 and d > 13 5/6, making 14 the least whole number of deliveries.
text.es: Ocho horas de salario dan 8 × $9 = $72, así que 72 + 6d > 155. Entonces 6d > 83 y d > 13 5/6, por lo que 14 es la menor cantidad entera de entregas.

alt.en: Question 23. Malika and Adrian prepared containers of potato salad at a deli. Each container was supposed to have a mass of one pound. The manager selected a random sample of containers prepared by each employee to check the mass of each container. A table titled “Mass of Each Container” has two columns. Malika’s containers, in pounds, have masses 1.10, 1.08, 1.05, 0.95, and 0.98. Adrian’s containers, in pounds, have masses 1.30, 1.21, 0.79, 0.90, and 0.88. Which inference is best supported by these data? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: Malika will produce more containers with a mass of exactly one pound than Adrian will. B: Adrian will produce more containers with a mass of exactly one pound than Malika will. C: Most of Malika’s containers will have a mass closer to one pound than most of Adrian’s containers. D: Most of Adrian’s containers will have a mass closer to one pound than most of Malika’s containers.

text.en: Malika's sampled masses differ from 1 pound by at most 0.10, while four of Adrian's five differ by 0.12 or more. Therefore, Malika's containers are generally closer to 1 pound.
text.es: Las masas de la muestra de Malika difieren de 1 libra por no más de 0.10, mientras que cuatro de las cinco de Adrian difieren por 0.12 o más. Por lo tanto, los recipientes de Malika suelen estar más cerca de 1 libra.

alt.en: Question 24. Which expression is equivalent to 8c + 6 − 3c − 2? Calculators allowed. Choices: A: 5c + 4 B: 5c + 8 C: 11c + 4 D: 11c + 8

text.en: Combine like terms: 8c − 3c = 5c and 6 − 2 = 4. The expression simplifies to 5c + 4.
text.es: Se combinan los términos semejantes: 8c − 3c = 5c y 6 − 2 = 4. La expresión se simplifica a 5c + 4.

alt.en: Question 25. A rhombus with side length s is depicted. The rhombus has four equal sides, and its bottom side is labeled s. The perimeter, P, of a rhombus is proportional to the length of each side, s. Which equation represents this relationship? Choices: A: P = 4s. B: s = 4P. C: P = 4 + s. D: s = 4 + P.

text.en: A rhombus has four equal sides, each with length s. Adding the sides gives P = s + s + s + s = 4s.
text.es: Un rombo tiene cuatro lados iguales, cada uno de longitud s. Al sumar los lados se obtiene P = s + s + s + s = 4s.

alt.en: Question 26. Sara is playing a board game. The probability that Sara will score a point on her next turn is 1/3. Which statement describes the probability that Sara will score a point on her next turn? Choices: A: likely. B: certain. C: unlikely. D: impossible.

text.en: A probability of 1/3 is greater than 0 but less than 1/2. Therefore, scoring is possible but less likely than not, so it is unlikely.
text.es: Una probabilidad de 1/3 es mayor que 0 pero menor que 1/2. Por lo tanto, anotar es posible pero menos probable que no anotar, así que es improbable.

alt.en: Question 27. Salid bought 35 feet of window trim at a hardware store. The trim cost $1.75 per foot, including sales tax. If Salid paid with a $100.00 bill, how much change should he have received? Choices: A: $20.00. B: $38.75. C: $61.25. D: $80.00.

text.en: The trim costs 35 × $1.75 = $61.25. Subtracting from the $100 payment gives $100.00 − $61.25 = $38.75 in change.
text.es: La moldura cuesta 35 × $1.75 = $61.25. Al restar esa cantidad del pago de $100 se obtiene $100.00 − $61.25 = $38.75 de cambio.

alt.en: Question 28. A pile of newspapers in Ms. McGrath’s art class was 17 3/4 inches high. Each consecutive week, for the next 5 weeks, the height of the pile of newspapers increased by 8 7/12 inches. What was the height, in inches, of the pile after 3 weeks? Choices: A: 25 3/4. B: 26 1/4. C: 42 1/4. D: 43 1/2.

text.en: In 3 weeks the pile grows by 3 × 8 7/12 = 25 3/4 inches. Adding the initial height gives 17 3/4 + 25 3/4 = 43 1/2 inches.
text.es: En 3 semanas la pila crece 3 × 8 7/12 = 25 3/4 pulgadas. Al sumar la altura inicial se obtiene 17 3/4 + 25 3/4 = 43 1/2 pulgadas.

title.en: New York Grade 8 Math — 2015 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 8 — preguntas publicadas de 2015

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2015 New York State Grade 8 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 8.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015.

sourceTitle.en: 2015 NYS Grade 8 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 8.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2015

alt.en: Question 2. The speed of light in a vacuum is 299,792,458 meters per second. Which number, written in scientific notation, is the best approximation of the speed of light? Choices: A: 0.3 × 10⁷ meters per second B: 0.3 × 10⁸ meters per second C: 3.0 × 10⁷ meters per second D: 3.0 × 10⁸ meters per second

text.en: The number 299,792,458 is approximately 300,000,000. In scientific notation, 300,000,000 = 3.0 × 10⁸.
text.es: El número 299,792,458 es aproximadamente 300,000,000. En notación científica, 300,000,000 = 3.0 × 10⁸.

alt.en: Question 5. The graph of a system of linear equations is given on a coordinate plane. Both axes extend from −12 to 12, with grid lines every 2 units. One line rises from lower left to upper right, passing through (−6, 0), (0, 4), and (12, 12), with arrows at both ends. The other line falls steeply from upper left to lower right, passing through (−8, 12), (−2, 0), and (4, −12), with arrows at both ends. The two lines intersect at approximately (−3, 2). Which ordered pair is the best estimate for the solution of this system of linear equations? Choices: A: (−6, −2) B: (−3, 2) C: (4, −4) D: (6, 8)

text.en: A system's solution is the point where its two lines intersect. The graph's intersection is approximately 3 units left and 2 units up from the origin, or (−3, 2).
text.es: La solución de un sistema es el punto donde se intersecan sus dos rectas. La intersección está aproximadamente 3 unidades a la izquierda y 2 hacia arriba del origen, es decir, (−3, 2).

alt.en: Question 6. Which equation does not represent a linear function? Choices: A: y = 2(x − 3) B: y = 2² − 3x C: y = (x + 1)/5 D: y = 2x² + 3x

text.en: A linear function has no variable raised above the first power. The term 2x² in y = 2x² + 3x makes its graph quadratic rather than linear.
text.es: Una función lineal no tiene variables elevadas a una potencia mayor que uno. El término 2x² en y = 2x² + 3x hace que su gráfica sea cuadrática y no lineal.

alt.en: Question 9. The graph of a function is given on a coordinate plane with grid lines every 1 unit. From the lower left, a straight ray with an arrow rises through approximately (−4, −2) and (−2, 4). From (−2, 4), a curved segment decreases nonlinearly to (0, 0). From (0, 0), another curved segment increases nonlinearly to (2, 8). From (2, 8), a straight ray with an arrow decreases through approximately (4, 1) and continues toward the lower right. For which interval of x is the function decreasing and nonlinear? Choices: A: between −4 and −2 B: between −2 and 0 C: between 0 and 2 D: between 2 and 4

text.en: From x = −2 to x = 0, the graph curves downward from about y = 4 to y = 0. It is decreasing because y falls as x increases, and it is nonlinear because the segment is curved.
text.es: Desde x = −2 hasta x = 0, la gráfica se curva hacia abajo desde aproximadamente y = 4 hasta y = 0. Es decreciente porque y baja al aumentar x, y no es lineal porque el tramo es curvo.

alt.en: Question 12. Jenny wants to rent a truck for one day. She contacted two companies. Laguna’s Truck Rentals charges $20 plus $2 per mile. Salvatori’s Truck Rentals charges $3 per mile. After how many miles will the total cost for both companies be the same? Choices: A: 4 B: 6 C: 20 D: 60

text.en: Let m be the miles driven. Equal costs require 20 + 2m = 3m, so subtracting 2m gives m = 20 miles.
text.es: Sea m la cantidad de millas recorridas. Los costos iguales requieren 20 + 2m = 3m, así que al restar 2m se obtiene m = 20 millas.

alt.en: Question 15. What is the value of n in the diagram below? A horizontal line and a vertical line intersect at right angles, as indicated by a right-angle marker in the upper-left quadrant. A diagonal line also passes through their intersection, extending from the lower left to the upper right with arrows at both ends. The acute angle between the upward vertical ray and the upper-right diagonal ray is labeled (n + 18)°. The acute angle between the leftward horizontal ray and the lower-left diagonal ray is labeled (2n)°. The diagram is not drawn to scale. Choices: A: 18 B: 24 C: 42 D: 48

text.en: The slanted line splits a right angle into angles 2n and n + 18, using vertical angles. Thus, 2n + (n + 18) = 90, so 3n = 72 and n = 24.
text.es: La recta inclinada divide un ángulo recto en ángulos de 2n y n + 18, usando ángulos opuestos por el vértice. Entonces, 2n + (n + 18) = 90, de donde 3n = 72 y n = 24.

alt.en: Question 16. The cost to rent a paddleboat at the city park includes an initial fee of $7.00, plus $3.50 per hour. Which equation models the relationship between the total cost, y, and the number of hours, x, that the paddleboat is rented? Choices: A: y = 3.5x + 7 B: y = 7x + 3.5 C: y = x/7 + 3.5 D: y = x/3.5 + 7

text.en: The hourly rate is the coefficient of x, $3.50, and the one-time initial fee is the constant $7. Therefore, the model is y = 3.5x + 7.
text.es: La tarifa por hora es el coeficiente de x, $3.50, y el cargo inicial único es la constante $7. Por lo tanto, el modelo es y = 3.5x + 7.

alt.en: Question 20. The scatter plot shows the numbers of customers in a restaurant for four hours of the dinner service on two different Saturday nights. The line shown models this relationship, and x = 0 represents 7 p.m. The graph is titled “RESTAURANT CUSTOMERS.” The horizontal axis is labeled “Time (hours)” and runs from 0 to 4 in increments of 1. The vertical axis is labeled “Number of Customers” and is marked from 0 to 60 in increments of 10. The plotted points are (0, 55), (0, 45), (1, 50), (1, 30), (2, 40), (2, 30), (3, 25), (3, 10), (4, 10), and (4, 5). The decreasing model line passes through (0, 50) and (4, 10). What does the value of the y-intercept represent? Choices: A: the average number of customers at 7 p.m. B: the average number of customers at 11 p.m. C: the average change in the number of customers each hour D: the average change in the number of customers during four hours of the dinner service

text.en: The y-intercept is the model's value when x = 0. Since x = 0 represents 7 p.m., the intercept represents the average number of customers at 7 p.m.
text.es: La intersección con el eje y es el valor del modelo cuando x = 0. Como x = 0 representa las 7 p. m., la intersección representa el número promedio de clientes a esa hora.

alt.en: Question 25. Triangle M is similar to triangle N. Triangle M has two angles with measures of 32° and 93°. Which two angle measures could be included in triangle N? Choices: A: 32° and 58°. B: 32° and 74°. C: 93° and 55°. D: 93° and 87°.

text.en: The third angle of triangle M is 180° − 32° − 93° = 55°. Similar triangle N must have the same angle measures, so it can include 93° and 55°.
text.es: El tercer ángulo del triángulo M mide 180° − 32° − 93° = 55°. El triángulo semejante N debe tener los mismos ángulos, así que puede incluir 93° y 55°.

alt.en: Question 26. Figure X and figure Y are drawn on a coordinate grid with unit spacing. Figure X is a pentagon with vertices, in order, at (−4, 4), (−2, 4), (−2, 2), (−3, 2), and (−4, 1), followed by a return to (−4, 4). Figure Y is a pentagon with vertices, in the corresponding order, at (1, −1), (3, −1), (3, −3), (2, −3), and (1, −4), followed by a return to (1, −1). Which statement about figures X and Y must be true? Choices: A: A series of translations will transform figure X to figure Y, and the figures will be congruent. B: A 180° clockwise rotation will transform figure X to figure Y, and the figures will be congruent. C: A series of translations will transform figure X to figure Y, but the figures will not be congruent. D: A 180° clockwise rotation will transform figure X to figure Y, but the figures will not be congruent.

text.en: Every vertex of X moves 5 units right and 5 units down to reach the corresponding vertex of Y, a translation by (5, −5). A translation preserves lengths and angles, so the figures are congruent.
text.es: Cada vértice de X se mueve 5 unidades a la derecha y 5 hacia abajo para llegar al vértice correspondiente de Y, una traslación de (5, −5). Una traslación conserva longitudes y ángulos, así que las figuras son congruentes.

alt.en: Question 27. Which exponential expression is equal to 2⁻⁵ • 2⁸? Choices: A: 2²/2⁻¹. B: (2³)⁻¹. C: 2⁻²/2⁻¹. D: (2⁻¹)³.

text.en: Using the product rule, 2⁻⁵ · 2⁸ = 2⁻⁵⁺⁸ = 2³. Option A also equals 2²/2⁻¹ = 2²⁻⁽⁻¹⁾ = 2³.
text.es: Con la regla del producto, 2⁻⁵ · 2⁸ = 2⁻⁵⁺⁸ = 2³. La opción A también equivale a 2²/2⁻¹ = 2²⁻⁽⁻¹⁾ = 2³.

alt.en: Question 29. During an experiment, the temperature of a substance increased at a constant rate of three degrees Celsius (°C) per hour. Which graph represents this relationship? Each choice is a graph titled “SUBSTANCE TEMPERATURE,” with Time (hours) on the horizontal axis and Temperature (°C) on the vertical axis. Time is marked in 1-hour intervals, and temperature is marked in 1°C intervals beginning at 20°C. Choices: A: An increasing straight ray starts at (0, 20) and passes through (3, 21). B: An increasing straight ray starts at (0, 20) and passes through (1, 21), (2, 22), (3, 23), and (4, 24). C: An increasing straight ray starts at (0, 20) and passes through (1, 22), (2, 24), and (3, 26). D: An increasing straight ray starts at (0, 20) and passes through (1, 23) and (2, 26). Calculators are allowed.

text.en: A rate of 3°C per hour means the graph must rise 3 degrees for each 1-hour move right. Graph D rises from 20°C at hour 0 to 26°C at hour 2, giving (26 − 20)/(2 − 0) = 3.
text.es: Una razón de 3°C por hora exige que la gráfica suba 3 grados por cada hora hacia la derecha. La gráfica D sube de 20°C en la hora 0 a 26°C en la hora 2, y (26 − 20)/(2 − 0) = 3.

alt.en: Question 30. A cone has a radius of 1.2 inches and a height of 2.9 inches. What is the volume, to the nearest tenth of a cubic inch, of the cone? Calculators are allowed. Choices: A: 3.6. B: 4.4. C: 10.6. D: 13.1.

text.en: Use V = (1/3)πr²h: V = (1/3)π(1.2)²(2.9) = 1.392π ≈ 4.4 cubic inches. This matches the choice showing about 4.4 cubic inches.
text.es: Se usa V = (1/3)πr²h: V = (1/3)π(1.2)²(2.9) = 1.392π ≈ 4.4 pulgadas cúbicas.

alt.en: Question 35. A line contains the points (4, 2) and (0, −1). What is the equation of the line? Calculators are allowed. Choices: A: y = 2x − 6. B: y = 3/4x − 1. C: y = 1/4x + 1. D: y = 4/3x − 10/3.

text.en: The slope through (0, −1) and (4, 2) is (2 − (−1))/(4 − 0) = 3/4. Since the y-intercept is −1, the equation is y = (3/4)x − 1.
text.es: La pendiente entre (0, −1) y (4, 2) es (2 − (−1))/(4 − 0) = 3/4. Como la intersección con el eje y es −1, la ecuación es y = (3/4)x − 1.

alt.en: Question 36. A system of equations is given: 5x + 3y = −6 and 2x + y = −4. Which statement about the ordered pair (−6, 8) is true? Calculators are allowed. Choices: A: It is the only solution to the system. B: It is not a solution to either equation. C: It is one of many solutions to the system. D: It is a solution to the first but not the second equation.

text.en: Substitution gives 5(−6) + 3(8) = −6 and 2(−6) + 8 = −4, so (−6, 8) satisfies both equations. The equations have different slopes, so this intersection is the only solution.
text.es: La sustitución da 5(−6) + 3(8) = −6 y 2(−6) + 8 = −4, así que (−6, 8) satisface ambas ecuaciones. Las rectas tienen pendientes distintas, por lo que esta intersección es la única solución.

alt.en: Question 38. What is the equation of the line that passes through point (4, 12) and has a y-intercept of −2? Calculators are allowed. Choices: A: y = 5/2x − 2. B: y = 7/2x − 2. C: y = 2x − 2. D: y = 6x − 2.

text.en: Using the y-intercept point (0, −2) and (4, 12), the slope is (12 − (−2))/(4 − 0) = 14/4 = 7/2. Thus, the line is y = (7/2)x − 2.
text.es: Con el punto de intersección (0, −2) y el punto (4, 12), la pendiente es (12 − (−2))/(4 − 0) = 14/4 = 7/2. Por lo tanto, la recta es y = (7/2)x − 2.

alt.en: Question 42. A solid object was sliced to form two new objects. Each of the two new objects had a circular base. Which shape could not have been the original object? Choices: A: cone. B: cylinder. C: prism. D: sphere.

text.en: Every plane cross-section of a prism is bounded by straight line segments, so it cannot produce two solids with circular bases. Cones, cylinders, and spheres can all be sliced to create circular faces.
text.es: Toda sección plana de un prisma está limitada por segmentos rectos, así que no puede producir dos sólidos con bases circulares. Los conos, cilindros y esferas sí pueden cortarse para crear caras circulares.

alt.en: Question 45. Function 1 is defined by the equation y = (3/4)x + 1, and function 2 is represented by a coordinate graph. The graph has x- and y-axes marked in increments of 2 from −10 to 10, with the square grid extending from −12 to 12 on each axis. Function 2 is an increasing straight line with arrows at both ends. It crosses the y-axis at (0, −3) and crosses the x-axis between 4 and 6, at approximately (5.1, 0). Which statement about the functions is true? Choices: A: Function 1 has the greater rate of change and the greater y-intercept. B: Function 2 has the greater rate of change and the greater y-intercept. C: Function 1 has the greater rate of change, and function 2 has the greater y-intercept. D: Function 2 has the greater rate of change, and function 1 has the greater y-intercept.

text.en: Function 1 has slope 3/4 and y-intercept 1. Using the grid, function 2 rises less than 3 units for every 4 units to the right and crosses the y-axis below 0, so function 1 has both the greater rate of change and the greater intercept.
text.es: La función 1 tiene pendiente 3/4 e intersección con el eje y igual a 1. Según la cuadrícula, la función 2 sube menos de 3 unidades por cada 4 hacia la derecha y cruza el eje y debajo de 0, así que la función 1 tiene la mayor razón de cambio y la mayor intersección.

alt.en: Question 46. A cylinder has a diameter of 14 centimeters and a volume of 112π cubic centimeters. What is the height, in centimeters, of the cylinder? Choices: A: 16. B: 4. C: 16/7. D: 4/7.

text.en: The radius is half the 14-centimeter diameter, so r = 7. Using 112π = π(7²)h gives 112 = 49h and h = 112/49 = 16/7 centimeters.
text.es: El radio es la mitad del diámetro de 14 centímetros, así que r = 7. Al usar 112π = π(7²)h se obtiene 112 = 49h y h = 112/49 = 16/7 centímetros.

alt.en: Question 47. Figure L and figure M are shown on a coordinate grid with x- and y-axes marked in units of 1 from −5 to 5. Figure L is a rectangle in the third quadrant with vertices (−5, −2), (−1, −2), (−1, −5), and (−5, −5); it is 4 units wide and 3 units high. Figure M is a square in the first quadrant with vertices (2, 2), (5, 2), (5, 5), and (2, 5); it is 3 units wide and 3 units high. Maria wants to transform figure L to figure M using only rotations, reflections, and translations. Which statement is true? Choices: A: The transformation can be done with a reflection followed by a rotation. B: The transformation can be done with a reflection followed by a translation. C: The transformation cannot be done because figure L is not congruent to figure M. D: The transformation cannot be done because figures L and M are in different quadrants.

text.en: Figure L is a 4-by-3 rectangle, while figure M is a 3-by-3 square. Rotations, reflections, and translations preserve side lengths, so they cannot transform these noncongruent figures into each other.
text.es: La figura L es un rectángulo de 4 por 3, mientras que la figura M es un cuadrado de 3 por 3. Las rotaciones, reflexiones y traslaciones conservan las longitudes, así que no pueden transformar estas figuras no congruentes una en otra.

alt.en: Question 49. Which statement explains why the point (−2, 2) is the solution to the system of linear equations graphed on a coordinate plane? The x- and y-axes are marked in increments of 2 from −10 to 10, and the square grid extends from −12 to 12 on each axis. Two straight lines with arrows at both ends intersect at (−2, 2). The increasing line crosses the x-axis at (−4, 0) and the y-axis at (0, 4). The decreasing line crosses the y-axis at (0, −4) and passes through approximately (−5.33, 12) and (2.67, −12). Choices: A: It lies on the graph of only one of the equations. B: It lies in the second quadrant of the coordinate plane. C: It is the only point that satisfies both equations simultaneously. D: It is one of many points that satisfies both equations simultaneously.

text.en: The two nonparallel lines intersect exactly once, at (−2, 2). That point is the unique solution because it is the only ordered pair lying on both lines simultaneously.
text.es: Las dos rectas no paralelas se intersecan una sola vez, en (−2, 2). Ese punto es la solución única porque es el único par ordenado que pertenece a ambas rectas simultáneamente.

alt.en: Question 53. The winning time for the men’s 400-meter race in each of the Olympic Games from 1976 to 1996 can be modeled by the equation y = −0.054x + 44.54, where x is the number of years after 1976 and y is the winning time in seconds. If the relationship continues, which equation could be used to predict the winning time in the year 2020? Choices: A: y = −0.054(1976) + 44.54. B: y = −0.054(2020) + 44.54. C: y = −0.054(24) + 44.54. D: y = −0.054(44) + 44.54.

text.en: The variable x counts years after 1976, and 2020 − 1976 = 44. Therefore, the prediction uses y = −0.054(44) + 44.54.
text.es: La variable x cuenta los años transcurridos desde 1976, y 2020 − 1976 = 44. Por lo tanto, la predicción usa y = −0.054(44) + 44.54.

alt.en: Question 54. An above-ground swimming pool in the shape of a cylinder has a diameter of 18 feet and a height of 4.5 feet. If the pool is filled with water to 6 inches from the top of the pool, what is the volume, to the nearest cubic foot, of the water in the pool? Choices: A: 226. B: 452. C: 1,018. D: 4,072.

text.en: The pool's radius is 18 ÷ 2 = 9 feet, and 6 inches = 0.5 foot, so the water depth is 4.5 − 0.5 = 4 feet. The volume is π(9²)(4) = 324π ≈ 1,018 cubic feet.
text.es: El radio de la piscina es 18 ÷ 2 = 9 pies, y 6 pulgadas = 0.5 pie, así que el agua tiene 4.5 − 0.5 = 4 pies de profundidad. El volumen es π(9²)(4) = 324π ≈ 1,018 pies cúbicos.

title.en: New York Grade 3 Math — 2014 Released Questions
title.es: Matemáticas de Nueva York, grado 3 — preguntas publicadas de 2014

description.en: Practice the multiple-choice questions officially released from the 2014 New York State Grade 3 Mathematics Test.
description.es: Practica las preguntas de opción múltiple publicadas oficialmente del examen de Matemáticas de 3.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2014.

sourceTitle.en: 2014 NYS Grade 3 Mathematics Test Released Questions
sourceTitle.es: Preguntas publicadas del examen de Matemáticas de 3.º grado del Estado de Nueva York de 2014

alt.en: Question 1. A model shows 4 rows with 9 equal squares in each row. Which expression is represented by the model? Choices: A: 4 × 9; B: 9 ÷ 4; C: 36 × 4; D: 9 ÷ 36.

text.en: “4 × 9”; This response correctly identifies the expression that represents the given model. The student most likely counted 4 groups of 9 objects and recognized that the model represents multiplication. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret products of whole numbers as the total number of objects in groups.
text.es: “4 × 9”; This response correctly identifies the expression that represents the given model. The student most likely counted 4 groups of 9 objects and recognized that the model represents multiplication. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret products of whole numbers as the total number of objects in groups.

alt.en: Question 2. A playground has an area of 30 square meters. The key says each small square equals 1 square meter. Which shape could represent the playground? Choices: A: a 5-by-5 grid; B: a 3-by-12 grid; C: a 3-by-10 grid; D: a 6-by-6 grid.

text.en: This response correctly identifies a figure with an area of 30 square units. The student may have counted the unit squares individually or multiplied the number of rows by the number of columns. The student who selects this response understands how to measure area by counting unit squares.
text.es: This response correctly identifies a figure with an area of 30 square units. The student may have counted the unit squares individually or multiplied the number of rows by the number of columns. The student who selects this response understands how to measure area by counting unit squares.

alt.en: Question 3. Which figure is 1/2 shaded? Choices: A: a triangle divided by a horizontal line, with the smaller top triangle shaded; B: a circle divided by a horizontal chord near the top, with the small top segment shaded; C: an irregular five-sided figure divided into two unequal regions, with the smaller left region shaded; D: a rectangle divided by a diagonal from one corner to the opposite corner, with one of the two triangles shaded.

text.en: This is the correct result when one part of a whole consisting of two equal-sized parts is shaded. The student may have understood that the denominator meant the whole consisted of two equal- sized parts and the numerator meant that one of those parts was shaded. The student who selects this response understands that a fraction 1/b is the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts.
text.es: This is the correct result when one part of a whole consisting of two equal-sized parts is shaded. The student may have understood that the denominator meant the whole consisted of two equal- sized parts and the numerator meant that one of those parts was shaded. The student who selects this response understands that a fraction 1/b is the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts.

alt.en: Question 4. Kai separated 36 index cards into 4 equal stacks. Which number sentence could be used to determine the number of cards in each stack? Choices: A: 4 × □ = 36; B: 4 ÷ □ = 36; C: □ + 4 = 36; D: □ ÷ 4 = 36.

text.en: “4 × ? = 36”; This is correct. The student who selects this response understands the relationship between multiplication and division. Dividing 36 by 4 means that 4 can be multiplied by an unknown number to equal 36. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret division as an unknown-factor problem.
text.es: “4 × ? = 36”; This is correct. The student who selects this response understands the relationship between multiplication and division. Dividing 36 by 4 means that 4 can be multiplied by an unknown number to equal 36. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret division as an unknown-factor problem.

alt.en: Question 5. Bruce made a game board by painting stripes on a rectangular piece of cardboard. Each stripe covered 1/8 of the rectangle. Which figure could represent the game board Bruce made? Choices: A: a rectangle divided into 8 columns and 2 rows; B: a rectangle divided into vertical regions of unequal widths; C: a rectangle divided into 3 columns and 2 rows; D: a rectangle divided into 8 equal vertical strips.

text.en: This is the correct response that shows a rectangle partitioned into 8 equal-sized parts. The student may have understood that the denominator of the fraction represents the number of equal-sized parts in the whole. The student who selects this response understands how to partition a shape into parts with equal areas.
text.es: This is the correct response that shows a rectangle partitioned into 8 equal-sized parts. The student may have understood that the denominator of the fraction represents the number of equal-sized parts in the whole. The student who selects this response understands how to partition a shape into parts with equal areas.

alt.en: Question 6. Hilda and Mallory each have the same number of seashells. • Hilda sorted her seashells into 3 groups with 8 seashells in each group. • Mallory sorted her seashells into 6 equal groups. How many seashells were in each of the groups Mallory made? Choices: A: 4; B: 9; C: 18; D: 24.

text.en: “4”; This is the correct number of seashells in each of Mallory’s groups. The student may have found the number of seashells in each of Mallory’s groups by multiplying to find the total number of seashells each girl had and then multiplied or divided to find the number of seashells in Mallory’s groups. The student who selects this response understands how to use multiplication and division within 100 to solve word problems involving equal groups. 3 × 8 = 24, 24 ÷ 6 = 4 or 6 × 4 = 24
text.es: “4”; This is the correct number of seashells in each of Mallory’s groups. The student may have found the number of seashells in each of Mallory’s groups by multiplying to find the total number of seashells each girl had and then multiplied or divided to find the number of seashells in Mallory’s groups. The student who selects this response understands how to use multiplication and division within 100 to solve word problems involving equal groups. 3 × 8 = 24, 24 ÷ 6 = 4 or 6 × 4 = 24

alt.en: Question 8. Which fraction is equivalent to 2/8? Choices: A: 1/8; B: 1/4; C: 2/4; D: 4/8.

text.en: “1/4”; This is a correct fraction that is equivalent to 2/8. The student may have drawn a number line or partitioned equal-sized shapes into fourths and eighths and noticed that 1/4 and 2/8 are the same position on the number line or that 1/4 of the area of a shape is the same size as 2/8 of the area. The student who selects this response understands how to recognize or generate an equivalent fraction.
text.es: “1/4”; This is a correct fraction that is equivalent to 2/8. The student may have drawn a number line or partitioned equal-sized shapes into fourths and eighths and noticed that 1/4 and 2/8 are the same position on the number line or that 1/4 of the area of a shape is the same size as 2/8 of the area. The student who selects this response understands how to recognize or generate an equivalent fraction.

alt.en: Question 10. What time is shown on the analog clock? The longer minute hand points a little past 7, and the shorter hour hand is between 10 and 11. Choices: A: 7:10; B: 10:07; C: 10:37; D: 11:37.

text.en: “10:37”; This is the correct result when the hour hand is read as the hour 10 and the minute hand as 37 minutes. The student may have understood what the hour and minute hands represent and what the numbers on the clock represent. The student who selects this response understands how to tell time to the nearest minute.
text.es: “10:37”; This is the correct result when the hour hand is read as the hour 10 and the minute hand as 37 minutes. The student may have understood what the hour and minute hands represent and what the numbers on the clock represent. The student who selects this response understands how to tell time to the nearest minute.

alt.en: Question 12. Mr. Jacobs had 56 books in his office. He put an equal number of books on each of 7 shelves. The equation 56 ÷ 7 = □ can be used to determine the number of books he put on each shelf. How many books, in all, did Mr. Jacobs put on each shelf? Choices: A: 7; B: 8; C: 49; D: 63.

text.en: “8”; This is the correct result when 56 is divided by 7. The student may have known basic division facts or basic multiplication facts. The student who selects this response understands how to determine the unknown whole number in a division equation relating three whole numbers.
text.es: “8”; This is the correct result when 56 is divided by 7. The student may have known basic division facts or basic multiplication facts. The student who selects this response understands how to determine the unknown whole number in a division equation relating three whole numbers.

alt.en: Question 13. Mandy’s garden is shaped like a rectangle. It has a total area of 40 square feet. Which figure could represent Mandy’s garden? Choices: A: a rectangle measuring 9 feet by 4 feet; B: a rectangle measuring 7 feet by 5 feet; C: a rectangle measuring 11 feet by 9 feet; D: a rectangle measuring 8 feet by 5 feet.

text.en: This is the correct response that shows a figure with an area of 40 square feet. The student may have understood that in order for a rectangle to have an area of 40 square feet, the length and width must multiply to equal 40. The student who selects this response understands how to find the area of a rectangle using whole-number side lengths.
text.es: This is the correct response that shows a figure with an area of 40 square feet. The student may have understood that in order for a rectangle to have an area of 40 square feet, the length and width must multiply to equal 40. The student who selects this response understands how to find the area of a rectangle using whole-number side lengths.

alt.en: Question 14. A number line from 0 to 1 is divided into 8 equal intervals. Point J is at 0, L is at 2/8, M is at 3/8, Q is at 7/8, and R is at 1. Which two points have a distance of 3/8 between them? Choices: A: J and L; B: J and M; C: L and Q; D: M and R.

text.en: “J and M”; This is the correct pair of points on the number line with a distance of 3/8 between them. The student may have understood that the number line represents eighths and counted three tick marks from J to M. The student who selects this response understands how to recognize a fraction a/b on a number line diagram by marking off a lengths 1/b from 0. The student recognizes that the resulting interval has size a/b.
text.es: “J and M”; This is the correct pair of points on the number line with a distance of 3/8 between them. The student may have understood that the number line represents eighths and counted three tick marks from J to M. The student who selects this response understands how to recognize a fraction a/b on a number line diagram by marking off a lengths 1/b from 0. The student recognizes that the resulting interval has size a/b.

alt.en: Question 17. Jerome had 23 farm animal stickers and 17 sea animal stickers. Jerome used all of the stickers to fill an 8-page scrapbook. He put the same number of stickers on each page. How many stickers did he put on each page? Choices: A: 5; B: 6; C: 32; D: 40.

text.en: “5”; This is the correct number of stickers on each page of the scrapbook. The student may have understood that the number of farm animal stickers and the number of sea animal stickers should be added together and that their sum should be divided by 8, the number of pages in the scrapbook. The student who selects this response understands how to solve two-step word problems using addition and division.
text.es: “5”; This is the correct number of stickers on each page of the scrapbook. The student may have understood that the number of farm animal stickers and the number of sea animal stickers should be added together and that their sum should be divided by 8, the number of pages in the scrapbook. The student who selects this response understands how to solve two-step word problems using addition and division.

alt.en: Question 18. Ms. Jones has six types of flowers in her garden. A bar graph titled Flowers in Garden shows: Roses, 15; Tulips, 25; Daisies, 10; Violets, 20; Pansies, 5; Lilies, 15. Based on the bar graph, which sentence is true? Choices: A: Ms. Jones has 25 more tulips than pansies; B: Ms. Jones has 10 more lilies than daisies; C: Ms. Jones has 5 more violets than lilies; D: Ms. Jones has 5 more roses than pansies.

text.en: “Ms. Jones has 5 more violets than lilies.”; This is the correct result when the categories and intervals on a scaled bar graph are interpreted and compared correctly. The student may have understood that the number of lilies, 15, should be subtracted from the number of violets, 20, to determine that there are, in fact, 5 more violets than lilies. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret the intervals on a scaled bar graph and interpret “how many more” problems on a bar graph.
text.es: “Ms. Jones has 5 more violets than lilies.”; This is the correct result when the categories and intervals on a scaled bar graph are interpreted and compared correctly. The student may have understood that the number of lilies, 15, should be subtracted from the number of violets, 20, to determine that there are, in fact, 5 more violets than lilies. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret the intervals on a scaled bar graph and interpret “how many more” problems on a bar graph.

alt.en: Question 22. Ryan used gray and white square tiles to make a rectangular design 7 inches high. The gray part is 3 inches wide, and the white part is 5 inches wide. Which expression could be used to find the total area, in square inches, of Ryan’s design? Choices: A: (7 × 3) + (7 × 5); B: (7 + 3) × (7 + 5); C: 3 × 5 × 7; D: 3 + 5 + 7.

text.en: “(7 × 3) + (7 × 5)”; This is the correct expression that can be used to find the total area of Ryan’s design. The student may have understood that the shaded area can be expressed by 7 × 3 and that the unshaded area can be expressed by 7 × 5, and that the two products can be added to find the total area of the rectangle. The student who selects this response understands how to write an expression to represent tiling on a rectangle with whole-number side lengths.
text.es: “(7 × 3) + (7 × 5)”; This is the correct expression that can be used to find the total area of Ryan’s design. The student may have understood that the shaded area can be expressed by 7 × 3 and that the unshaded area can be expressed by 7 × 5, and that the two products can be added to find the total area of the rectangle. The student who selects this response understands how to write an expression to represent tiling on a rectangle with whole-number side lengths.

alt.en: Question 24. If each side of a square has a length of 1 unit, which statement about the square is true? Choices: A: The square is a unit square that can be used to measure mass; B: The square is a unit square that can be used to measure area; C: The square is a unit square that can be used to measure volume; D: The square is a unit square that can be used to measure weight.

text.en: “The square is a unit square that can be used to measure area.” This is the correct response because a unit square can be used to measure area. The student who selects this response understands that a square with side length of 1 unit, called a “unit square,” is said to have “one square unit” of area and can be used to measure area.
text.es: “The square is a unit square that can be used to measure area.” This is the correct response because a unit square can be used to measure area. The student who selects this response understands that a square with side length of 1 unit, called a “unit square,” is said to have “one square unit” of area and can be used to measure area.

alt.en: Question 25. Which number sentence can be used to determine the value of 72 ÷ 9? Choices: A: 9 × □ = 72; B: 9 + □ = 72; C: 9 × 72 = □; D: 9 + 72 = □.

text.en: “9 × ? = 72”; This is the correct number sentence that can be used to determine 72 ÷ 9. The student may have understood that the unknown factor in the multiplication equation is equal to the quotient of 72 ÷ 9. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret division as an unknown- factor problem.
text.es: “9 × ? = 72”; This is the correct number sentence that can be used to determine 72 ÷ 9. The student may have understood that the unknown factor in the multiplication equation is equal to the quotient of 72 ÷ 9. The student who selects this response understands how to interpret division as an unknown- factor problem.

alt.en: Question 26. Mr. Lopez divided his garden into 6 equal parts arranged in 2 rows and 3 columns. The top middle part is shaded to show where he planted carrots. Which fraction of the garden is planted with carrots? Choices: A: 1/6; B: 1/5; C: 1/3; D: 1/2.

text.en: “1/6”; This is the correct fraction that represents a whole partitioned into 6 equal parts, when 1 of the parts is shaded. The student may have understood that the denominator was the number of equal-sized parts and the numerator was the number of parts shaded. The student who selects this response may understand how to represent a fraction as 1/b when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts.
text.es: “1/6”; This is the correct fraction that represents a whole partitioned into 6 equal parts, when 1 of the parts is shaded. The student may have understood that the denominator was the number of equal-sized parts and the numerator was the number of parts shaded. The student who selects this response may understand how to represent a fraction as 1/b when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts.

alt.en: Question 28. Points on a number line represent distances from Jordan’s house. Jordan’s house is at 0 mile, the park is at 1/4 mile, the soccer field is at 2/4 mile, the school is at 3/4 mile, and the library is at 1 mile. What location is 2/4 mile from Jordan’s house? Choices: A: the park; B: the school; C: the library; D: the soccer field.

text.en: “the soccer field”; This is the correct location that is 2/4 mile from Jordan’s house. The student may have understood that each interval on the number line represented one-fourth and that the numerator in 2/4 meant to count over two intervals from 0. The student who selects this response understands how to read and interpret a number line.
text.es: “the soccer field”; This is the correct location that is 2/4 mile from Jordan’s house. The student may have understood that each interval on the number line represented one-fourth and that the numerator in 2/4 meant to count over two intervals from 0. The student who selects this response understands how to read and interpret a number line.

alt.en: Question 29. The rectangular floor of a bathroom is 6 feet wide and 7 feet long. What is the total area, in square feet, of the floor of the bathroom? Choices: A: 13; B: 26; C: 42; D: 48.

text.en: “42”; This is the correct result when a side length 6 feet is multiplied by a side length 7 feet to get an area of 42 square feet. The student may have understood that the two side lengths of the rectangular bathroom floor should be multiplied to find the area. The student who selects this response understands to multiply side lengths to determine rectangular area. 6 × 7 = 42
text.es: “42”; This is the correct result when a side length 6 feet is multiplied by a side length 7 feet to get an area of 42 square feet.
